The Rival Party / "Blackwater"

[No direct dialogue recorded]

Overview

The rival party is a competing group of adventurers who learned about Fizzleblast's expedition through his accidentally public forum post. They arrived approximately one hour behind the party and approached the temple from the roof rather than the front entrance, losing three members in the process. Their strategy was cowardly but effective in concept: break the mirrors heating the T-Rex's area, wake the dinosaur, let it kill the party, then steal the idol in the chaos.

They are referred to by several names — Fizzleblast guessed "Red Fangs" or "Black Skulls," while the players dubbed them "Blackwater." Their plan partially worked: they fired a fireball that woke [[Ransom's Rex]], but the party escaped with the idol. The two surviving mages attempted a final ambush using Dimension Door at the temple exit but were thwarted by [[Moor]]'s Command spell (forcing one to flee) and [[Aramil]]'s Phantasmal Force (causing a Nat 1 panic). They were subsequently devoured by undead rising from the Chultan jungle as darkness fell.

Appearance & Mannerisms

  • Physical Description: Included mages and melee fighters. No specific physical descriptions were given beyond their general composition.
  • First Impression: Not directly encountered until the rooftop, where they were heard speaking ("sleeping, heat, break") and observed through mirrors and [[Tika]]'s invisible scouting.

[!note] Description Completeness

The DM provided minimal physical description. The rival party was primarily a narrative threat — heard, scouted, and feared rather than seen face-to-face until the final ambush.

Role in Story

Time pressure escalation and climactic antagonists. The rival party serves a dual narrative function: they create urgency from the very beginning (Fizzleblast's warning) and they trigger the climax by breaking the mirrors, launching the fireball, and waking the T-Rex. Their final Dimension Door ambush at the exit provides the last obstacle before the party reaches safety.

Key Contributions

  • Created background time pressure throughout the session via Fizzleblast's warning (Session 1)
  • Lost three members navigating the temple from the roof, reducing to three survivors (Session 1)
  • Broke one of the mirrors on the temple roof (Session 1)
  • Launched a Fireball at [[Bob]] as he flew away with the idol, waking [[Ransom's Rex]] (Session 1)
  • Two mages used Dimension Door to ambush the party at the temple exit (Session 1)
  • Were defeated by Command and Phantasmal Force, then devoured by undead outside the [[Fizzle Field]] (Session 1)

Information Held

What They Told the Party

Information Context Session Reliability
No direct communication with the party They were enemies; all information came via scouting 1 N/A

Intelligence Gathered About Them

Information Source Session Confidence
They planned to break mirrors, wake the T-Rex, let it kill the party, then steal the idol [[Tika]]'s invisible scouting 1 HIGH — confirmed by their actions
They were down to three members after losing three on the rooftop approach [[Tika]]'s observation 1 HIGH — confirmed by DM
They were nervous and scared [[Tika]]'s observation 1 HIGH — confirmed by DM
They included at least two mages capable of Fireball and Dimension Door Observed during combat 1 CONFIRMED

Connections

Relationships to Other Characters

Character Nature of Relationship Details Source
[[Fizzleblast]] Competitor Learned of the expedition from his accidental public forum post Fizzleblast's briefing
The Party Enemy Tried to kill the party indirectly (via T-Rex) and directly (Fireball, Dimension Door ambush) Session events

DM Voice Notes

Portrayal Summary

The rival party is a faceless threat — never truly personalized, always lurking. They are competent enough to reach the temple and devise a clever indirect strategy, but ultimately outmatched by the party's resourcefulness and luck. If reprised in any form, play them as desperate and calculating: they fight smart, not fair. They preferred to let a T-Rex do their dirty work rather than face the party directly. Their final ambush was a last-ditch gamble, and they paid for it with their lives.

Unresolved Threads

  • [ ] The DM mentioned one person "survived by riding an eagle" — was this from the rival party or a previous expedition? If a rival party member, they could be a future threat.
  • [ ] What was their actual group name? Fizzleblast guessed several names; "Blackwater" was coined by the players.
  • [ ] Were they about to ask to enter the [[Fizzle Field]] bubble before the undead devoured them? (DM hinted: "they were about to ask you if they could come in the dome with you")

Ransom's Rex

[Snoring sounds, then fury]

Overview

Ransom's Rex is a Tyrannosaurus Rex that had made its lair in the open-air canyon at the top of the [[Temple of the Man and Crocodile]], directly beneath the [[Golden Snake Idol]]. The mirrors' heliographic arrays functioned as heat lamps for the cold-blooded predator, keeping it warm and content. Its presence served as a natural guardian for the idol — no creature or person could approach while the T-Rex was there, and its reputation kept other apex predators at bay.

The T-Rex was sleeping peacefully when the party arrived. [[Bob]] successfully sneaked past it to steal the idol, but the rival party's Fireball struck the T-Rex directly, waking it in a rage. The fireball also destroyed the archway the idol had hung from. The party fled during the ensuing chaos.

Appearance & Mannerisms

  • Physical Description: A full-sized Tyrannosaurus Rex. The DM described it sleeping under heat lamp mirrors, bathing in warmth like a cold-blooded reptile.
  • Notable Mannerisms: Sleeps under heat. Annoyed by pterodactyl screeching. Wakes violently when disturbed.

Role in Story

Environmental obstacle and climactic hazard. Ransom's Rex is the final guardian before the idol — not placed there intentionally, but naturally drawn to the warmth of the mirrors. Its presence creates the session's most tense stealth sequence and, when awakened by the rival party's Fireball, becomes the catalyst for the desperate escape.

Key Contributions

  • Guarded the [[Golden Snake Idol]] by sleeping directly beneath it (Session 1)
  • Its presence kept other predators from entering the roof canyon area (Session 1)
  • Was struck by the rival party's Fireball, triggering the escape sequence (Session 1)
  • Destroyed the archway when it reared up in rage (Session 1)

DM Voice Notes

Portrayal Summary

Ransom's Rex is a force of nature, not a character. When sleeping, it is serene — snoring, basking. When awakened, it is pure kinetic fury. Do not voice it with personality; voice it with sound effects — ground-shaking footsteps, ear-splitting roars, the crunch of the archway breaking. It is the ticking time bomb of the rooftop sequence.

Unresolved Threads

  • [ ] Is Ransom's Rex still alive in the now-permanently-dark Chultan jungle?
  • [ ] Without the mirror heat lamps and the sun, how will the cold-blooded T-Rex survive?
  • [ ] The name "Ransom's Rex" — does "Ransom" refer to a person who previously encountered or named this T-Rex?

Last updated after Session 1 (One-shot). Profile compiled from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip session transcript.

The Giant Constrictor Snake

[Hissing, constricting, dying]

Overview

A massive Giant Constrictor Snake (approximately 40 feet long, two feet wide) that emerged from one of the snake statue's mouths in the prism room when the rising heat activated it. It possessed both venomous fangs and constricting abilities, making it a serious threat to the level-3 party. The snake nearly killed [[Aramil]], grappling and squeezing him for 22 damage (knocking him to -10 HP), and killed [[Tika]] (paralyzed and consumed the imp familiar in raven form). It was ultimately slain by [[Opal]] with a critical hit from her Great Axe while she was blind, dealing 30 damage and cleaving through multiple coils of the snake wrapped around Aramil.

Appearance & Mannerisms

  • Physical Description: About 40 feet long, two feet in diameter. Emerged from the mouth of a snake statue. Had venomous fangs (paralytic venom in addition to constriction).
  • Notable Mannerisms: Aggressive and territorial. Could not be persuaded by [[Aramil]]'s Speak with Animals attempts. Targeted the raven ([[Tika]]) as an easy snack, then went for Aramil specifically because he had angered it.

Role in Story

Mid-session combat encounter and puzzle complication. The snake emerged as a direct consequence of the rising temple heat (caused by removing the [[Zephyr Reliquary]]). It complicated the mirror puzzle by forcing the party to split attention between combat and mechanism manipulation.

Key Contributions

  • Emerged from a snake statue mouth when heat rose in the prism room (Session 1)
  • Nearly killed [[Aramil]] (22 constriction damage, knocked to -10 HP) (Session 1)
  • Killed [[Tika]] (paralyzed and bit with a critical hit) (Session 1)
  • Slain by [[Opal]] with a critical Great Axe hit while blind (30 damage) (Session 1)

DM Voice Notes

Portrayal Summary

A mindless predator. It does not negotiate, it does not retreat, it hunts. It targeted the smallest/weakest prey first (Tika as a raven), then went after whoever angered it (Aramil, who tried and failed to intimidate it). Play it as relentless — it does not care about the party's puzzle; it just wants to eat.

Last updated after Session 1 (One-shot). Profile compiled from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip session transcript.

The Gorallons

```yaml

type: npc

name: "Gorallons"

aliases:

  • "Four-Armed Jungle Apes"
  • "Alan the Growlin"
  • "Brother Gorallon"

race: "Gorallon (Monstrosity)"

occupation: "Wild Predators"

affiliation: "None (wild creatures)"

location: "[[Chultan Jungle]] / Temple Entrance"

status: "One Dead (melted by mirror), One Fled (intimidated by Aramil)"

dispositiontoparty: "Hostile"

first_appearance: "Session 1 (One-shot)"

last_appearance: "Session 1 (One-shot)"

sessions_appeared: [1]

importance: "Minor"

tags:

  • npc
  • creature
  • monstrosity
  • jungle

```

The Gorallons

[Four-armed screeching, then fleeing]

Overview

The Gorallons are four-armed jungle apes classified as monstrosities that attacked the party immediately upon arrival at the temple. Two Gorallons confronted the party at the entrance, pacing at the edge of the mirror beams. They were "not smart" but aggressive, wanting to "rip your guts out." One was melted by [[Moor]] redirecting a mirror beam at it (its insides burned through by the focal point, described as smelling like "cooked steak plus burnt hair"). The second was intimidated into fleeing by [[Aramil]] using Speak with Animals, who convinced it that the mirrors would kill it too, telling it to "think about your wife and kids."

Role in Story

Tutorial encounter and environmental mechanics introduction. The Gorallons taught the party that mirrors could be redirected as weapons and that non-combat solutions (speaking with animals, intimidation) were viable options. They established the danger of the Chultan jungle.

Key Contributions

  • Served as the first combat encounter, establishing mirror-based environmental combat (Session 1)
  • One melted by redirected mirror beam, demonstrating the mirrors' lethal potential (Session 1)
  • One persuaded to flee by [[Aramil]]'s Speak with Animals, establishing non-combat problem-solving (Session 1)
  • Additional blind Gorallons appeared later in the prism room, drawn by sounds, and died from poison gas and mirror beams (Session 1)

DM Voice Notes

Portrayal Summary

Gorallons are dumb, aggressive brutes with four arms. They screech, they pace, they attack — but they can be scared off with a sufficient display. Voice them with guttural, animalistic sounds. The one that was spoken to by Aramil had a moment of animal intelligence — recognizing the danger of the mirrors after watching its companion get melted — before fleeing in terror.

Last updated after Session 1 (One-shot). Profile compiled from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip session transcript.

Minor Creatures of the Chultan Temple

```yaml

type: npc

name: "Minor Creatures of the Chultan Temple"

aliases: []

race: "Various"

occupation: "Wildlife / Hazards"

affiliation: "None (wild creatures)"

location: "[[Temple of the Man and Crocodile]] / [[Chultan Jungle]]"

status: "Various (mostly dead)"

dispositiontoparty: "Hostile"

first_appearance: "Session 1 (One-shot)"

last_appearance: "Session 1 (One-shot)"

sessions_appeared: [1]

importance: "Minor"

tags:

  • npc
  • creature
  • wildlife
  • hazard

```

Minor Creatures of the Chultan Temple

Feral Baboons

Small, aggressive primates that swarmed into the temple as the sun set and the mirror defenses weakened. They arrived in large numbers (30-40), with melted skin from pushing through weakened mirror beams. Many died from the poison gas trap. Two surviving baboons chased [[Bob]] through the temple before running into spike traps. Two others attacked [[Lavender]] during her snake-statue mission and were killed by her Hellish Rebuke (fire spread between them in the volatile heat). They had Pack Tactics, giving them advantage on attacks.

Pteranodons

Small pteranodons found gnawing on fallen monkey corpses in the T-Rex's canyon. Described as screechy and prone to alerting the T-Rex when they spot something unknown. The party identified them as a threat to stealth operations. They were not engaged in combat but served as a potential alarm system for [[Ransom's Rex]].

Skeletons

A horde of nine skeletons discovered behind a blocked door that [[Moor]] was digging through. Seven were instantly destroyed by Moor's Turn Undead (Channel Divinity), and the remaining two were killed by mirror beams reflected through the room's traps. The DM noted the skeletons were created by the temple's own traps — victims of the pressure plate mechanisms.

Undead Horde

The rising dead that emerged from the Chultan jungle ground as night fell and the sun failed to rise (due to [[Aramil]] stealing the [[Eye of the Serpent]]). They devoured the surviving rival party members outside the [[Fizzle Field]] bubble. They could not penetrate Fizzleblast's protective barrier.

Crocodiles, Boas, and Other Predators

Observed on the ridges surrounding the T-Rex's canyon. None entered while the T-Rex was present. They represented the ambient danger of the Chultan jungle closing in as night fell.

Last updated after Session 1 (One-shot). Profile compiled from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip session transcript.

MVP Scorecard

MVP

🏅 MVP Scorecard — Session: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip

Scoring Weights

Category Weight Description
Plot Advancement 25% Driving the story forward through decisions, discoveries, or key actions
Combat Effectiveness 20% Impact in combat encounters — damage, tactics, clutch saves
Roleplay Depth 25% Quality and commitment to in-character portrayal
Team Play 15% Supporting other players, enabling combos, sharing spotlight
Memorable Moments 15% Creating moments the table will remember and retell

Individual Scorecards

Lavender (Kelsey) — Tiefling Warlock

Plot Advancement — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • Solved the crucial light puzzle by realizing the snake statues could be moved: "I need to get it just this way so that... Like you need to push it. So that they are aligned on this line."
  • Used Gust of Wind to clear the noxious gas room, allowing the party to proceed without taking further massive damage.
  • Sent Tika (Imp) to spy on the rival party, gaining crucial intel ("sleeping, heat, break") regarding the T-Rex.

Justification: Lavender was the intellectual engine of the dungeon. Her use of utility spells (Gust of Wind) and puzzle-solving logic (moving the statues) cleared the two biggest hurdles between the party and the final room.

Combat Effectiveness — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • Executed a highly efficient double-kill using Hellish Rebuke on the blind monkeys: "It catches on fire... that fire actually spreads to the one right next to it... You kill two of them."
  • Consistent damage output with Eldritch Blast throughout the session.
  • Used the Zephyr Reliquary (immune to fire) to walk through light beams unharmed to solve puzzles during combat.

Justification: While not the tank, Lavender turned defense into offense beautifully. The Hellish Rebuke moment was a tactical highlight, clearing the mob so others could escape.

Roleplay Depth — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • Maintained a consistent, specific character voice ("It is Taylor Swift's reputation") and relationship with her patron/familiar ("She's my best friend... Her name is Jezebel").
  • Engaged in tender PC-to-PC moments, such as comforting the blind Opal: "I hold your hand... I come over and I give you another very pretty bandana."

Justification: Kelsey fully embodied Lavender's "fabulous warlock" persona. She balanced humor with genuine care for the party, particularly Opal, adding emotional weight to a chaotic session.

Team Play — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • Offered her Genie Vessel as a sanctuary for the whole party to avoid gas/enemies (even if they didn't take it, the offer was significant).
  • Guided the blinded Opal through the dungeon: "Come to the stairs. I'll walk you over with my shield."
  • Cast Gust of Wind specifically to save the party from the gas cloud.

Justification: Lavender was the glue holding the party together. She consistently used her spell slots and actions to mitigate danger for the group rather than just herself.

Memorable Moments — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • The Hellish Rebuke chain reaction that incinerated two monkeys.
  • The "Taylor Swift" commentary on the snake artifact.

Justification: Her reactions were consistently entertaining, and her combat prowess provided one of the most cinematic kills of the night.

Composite Score: 8.40 / 10

Bob (Josh) — Halfling Rogue

Plot Advancement — 10 / 10

Evidence:

  • Solved the weight puzzle using the Indiana Jones switch method with oil flasks: "I mean, I have oil... I would say that would be five oils... I'm doing Sleight of Hand."
  • Physically retrieved the win condition (the Golden Snake Idol) from the T-Rex: "I'm going to jump off... grabbing it and put it in my bag."

Justification: Bob was the MVP of the actual heist. He executed the two most critical physical interactions with the dungeon's mechanics (the scales and the idol), directly securing the victory.

Combat Effectiveness — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • Used Sneak Attack effectively against the giant snake and monkeys.
  • Executed a brilliant distraction play: "I am going to be sneaking on the ceiling... I'm going to start screaming and making as much noise as I can running down the hallway... trying to draw them away."

Justification: While he didn't deal the most raw damage, his distraction tactic against the monkey horde was a masterclass in rogue warfare, preventing the party from being swarmed.

Roleplay Depth — 7 / 10

Evidence:

  • Intro was hilarious and set the tone: "Bob... Bob... Bob... Bob..."
  • Played the "professional thief" angle well, focusing on loot and mechanics ("How many squirtons in a gusher?").

Justification: Bob was a distinct, funny character. The RP was often functional/comedic, fitting the tone of the one-shot perfectly.

Team Play — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • Put himself in extreme danger to draw the monkeys away from the rest of the group.
  • Helped Opal move the mirror despite the size difference: "I'm literally in between your legs... helping."

Justification: The distraction play was a selfless move that likely saved the slower party members. He acted as a scout and a decoy effectively.

Memorable Moments — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • The ceiling run/distraction play to lure away the monkeys.
  • The Indiana Jones moment swapping the oil for the scales.
  • Flying away with the idol using the Cloak of the Bat ("I'm a motherfucking knight").

Justification: Bob had the coolest action-movie sequences of the session. The scale swap and the final flight were quintessential D&D high-roller moments.

Composite Score: 8.40 / 10

Opal (Shannon) — Goliath Barbarian

Plot Advancement — 6 / 10

Evidence:

  • Used raw strength to enable progression, holding up the swords/portcullis: "You both pull the swords down... holding the mechanism."
  • Attempted to clean the mural (though partially destroyed it), revealing the button mechanism.

Justification: Opal wasn't solving the riddles, but she was the physical key that unlocked the heavy doors and moved the heavy mirrors. Essential, if blunt, progression.

Combat Effectiveness — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • Landed the killing blow on the Giant Constrictor Snake with a Critical Hit: "I crit it with my Great Axe... I'm going to chop like a cake."
  • Tanked massive damage (snake grapple, fire beams) so others didn't have to.

Justification: A classic barbarian performance. She took the hits, dealt the big crit when it mattered most, and physically dominated the space.

Roleplay Depth — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • Played the "blindness" condition with commitment and humor: "Some of us have an extra spell slot, but we don't have any spells... Yet. Yet." and "I'm just going to cry about how I'm blind."
  • Simple but evocative character work: "Goliath feel calm or angry."

Justification: Shannon turned a debilitating mechanical penalty (blindness) into the session's best running gag and emotional hook, creating opportunities for others to help her.

Team Play — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • Physically carried/shielded teammates during the "Man and Crocodile" walk.
  • Acted as the muscle for every group task (moving mirrors, holding doors).

Justification: Opal was the literal and metaphorical anchor of the team. She enabled the squishier characters to do their cool things by holding the world up for them.

Memorable Moments — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • The critical hit description: "Chop like a cake, but less delicately."
  • The comedy of being a blind giant stumbling through a laser dungeon.

Justification: Her combat finisher was the highlight of the snake fight, and her blindness provided constant levity.

Composite Score: 8.10 / 10

Moor (Kyle 1) — Dragonborn Cleric

Plot Advancement — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • Figured out the "Man and Crocodile" synchronized walking puzzle logic.
  • Grabbed the scales of justice (triggering the trap, but acquiring the item needed for the puzzle).

Justification: Moor was quietly solving the logic puzzles while others executed them. His deduction on the synchronized walking was the key to crossing the trapped room.

Combat Effectiveness — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • Used Turn Undead to instantly destroy 7 out of 9 skeletons, trivializing a potentially deadly encounter.
  • Cast Mass Cure Wounds to erase the massive gas damage the party took, preventing a TPK state.

Justification: Moor was the MVP of survival. Without his Turn Undead and Mass Cure Wounds, the party likely dies in the gas room or gets overwhelmed by skeletons.

Roleplay Depth — 6 / 10

Evidence:

  • Played the "straight man" to the group's chaos: "Why use many words? Do trick."
  • Showed concern for the party's health and cohesion amid the greed.

Justification: Solid, functional RP. He kept the group grounded, though he had fewer "spotlight" character moments than the wackier party members.

Team Play — 10 / 10

Evidence:

  • Mass Cure Wounds, Aid, Shield of Faith — poured all resources into keeping the team alive.
  • "I'm going to toss the rock over near the T-Rex so that you can at least see what you're doing." (Helping Bob sneak).

Justification: The definition of a support player. He healed, buffed, lit up the area for the rogue, and cleared minions. A perfect 10 for enabling the party's success.

Memorable Moments — 7 / 10

Evidence:

  • The Turn Undead moment where the skeletons exploded into bone meal.
  • The "Do trick" logic explanation.

Justification: His quiet competence saved the day, even if it wasn't flashy. The skeleton explosion was his cinematic peak.

Composite Score: 7.85 / 10

Aramil (Kyle 2) — Half-Elf Warlock

Plot Advancement — 9 / 10

Evidence:

  • Identified the "Man and Crocodile" mural meaning via history check.
  • MAJOR: Stole the Obsidian Eye from the mechanism, plunging the entire continent of Faerun into eternal darkness: "You have plunged the entire continent into eternal darkness... Vecna is laughing."

Justification: While Bob secured the mission success, Aramil drove the world plot. His decision to steal the eye had the biggest narrative consequence of the entire session (setting up Tomb of Annihilation).

Combat Effectiveness — 5 / 10

Evidence:

  • Got grappled and knocked unconscious by the snake ("I am at negative 10").
  • Helped finish the snake after being revived.
  • Cast Phantasmal Force at the end to delay the rival party.

Justification: He spent the major boss fight unconscious, which hurts the score. However, his final spell to delay the rivals was a smart delaying tactic.

Roleplay Depth — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • Played the "washed up/arrogant archaeologist" trope perfectly: "I am adjunct professor emeritus... Maybe my dad finally noticed me."
  • Tried to reason with the snake ("Brother Snake") before getting crushed by it.

Justification: Excellent commitment to a flawed, greedy character. His hubris (stealing the eye) was perfectly in-character, even if it doomed the world.

Team Play — 6 / 10

Evidence:

  • Used Dimension Door to save himself and Moor at the very end.
  • Generally acted in self-interest (looting, stealing the eye) rather than group cohesion.

Justification: He saved the healer at the end, which saves his score, but he was generally the most self-serving member of the party (which fit his character).

Memorable Moments — 8 / 10

Evidence:

  • Being squeezed to death by a snake he tried to befriend.
  • The ending reveal that he stole the eye and doomed the sun.

Justification: The twist ending was entirely his doing. It turned a generic "we escaped" ending into a "we escaped but at what cost" cliffhanger.

Composite Score: 7.35 / 10

🏆 Final MVP Ranking

Rank Character Player Plot (25%) Combat (20%) RP (25%) Team (15%) Moments (15%) Weighted Total
1 Lavender Kelsey 8.0 8.0 9.0 9.0 8.0 8.40
2 Bob Josh 10.0 8.0 7.0 8.0 9.0 8.40
3 Opal Shannon 6.0 9.0 9.0 9.0 8.0 8.10
4 Moor Kyle 1 8.0 9.0 6.0 10.0 7.0 7.85
5 Aramil Kyle 2 9.0 5.0 8.0 6.0 8.0 7.35

Score Spread Analysis

  • Tightest Race: The battle for #1 was a dead heat between Lavender and Bob (both 8.40). The tie was broken by Roleplay Depth (Lavender's 9 vs. Bob's 7), giving the edge to Kelsey.
  • Biggest Gap: The gap between Moor (7.85) and Aramil (7.35) — 0.50 points — reflects the weight of Moor's perfect Team Play (10/10) as much as anything else. Notably, the creative decisions that led to Aramil's snake encounter earned him strong RP and Memorable Moments scores; his limited combat window was a consequence, not a verdict.
  • Most Balanced Player: Lavender (Kelsey). She didn't score below an 8 in any category. A flawless all-rounder performance.
  • Most Specialized Player: Moor (Kyle 1). He scored a perfect 10 in Team Play but lower in RP/Moments, reflecting a pure support playstyle.

🎖️ Category Awards

Award Winner Score Key Evidence
🗺️ Plot Driver Bob (Josh) 10/10 Physically executed the scale puzzle swap and the T-Rex heist to secure the win.
⚔️ Combat Champion Moor (Kyle 1) & Opal (Shannon) 9/10 Moor's Turn Undead and Mass Cure Wounds saved the party; Opal's critical hit ended the boss fight.
🎭 Roleplay Royalty Lavender (Kelsey) & Opal (Shannon) 9/10 Lavender's consistent fabulous persona and Opal turning blindness into the session's best ongoing arc.
🤝 Team Player Moor (Kyle 1) 10/10 Kept the party alive through gas, fire, and snakes with selfless resource use.
✨ Moment Maker Bob (Josh) 9/10 The ceiling run, the Indiana Jones swap, and the final flight were cinematic gold.
🌟 Session MVP Lavender (Kelsey) 8.4/10 The intellectual and social leader who solved the puzzles, managed the party, and blasted the enemies.

Honorable Mentions

  • Lavender (Kelsey): Her use of the Gust of Wind spell was a subtle but critical move that prevented the party from taking massive poison damage, showing great spell utility.
  • Bob (Josh): His willingness to split from the party and draw aggro from the monkeys was a brave, high-risk play that paid off beautifully for the team.
  • Opal (Shannon): Despite rolling poorly on intelligence checks, her player instinct to chop the snake "like a cake" resulted in a satisfying, gore-filled victory.
  • Moor (Kyle 1): His calm demeanor ("Do trick") provided a perfect comedic foil to the chaotic energy of the Warlocks and Rogue.
  • Aramil (Kyle 2): While he doomed the world, his Dimension Door at the buzzer ensured that the party's healer made it out alive, preventing a partial TPK.

Encounter Narrative: Moor used Turn Undead (Destroy Undead) to instantly obliterate seven skeletons. The party took significant damage from the room's blade traps and gas before Lavender dispersed the toxins with Gust of Wind.

Encounter 3: The Giant Constrictor Snake

Encounter Narrative: A brutal close-quarters fight. Aramil was constricted and dropped to 0 HP instantly. Bob provided consistent chip damage, but Opal ended the fight with a massive critical hit of 30 damage, cleaving the snake in half. Moor’s healing kept the party from a death state.

Encounter 4: The T-Rex Escape

Rounds: 3 [Skill Challenge]

Encounter Narrative: The party focused on movement. Lavender accidentally hit Moor with a friendly-fire Eldritch Blast during a fumble. Bob successfully kited a pack of baboons while flying. The encounter ended when the party reached the safe zone as rival mages were devoured.

Missed Opportunity Analysis

  • The Gas Trap: Moor prioritized Preserve Life immediately after the damage occurred, which was the optimal use of resources given the party's low levels and the continuing threat of the gas.
  • Lavender's Friendly Fire: When Lavender accidentally blasted Moor, he did not immediately heal himself, prioritizing the party's movement. This was a tactical choice that paid off, though it left him at a higher risk of being downed by the scythe trap later.

Self-Sufficiency Ratings

Character Self-Healing Available Self-Healing Used External Healing Received Self-Sufficiency Rating
Moor Entire Kit 19 0 A
Opal Supreme Potion 0 26 D
Aramil None 0 28 F
Lavender None 0 22 D
Bob None 0 22 D

Party Sustainability Analysis

Damage Distribution

Character Damage Taken % of Party Total Expected Share (20%) Over/Under
Moor 33 19.3% 20% -0.7%
Opal 42 24.5% 20% +4.5%
Aramil 51 29.8% 20% +9.8%
Lavender 27 15.8% 20% -4.2%
Bob 31 18.1% 20% -1.9%

Interpretation: Damage was relatively well-spread due to the "all-hit" traps (Gas and Scythes). Aramil took the largest disproportionate share due to the Snake's critical focus, while Lavender's fire immunity and range kept her the healthiest.

Healing Distribution

Character Healing Received % of Total Healing Damage Taken Healing Gap
Moor 19 12.8% 33 14
Opal 26 17.6% 42 16
Aramil 28 18.9% 51 23
Lavender 22 14.9% 27 5
Bob 22 14.9% 31 9

Interpretation: Healing was prioritized for Aramil (rightfully so, as he was downed) and the frontline. Moor took the largest healing deficit relative to his max HP, effectively "tanking" through his own survival.

Party Health Timeline

Moment Party HP % (est.) Status Trigger
Session Start 100% Healthy —
Mirror Room 88% Healthy Opal hit by beam
Post-Gas Trap 36% Critical Entire party hit by 14 damage trap
Post-Moor Heal 61% Strained Preserve Life/Mass Cure Wounds
Snake Combat 44% Strained Aramil downed (0 HP)
Exit Challenge 40% Strained Multi-person Scythe Trap damage
Session End 65% Strained Final escape reached

Party Sustainability Rating

Rating: B-

The party survived several high-lethality moments (Toxic Gas and the T-Rex chase) primarily due to the discovery of the Staff of Healing. Without that mid-session loot, the party aggregate HP would have likely zeroed out during the final escape sequence.

Key Findings

  1. The Healer's MVP Status: Moor utilized the Staff of Healing and Life Cleric features with near-perfect timing, effectively resetting the party's health pool twice.
  2. The "Trap-Heavy" Toll: Over 60% of total party damage came from environmental traps (Gas, Scythes, Heat) rather than direct monster attacks.
  3. The Glass Cannon: Aramil absorbed nearly 30% of the party's damage and was the only character to be downed, highlighting the vulnerability of the Warlock when targeted by high-CR monsters like the Giant Snake.

    4. Recommendations

    • Asset Redistribution: The party has a Potion of Supreme Healing on Opal. Given her high HP and damage resistance (while raging), this is a massive emergency reserve.
    • Environmental Awareness: The "Scythe Trap" failure during the skill challenge was a major health sink. The data suggests the party excels in combat but struggles with coordinated dex-based hazards.
    • Resource Management: Moor is low on Staff charges. If the adventure continues without a Long Rest, the party will need to rely more heavily on Bob's stealth and Opal's rage to mitigate damage before it happens.

HOT STREAK DETECTED — Opal — 4 Rolls
20, 15, 14, 16
Session avg during streak: 16.25
Lifting mechanisms and moving heavy statues
Luck Deviation Over Time
SESSION LUCK NARRATIVE
The dice gods clearly favored the party's rogue and barbarian, with Bob and Opal both maintaining averages nearly 5 points above the statistical norm. Bob's high initiative and clutch fireball dodge ensured his survival while carrying the heavy idol. Opal's series of natural 20s turned potentially lethal encounters into mere roadblocks, particularly against the giant snake. Meanwhile, Aramil suffered the session's most thematic failure, rolling a natural 1 while searching for treasure, highlighting his 'dirty Indiana Jones' aesthetic.
Combat Replay

Combat

Combat Replay Timeline — Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip

Session Overview

  • Session: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip (One-shot)
  • System: D&D 5e, All Players Level 3
  • DM: Brandon (Blue Orbit)
  • Total Encounters Identified: 3 (plus 1 skill challenge escape with combat elements)
  • Total Combat Rounds: ~8 across structured encounters
  • Total Structured Turns: ~22

Encounter 1: Gorallon Ambush at the Temple Entrance

Summary: Two Gorallons (giant jungle apes) attacked the party as they arrived at the temple. Moor cleverly turned a thermal mirror to incinerate Gorallon 1 without a single attack roll. Lavender blasted Gorallon 2 with Eldritch Blast for 9 damage, and Aramil used Speak with Animals to intimidate it into fleeing (rolling an 18+4=22 on Intimidation with advantage after a Nat 1 on the first roll).

Rounds: ~1 (not formally structured with initiative, but initiative was rolled: Lavender 19, Bob ~20, Opal 12-13, Aramil 3)

Play-by-Play Commentary

Round 1 kicks off at the forbidden shores of Chult, and the party barely has time to admire the scenery before two massive Gorallons come charging out of the jungle! But this party has BRAINS over brawn today. Moor, the stoic Dragonborn Cleric, steps up first and asks the million-gold-piece question: "Can I turn the mirror?" An 18 on the Strength check — MEETS IT, BEATS IT — and he angles that heliographic thermal array directly at Gorallon Number One. The beam hits, the ape tries to dodge, walks into ANOTHER beam, and we get front-row seats to what can only be described as "cooked steak plus burnt hair." One Gorallon down without a single weapon swing! Lavender follows up with a crisp Eldritch Blast — 13 to hit, connects! — dealing 9 damage (7 force + 2 thunder from the Genie patron) to Gorallon Number Two. Then Aramil steps up, channeling his inner Indiana Jones, and starts TALKING to the beast using Speak with Animals. "Think about your wife and kids," he says. First Intimidation roll? A NATURAL ONE. The crowd winces. But with advantage from speaking in its own language, he re-rolls an 18+4 for a MONSTROUS 22. The Gorallon's demeanor shifts completely — it freaks out, bolts through the jungle, and the entrance is CLEAR. Opal tried to move a mirror too (rolling a Nat 1 on Strength, raging as a bonus action, then getting a 16 with advantage — but the DC was 18, so she couldn't budge it). Bob, ever the team player, helped her with the assist, and together they moved it on a natural 20. Encounter over before the real dungeon even starts!

Encounter 2: Giant Constrictor Snake — The Crystal Prism Room

Summary: A Giant Constrictor Snake (with venomous bite — a Vipoa variant) emerged from a snake statue's mouth in the crystal prism room. This was the session's main combat encounter. Initiative was rolled: Lavender 19, Opal dirty 20, Snake (DM). The snake bloodied quickly but downed Aramil to 0 HP with a devastating 22-damage constriction. Opal finished it with a CRITICAL HIT from her Greataxe for 30 damage while blind from the earlier mirror incident (her blindness had worn off by this point per the 1-hour timer). Moor healed Aramil with Cure Wounds, bringing him back to 15 HP.

Rounds: 3

Combatants: Opal, Lavender, Bob, Moor, Aramil, Tika (Imp/Raven familiar), Giant Constrictor Snake

Play-by-Play Commentary

Welcome to the MAIN EVENT, folks! A Giant Constrictor Snake slithers out of a stone serpent's mouth as the heat cranks up in the crystal prism room. Initiative is rolled — Lavender with a 19, Opal with a dirty 20, and the snake is ready to rumble!

ROUND 1 opens with Aramil firing an Eldritch Blast — a 9 to hit — MISS! The blast sails right over the snake's head. Opal charges in with her Greataxe swinging — a 12 to hit — also MISSES! The snake is more agile than it looks! But Aramil (from the prior initiative block) fires ANOTHER Eldritch Blast — 16 to hit — CONNECTS for 6 damage! Lavender's familiar Tika, shapeshifted into a raven, swoops in for an eye-poke — 19 to hit with the beak — 7 piercing damage and GOUGES OUT ONE EYE! Moor steps up and casts Toll the Dead — the snake fails its Wisdom save against DC 15 (not wise at all!) — and takes 8 necrotic damage from the d12 (already damaged). Then Bob cycles around for Line of Sight and fires his shortbow — 15 to hit with Sneak Attack (Opal is within 5 feet) — 8 damage! The DM announces the snake is now BLOODIED — half health gone!

ROUND 2 — the snake STRIKES BACK. It bites at Tika the raven — an 18 to hit against AC 13. Lavender uses her Investment of the Chain Master reaction to grant resistance, but the bite deals 6 damage after resistance to the imp (who has 21 HP in imp form, not raven's 1 HP). Constitution save for the paralysis venom — a 7 — FAILED. Tika is PARALYZED. The snake's second attack is a constriction attempt on Opal! She needs a Strength save — rolls a 16+8 = 24 with her Belt of Hill Giant Strength — she FLEXES so hard the snake literally cannot wrap around her! Bob fires again with advantage (Vex applied) — 16+6 to hit, CONNECTS for another 8 damage with Sneak Attack. The rolls are cruel (1, 1, 2 on the d6s) but the damage stacks up!

The snake gets one more turn — it auto-crits the paralyzed Tika (destroying the familiar), then slithers toward Aramil and attempts a grapple. Aramil rolls Athletics — a FIVE. The snake's grapple succeeds with an 18. It squeezes for 3d8+4 = 22 DEVASTATING DAMAGE. Aramil drops to NEGATIVE 10 HP — he's DOWN! The crowd gasps!

ROUND 3 — COMEBACK TIME. Opal steps up with her Greataxe and rolls... A NATURAL 20! CRITICAL HIT! The first crit of the entire session! She rolls 2d12+6 — 12+12+6 = 30 DAMAGE! She chops through the coiled snake like a layered cake, slicing through multiple loops wrapped around Aramil! THE SNAKE IS DEAD! Meanwhile, Moor rushes in and casts Cure Wounds on the unconscious Aramil, bringing him back to 15 HP. WHAT A FINISH! Opal, the Goliath Barbarian, proving that sometimes you just need a big axe and a lucky roll!

Encounter 3: Baboon Swarm Skirmish

Summary: A swarm of feral baboons and injured Gorallons dropped into the crystal prism room from the roof as darkness fell. This was a running fight rather than a structured combat — the party was trying to solve the mirror puzzle and escape while dealing with incoming creatures. Aramil killed one baboon with Eldritch Blast (11 hits, 9 damage). Lavender rolled a Nat 1 on her Eldritch Blast and hit Moor for 5 friendly fire force damage. Lavender killed 2 baboons with Hellish Rebuke (2d10 fire damage, rolled 10, halved to 5 on the dex save but enough to kill two bloodied baboons). Bob distracted the remaining baboons by screaming and running down a hallway, then used his Spider Climbing Slippers to sneak onto the ceiling (Stealth 27).

Play-by-Play Commentary

The jungle is closing in, folks! Feral baboons are RAINING from the roof like furry, angry hail! Their skin is half-melted from the thermal mirrors, but they're still coming! Moor casts Shield of Faith on Lavender (+2 AC), and Aramil fires an Eldritch Blast — 11 to hit — CONNECTS on the weakened baboons for 9 damage, popping one like a grape!

Lavender winds up her own Eldritch Blast and... rolls a NATURAL ONE. OH NO. The worst case scenario — a baboon jumps on her as she's casting and she BLASTS MOOR RIGHT IN THE FACE for 5 force damage! Friendly fire! The Dragonborn Cleric takes it like a champ. Then the baboons bite at Lavender — 19 to hit — 5 damage gets through. But Lavender fires back with HELLISH REBUKE! 2d10 fire damage — she rolls a 10. The baboon makes its Dex save (at disadvantage because it's blind) with a 14 against her DC 14 — meets it, so half damage, but 5 fire damage is enough! The volatile heat in the room causes the flames to SPREAD to the adjacent baboon, killing BOTH of them!

Then Bob pulls the play of the sequence — he starts SCREAMING and running down the hallway, drawing every remaining baboon after him with a performance check. Once out of sight, he runs up the WALL with his Spider Climbing Slippers, sneaks across the ceiling with a 27 Stealth check, and the baboons charge right into the spike trap room! BRILLIANT!

Encounter 4: Rival Party Ambush (Skill Challenge / Brief Combat)

Summary: During the final escape, two rival mages (from the Red Fangs/Black Skulls) Dimension Doored in front of the party at the temple exit. Bob was also hit by a Fireball from the rooftop (Dex save 24, half damage = 9 fire). The T-Rex took the full Fireball and woke up, triggering a stampede. Moor cast Command ("Flee") on one mage — Wisdom save DC 12, the mage saved. Aramil cast Phantasmal Force (raptors chasing them) on the other — Intelligence save resulted in a Nat 1, causing that mage to grab his buddy and flee. The party barely made it into Fizzleblast's safety bubble.

Play-by-Play Commentary

IT ALL COMES DOWN TO THIS! Bob has the golden snake idol in his bag, the T-Rex just got hit by a FIREBALL from the rival adventurers (Bob took 9 damage on a half-save of 24 — the T-Rex took the FULL brunt!), and now it's JURASSIC PARK out here! The archway crumbles, the T-Rex is FURIOUS, every apex predator in the canyon is going berserk, and the party has FOUR MINUTES AND TWENTY SECONDS to reach Fizzleblast's bubble!

The skill challenge is INTENSE — Athletics check from Opal (14, pass!), Acrobatics from Lavender (17, pass!), Acrobatics from Bob (18, pass!), then Opal and Moor pull the sword mechanisms (Opal 19 with Guidance, barely making the DC!). But disaster strikes on the pressure plates — Moor and Aramil step wrong and BLADES shoot up for 13 damage EACH! That's TWO FAILURES out of THREE allowed!

Bob launches himself 36 feet with the Boots of Striding and Springing — CLEAR! They reach the stone door, everyone pulls the ropes (combined rolls of 28 and 22 — JUST enough!), and then — DIMENSION DOOR — two rival mages appear blocking the exit!

Moor fires off Command: "FLEE!" — the first mage saves. Aramil casts Phantasmal Force, creating an illusion of raptors charging — the second mage rolls a NATURAL ONE on the Intelligence save! He PANICS, grabs his buddy, and they BOTH run! Opal flips them off as the party sprints into Fizzleblast's safety bubble with seconds to spare! THE PARTY SURVIVES! ALL FIVE MEMBERS! A feat that has apparently NEVER been accomplished in this one-shot before!

```json
"notable": "Terrible Sneak Attack damage rolls again (1, 1, 2 on d6s). Bob lamented his dice.",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "16 plus six to hit... Sneak attack again... another eight damage. Fuck my life, dude. Three D6s in a row, a one, a one, and a two"

},

"reaction": null,

},

{

"turn_index": 17,

"round": 3,

"combatant_id": "opal",

"combatant_name": "Opal",

"side": "Party",

"action_type": "Attack",

"action_detail": "Greataxe Attack — CRITICAL HIT (Natural 20)",

"target": "Giant Constrictor Snake",

"attack_roll": 20,

"hit": true,

"is_crit": true,

"iscritfail": false,

"damage_dealt": 30,

"damage_type": "slashing",

"healing_done": null,

"condition_inflicted": null,

"condition_received": null,

"damagetakenthis_turn": null,

"is_downed": false,

"is_killed": false,

"notable": "CRITICAL HIT! First crit of the session! 2d12+6 = 12+12+6 = 30 damage! Killed the snake! Opal chopped through the coiled snake 'like a cake but less delicately.' Previously blinded by the mirror beam, proving even temporary setbacks can't stop a Goliath.",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "Critical!... Maybe the first one ever... Greataxe is d12... Plus 12 is 24. Plus 6 is 30... You're going to kill it"

},

{

"turn_index": 18,

"round": 3,

"combatant_id": "snake",

"combatant_name": "Giant Constrictor Snake",

"side": "Enemy",

"action_type": "Death/Downed",

"action_detail": "Killed by Opal's critical hit Greataxe",

"target": null,

"attack_roll": null,

"hit": null,

"is_crit": false,

"iscritfail": false,

"damage_dealt": null,

"damage_type": null,

"healing_done": null,

"condition_inflicted": null,

"condition_received": "dead",

"damagetakenthis_turn": 30,

"is_downed": true,

"is_killed": true,

"notable": "Chopped through multiple coiled layers while wrapped around Aramil",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "You're going to off it. You're going to kill it... I'm going to chop like a cake, but less delicately"

},

{

"turn_index": 19,

"round": 3,

"combatant_id": "moor",

"combatant_name": "Moor",

"target": "Aramil",

"attack_roll": null,

"hit": null,

],

"round_summaries": [

{

"round": 1,

"summary": "Opening salvo of attacks. Aramil and Opal both missed (9 and 12 vs AC 14). Aramil's second Eldritch Blast connected for 6 damage. Tika gouged out an eye for 7 damage. Moor's Toll the Dead dealt 8 necrotic, and Bob's Sneak Attack shortbow added 8 more. Snake bloodied.",

"round_mvp": "Bob",

"totaldamagedealt": 29,

"casualties": []

},

{

"round": 2,

"summary": "Snake struck back — bit and paralyzed Tika, tried to constrict Opal but her 24 Strength save held firm. Bob hit for another 8. Opal and others missed. Moor healed Opal for 14 HP via Staff of Healing.",

"round_mvp": "Moor",

"totaldamagedealt": 8,

"casualties": []

},

{

"round": 3,

"summary": "The climactic round. Snake auto-critted paralyzed Tika (destroying the familiar), then constricted Aramil for 22 damage, dropping him to 0 HP. Opal answered with a NATURAL 20 CRIT on her Greataxe for 30 damage, killing the snake! Moor revived Aramil with Cure Wounds to 15 HP.",

"round_mvp": "Opal",

"totaldamagedealt": 52,

"casualties": ["Tika (familiar destroyed)", "Aramil (downed to 0 HP, revived)", "Giant Constrictor Snake (killed)"]

}

],

"encounter_stats": {

"totaldamagedealtbyparty": 67,

"totaldamagedealtbyenemies": 27,

"total_healing": 29,

"partymembersdowned": 1,

"enemies_defeated": 1,

"critsbyparty": 1,

"critsbyenemies": 1,

"longest_round": 1,

"deadliest_round": 3

},

"playbyplay": "The MAIN EVENT! A Giant Constrictor Snake slithers out of a stone serpent's mouth. Round 1 opens with misses from Aramil (9) and Opal (12) — AC 14 is tricky! But Aramil's second Eldritch Blast connects at 16 for 6 damage! Tika the raven swoops in — 19 to hit — GOUGES OUT AN EYE for 7 piercing! Moor drops Toll the Dead for 8 necrotic, and Bob's Sneak Attack shortbow adds 8 more. The snake is BLOODIED! Round 2 the snake strikes back — bites Tika for 5 (halved by Lavender's reaction), paralyzing the familiar on a failed Con save of 7! It tries to constrict Opal but her 24 Strength save says NO WAY. Bob nails another 8 damage. Moor heals Opal for 14 HP. Round 3 — CHAOS. The snake auto-crits paralyzed Tika, destroying the familiar! Then it grapples Aramil (he rolled a 5) and SQUEEZES for 22 DAMAGE! Aramil drops to negative 10 HP! But Opal steps up and rolls a NATURAL 20! CRITICAL HIT! 2d12+6 = 30 DAMAGE! She CLEAVES through the coiled snake like layered cake! DEAD! Moor rushes in with Cure Wounds, bringing Aramil back to 15 HP. WHAT. A. FINISH!"

},

{

"encounter_number": 3,

"encounter_name": "Baboon Swarm Skirmish",

"encounter_summary": "Feral baboons and injured Gorallons dropped into the rooms from the roof as darkness fell. The party fought while solving puzzles. Aramil killed a baboon with Eldritch Blast, Lavender accidentally hit Moor with a Nat 1 Eldritch Blast (5 friendly fire), then killed 2 baboons with Hellish Rebuke. Bob distracted the rest by screaming and running, then hiding on the ceiling.",

"total_rounds": 2,

"combatants": [

{ "id": "aramil", "name": "Aramil", "side": "Party", "ispc": true, "swimlaneindex": 0, "color": "#81C784", "statusat_end": "Alive" },

"condition_inflicted": null,

"condition_received": null,

"damagetakenthis_turn": null,

"is_downed": false,

"is_killed": false,

"notable": "Killed the baboon outright — they are very fragile",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "I'll send an Eldritch Blast at the nearest one... 11 hits... Nine"

},

{

"turn_index": 3,

"round": 1,

"combatant_id": "lavender",

"combatant_name": "Lavender",

"side": "Party",

"action_type": "Attack",

"action_detail": "Eldritch Blast — CRITICAL FAIL (Nat 1, hit Moor for friendly fire)",

"target": "Baboon (intended) / Moor (actual)",

"attack_roll": 1,

"hit": false,

"is_crit": false,

"iscritfail": true,

"damage_dealt": 5,

"damage_type": "force (friendly fire)",

"healing_done": null,

"condition_inflicted": null,

"condition_received": null,

"damagetakenthis_turn": null,

"is_downed": false,

"is_killed": false,

"notable": "Nat 1! A baboon jumped on Lavender as she was casting, and she blasted Moor in the face for 5 force damage. Friendly fire!",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "I rolled a 1... one of the baboons jumps up onto you as you're trying to cast your Eldritch Blast and you turn and you cast it at Moor... 5 friendly fire damage"

},

{

"turn_index": 4,

"round": 1,

"combatantid": "baboonpack",

"combatant_name": "Baboon Pack",

"side": "Enemy",

"action_type": "Attack",

"action_detail": "Bite Attack (Pack Tactics, advantage) on Lavender",

"target": "Lavender",

"attack_roll": 19,

"hit": true,

"is_crit": false,

"iscritfail": false,

"damage_dealt": 5,

"damage_type": "piercing",

"healing_done": null,

"condition_inflicted": null,

"condition_received": null,

"damagetakenthis_turn": null,

"is_downed": false,

"is_killed": false,

"notable": "1d4+2 = 5 damage. Triggered Lavender's Hellish Rebuke reaction.",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "it's going to try to actually take a bite... 19... 1d4 plus two... Five damage"

},

{

"turn_index": 5,

"round": 1,

"combatant_id": "lavender",

"combatant_name": "Lavender",

"side": "Party",

"action_type": "Spell (Damage)",

"action_detail": "Hellish Rebuke (Reaction, 2d10 fire damage)",

"target": "Baboon (2 killed by fire spread)",

"attack_roll": null,

"hit": true,

"is_crit": false,

"iscritfail": false,

"damage_dealt": 10,

"damage_type": "fire",

"healing_done": null,

"condition_inflicted": null,

"condition_received": null,

"damagetakenthis_turn": 5,

"is_downed": false,

"is_killed": false,

"notable": "Rolled 10 on 2d10, baboon saved (DC 14 met) for half = 5 damage, but enough to kill it. The volatile heat caused the fire to spread to an adjacent baboon, killing both!",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "Hellish Rebuke... 2d10 fire damage... 10... fire actually spreads to the one right next to it... You kill two of them"

},

{

"turn_index": 6,

"round": 2,

"combatant_id": "bob",

"combatant_name": "Bob",

"side": "Party",

"action_type": "Other",

"action_detail": "Distraction — screaming and running to lure baboons, then Stealth on ceiling (27)",

"target": "Baboon Pack",

"attack_roll": null,

"hit": null,

"is_crit": false,

"iscritfail": false,

"damage_dealt": 0,

"damage_type": null,

"healing_done": null,

"condition_inflicted": null,

"condition_received": null,

"damagetakenthis_turn": null,

"is_downed": false,

"is_killed": false,

"notable": "Bob screamed and ran to draw the baboons into the spike trap room, then used Spider Climbing Slippers to run up the wall and ceiling. Stealth check: 19+8=27. The baboons ran into the spike traps.",

"bonus_action": null,

"reaction": null,

"transcript_excerpt": "I'm going to start screaming and making as much noise as I can running down the hallway... 19 plus 8, 27. They have no idea where you went... you hear the spikes in the other room"

}

],

{

"round": 1,

"summary": "Moor buffed Lavender with Shield of Faith. Aramil killed a baboon with Eldritch Blast (9 damage). Lavender rolled a Nat 1 and blasted Moor for 5 friendly fire. A baboon bit Lavender for 5 damage, triggering Hellish Rebuke which killed 2 baboons via fire spread.",

"round_mvp": "Lavender",

"totaldamagedealt": 24,

"casualties": ["3 baboons killed"]

},

"encounter_stats": {

"totaldamagedealtbyparty": 24,

"totaldamagedealtbyenemies": 5,

"total_healing": 0,

"enemies_defeated": 5,

"critsbyenemies": 0,

"longest_round": 1,

"deadliest_round": 1

},

"playbyplay": "The jungle is closing in! Feral baboons are RAINING from the roof! Moor buffs Lavender with Shield of Faith, and Aramil fires an Eldritch Blast — 11 to hit — POPS a baboon for 9 damage! Lavender winds up her own blast and rolls a NATURAL ONE! A baboon jumps on her mid-cast and she BLASTS MOOR IN THE FACE for 5 force damage! FRIENDLY FIRE! Then a baboon bites Lavender for 5 damage — but she fires back with HELLISH REBUKE! 2d10 fire, rolled 10! The volatile heat causes flames to SPREAD to the adjacent baboon, killing BOTH! Then Bob pulls the play of the night — screaming and running down the hallway, drawing every baboon after him, then RUNNING UP THE WALL with Spider Climb Slippers and sneaking on the ceiling with a 27 Stealth! The baboons charge straight into the spike traps! BRILLIANT!"

},

{

"encounter_number": 4,

"encounter_name": "Escape and Rival Party Ambush",

"encounter_summary": "During the final timed escape (4 minutes 20 seconds), a rival party of mages attacked. Bob was hit by a Fireball for 9 damage (half on Dex save 24). Two rival mages Dimension Doored to block the exit. Moor's Command ('Flee') was saved against, but Aramil's Phantasmal Force caused a Nat 1 Intelligence save, making both mages flee. The party barely reached safety.",

"round_summaries": [

{

"round": 1,

"summary": "A rival caster on the rooftop launched a Fireball at Bob as he flew away with the idol. Bob saved (Dex 24) for half damage (9). The Fireball hit the sleeping T-Rex, waking it and triggering a stampede.",

"round_mvp": "Bob",

"totaldamagedealt": 9,

"casualties": []

},

{

"round": 2,

"summary": "Two rival mages Dimension Doored to block the exit. Moor's Command ('Flee') failed. But Aramil's Phantasmal Force caused a Nat 1 Intelligence save — the second mage grabbed his buddy and both fled, clearing the path. The party sprinted into Fizzleblast's safety bubble.",

"round_mvp": "Aramil",

"totaldamagedealt": 0,

"casualties": []

}

],

"encounter_stats": {

"totaldamagedealtbyparty": 0,

"totaldamagedealtbyenemies": 9,

"total_healing": 0,

"partymembersdowned": 0,

"enemies_defeated": 0,

"critsbyparty": 0,

"critsbyenemies": 0,

"longest_round": 2,

"deadliest_round": 1

},

"playbyplay": "IT ALL COMES DOWN TO THIS! Bob has the golden snake idol and he's Batman-caping off the archway when — FIREBALL! A rival caster on the roof launches one straight at him! Bob rolls a 24 Dex save — HALF DAMAGE, only 9 fire! But the T-Rex takes the FULL brunt and it is FURIOUS! Jurassic Park mode activated! The party has 4 minutes 20 seconds to reach the safety bubble! They blast through the skill challenge — Athletics, Acrobatics, strength checks on the sword mechanisms — taking 13 damage from blade traps (two failures out of three allowed!). Bob launches 36 feet with the Boots of Striding! They pull the ropes, the door opens, and — DIMENSION DOOR — two rival mages appear! Moor fires Command: FLEE! The first mage SAVES. Aramil casts Phantasmal Force — illusory raptors! The second mage rolls... a NATURAL ONE on Intelligence! He PANICS, grabs his buddy, and they BOTH flee! Opal flips them off as the party sprints into Fizzleblast's bubble with SECONDS to spare! ALL FIVE MEMBERS SURVIVE — a first for this one-shot!"

}

],

{ "yindex": 3, "xstart": 4, "xwidth": 1, "color": "#FF7043", "label": "Toll the Dead -> Snake (8 necrotic)", "round": 1, "iscrit": false, "tooltip": "Snake fails Wis save vs DC 15. d12 = 8 necrotic damage." },

{ "yindex": 2, "xstart": 5, "xwidth": 1, "color": "#E53935", "label": "Shortbow+Sneak -> Snake (Hit, 8 dmg) BLOODIED", "round": 1, "iscrit": false, "tooltip": "Bob rolls 15, hits with Sneak Attack for 8 piercing. Snake bloodied." },

{ "yindex": 6, "xstart": 6, "xwidth": 1, "color": "#E53935", "label": "Bite -> Tika (Hit 18, 5 dmg, paralyzed) + Constrict Opal (resisted)", "round": 2, "iscrit": false, "tooltip": "Snake bites Tika (18 vs AC 13), 5 damage after resistance. Tika paralyzed (Con 7). Constrict on Opal fails (Str 24)." },

{ "yindex": 2, "xstart": 7, "xwidth": 1, "color": "#E53935", "label": "Shortbow+Sneak -> Snake (Hit, 8 dmg)", "round": 2, "iscrit": false, "tooltip": "Bob rolls 16 with advantage (Vex), hits for 8 piercing. Bad sneak attack dice (1,1,2)." },

{ "yindex": 3, "xstart": 8, "xwidth": 1, "color": "#66BB6A", "label": "Cure Wounds -> Opal (14 HP healed)", "round": 2, "iscrit": false, "tooltip": "Moor uses Staff of Healing (1 charge) for 2d8+3 = 14 HP on Opal." },

{ "yindex": 6, "xstart": 9, "xwidth": 1, "color": "#E53935", "label": "Auto-Crit Bite -> Tika (destroyed) + Constrict Aramil (22 dmg)", "round": 3, "iscrit": true, "tooltip": "Auto-crit on paralyzed Tika, destroying the familiar. Grapples Aramil (Ath 5), squeezes for 3d8+4 = 22 damage." },

{ "yindex": 5, "xstart": 10, "xwidth": 1, "color": "#212121", "label": "DESTROYED (auto-crit)", "round": 3, "iscrit": false, "tooltip": "Tika destroyed by auto-crit. Requires 10-min ritual to resummon." },

```

Session Combat Summary

Stat Value
Total Encounters 4
Total Combat Rounds ~8
Total Party Damage Dealt ~100 HP
Total Enemy Damage Dealt ~41 HP (+ 14 poison trap to all, + 13 blade trap to Moor/Aramil)
Total Healing Done ~29 HP (in combat) + extensive out-of-combat healing by Moor
PCs Downed 1 (Aramil, revived)
Familiars Destroyed 1 (Tika, resummoned)
Enemies Killed Gorallon 1, Giant Constrictor Snake, 3+ Baboons, 1 Blind Gorallon
Enemies Fled Gorallon 2, 2 Rival Mages
Critical Hits (Party) 1 (Opal, Nat 20 Greataxe, 30 damage — the killing blow on the snake)
Critical Hits (Enemy) 1 (Snake auto-crit on paralyzed Tika)
Critical Fails (Party) 2 (Aramil Intimidation Nat 1 on Gorallon, Lavender Eldritch Blast Nat 1 hitting Moor)
Critical Fails (Enemy) 1 (Rival Mage Nat 1 Int save vs Phantasmal Force)
Combat MVP Opal (critical hit kill on the snake while previously blind)
Tactical MVP Bob (ceiling Stealth distraction, solo idol heist, Fireball survival)
Healing MVP Moor (all session healing — Staff of Healing, Cure Wounds, Aid, Prayer of Healing, Lesser Restoration)
Control MVP Aramil (Speak with Animals/Intimidation, Phantasmal Force Nat 1 on rival mages)

Analysis generated from session transcript. Damage values, attack rolls, and action sequences were extracted directly from the transcript where stated. Values marked as null in the JSON indicate information that was not clearly stated in the transcript. Round/turn assignments are estimated based on the order actions were described, as the session used a loose initiative structure for most encounters.

Session Pacing

Pacing

Session Pacing Report — Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip (Charisma Company)

Session Overview

  • Total Runtime: ~4 Hours (Estimated based on "It's two hours in" remark at the halfway break point)
  • Number of Scenes: 9
  • Scene Types Represented: Social, Looting/Admin, Puzzle, Combat, Exploration, Skill Challenge
  • Dominant Mode: Puzzle / Exploration
  • Pace Classification: Late Bloomer (Heavy mechanical/admin load in the first half; high-stakes action and rapid pacing in the final hour)

Scene Breakdown Table

# Scene Title Type Est. Start Est. End Est. Duration Key Activity Energy (1-10) Notes
1 Welcome to Chult Social / Exposition 0:00 0:25 25 min Introductions, setting the scene, meet Fizzleblast & Niles. 6 Good banter, established tone quickly.
2 The Loot Explosion Downtime / Admin 0:25 0:55 30 min Niles drops the bag; players roll for random magic items and attune. 4 Fun chaos, but slowed down by mechanics/reading item cards.
3 The Burning Entry Puzzle / Combat 0:55 1:25 30 min Mirror puzzle with the Girallons (gorilla monsters). 7 Creative puzzle solving combined with threat.
4 Man & Crocodile Puzzle 1:25 2:00 35 min Pressure plates, syncing movement, piling gold on plates. 5 Creative solution (piling gold), but execution dragged slightly.
5 The 10-Minute Lull Exploration / Looting 2:00 2:30 30 min Post-break activity; players split up to loot/investigate. 4 Momentum dip immediately following the break.
6 The Prism & The Snake Combat / Puzzle 2:30 3:10 40 min Giant snake fight while managing light beams/heat. 7 Good mix of combat and environmental hazard.
7 Scales of Justice Trap / Puzzle 3:10 3:25 15 min Bob swaps oil for the scales (Indiana Jones style). 6 Quick, tense, classic dungeon trope executed well.
8 Sleeping T-Rex & Rivals Stealth / Tension 3:25 3:45 20 min Sneaking past T-Rex to get the idol; Rival party attacks. 9 Peak tension; high stakes; immediate danger.
9 The Escape Skill Challenge 3:45 4:00 15 min Running the gauntlet to escape before the door closes. 10 Frantic, high-energy, rapid-fire rolling.

Time by Scene Type

Scene Type Total Time % of Session Scene Count Avg Scene Length
Combat 45 min 19% 2 22.5 min
Social / Roleplay 25 min 10% 1 25 min
Exploration / Looting 60 min 25% 2 30 min
Puzzle / Trap 80 min 33% 3 26 min
Skill Challenge 15 min 6% 1 15 min
Stealth / Tension 20 min 8% 1 20 min
TOTAL 240 min 100% 10 24 min

Note: Several scenes were hybrids. Classification is based on the dominant activity consuming table time.

Engagement Waveform

The session utilized a Late Bloomer structure. The first half was heavy on mechanics, logistics (carrying capacity, looting, reading item descriptions), and cautious puzzle-solving. The energy dipped significantly coming out of the mid-session break but skyrocketed in the final hour with the introduction of the T-Rex and the rival party, leading to an explosive finale.

Chunk Detail

Chunk Time Range Energy Dominant Activity Why This Rating
C1 0:00-0:24 6 Intros / Setup High engagement with character voices and funny descriptions (Fizzleblast's cashmere).
C2 0:24-0:48 4 Looting Mechanics Energy dipped as players managed inventory, checked carrying capacity, and read item cards.
C3 0:48-1:12 7 Mirror Puzzle Physical interaction with the environment; Opal burning her hands; first threat (Girallons).
C4 1:12-1:36 5 Pressure Plates A lot of logistics: "I put the mace down," "I step here." Functional but slow.
C5 1:36-2:00 5 Door / Break Prep Finishing the puzzle; transitioning to break. "Action sequence" mechanics discussed.
C6 2:00-2:24 4 Post-Break Looting 10-minute in-game timers used for looting/investigating. Slow re-start after break.
C7 2:24-2:48 7 Snake Combat "Brother Snake" dialogue attempt; combat initiation; danger levels rising.
C8 2:48-3:12 6 Scales / Puzzles Combat cleanup; trap resolution. Good focus, but procedural.
C9 3:12-3:36 9 T-Rex & Rivals Massive stakes increase. Bob flying, T-Rex waking up, Fireball surprise. Chaotic fun.
C10 3:36-4:00 10 The Escape "Run!" skill challenge. Life-or-death rolls. Perfect climax energy.

Waveform Shape: Late Bloomer / Crescendo. The session started with a plateau of moderate energy due to heavy mechanics/looting, dipped after the break, and then built aggressively toward a massive, high-stakes finale.

Pacing Metrics

Metric Value Context
Peak Energy Moment Chunk 10 (10/10) The Skill Challenge escape. The "Fireball" hitting Bob mid-air and the subsequent sprint against the clock.
Lowest Energy Valley Chunk 6 (4/10) Immediately following the break. The group spent significant time resolving "10-minute" looting actions individually.
Peak-to-Valley Range 6 High Dynamic Range. The difference between the quiet looting and the screaming escape was massive.
Longest Scene The Prism & Snake ~40 min. Combat combined with environmental puzzle elements naturally extends duration.
Shortest Scene The Escape ~15 min. Perfectly timed. Any longer would have dragged; any shorter would have felt cheap.
Longest Combat Giant Snake ~3 rounds. Felt appropriate for a mid-dungeon mini-boss.
Slowest Mechanics Inventory Management Discussions about carrying capacity, dropping items (crossbows vs. loot), and slot management bogged down Scene 2.
Scene Transition Speed Moderate The DM used "Time Passing" (10-minute chunks) to move things, which was effective but sometimes led to decision paralysis.
Planning-to-Action Ratio 1:4 Very healthy. The players rarely spent long debating; they usually tried physical solutions (piling gold, pulling ropes) quickly.

Pacing Verdict

What Worked

  • The "Ticking Clock" Mechanic: The DM tracked in-game time (sunset/heat rising) and rolled for consequences every 10 minutes. This kept the players moving even when they wanted to dither. The consequence of the "heat rising" forcing damage was excellent motivation.
  • The Climactic Skill Challenge: Transitioning from the T-Rex stealth encounter directly into a high-speed chase/escape was brilliant. It prevented a slog of a combat against a T-Rex (which might have TPK'd them) and turned it into a cinematic exit.
  • Physics-Based Puzzles: Allowing players to solve the pressure plate puzzle by piling loot/gold rather than just rolling dice made for an engaging, tactile scene.

What Dragged

  • Loot Logistics: There were two distinct phases (Scene 2 and Scene 6) where the game stopped for loot distribution. The "random bag" mechanic is fun in theory but slow in practice as players have to look up what items do, figure out attunement, and calculate carrying capacity.
  • Post-Break Momentum: Returning from the break directly into individual "10-minute looting/investigation" turns killed the table momentum. It isolated players (Bob sneaking, Lavender investigating) rather than bringing the party back together.
  • Carrying Capacity Math: The debate about dropping a crossbow vs. rations to fit gold items took up valuable time that could have been spent on the adventure.

Actionable DM Feedback

  1. Pre-Roll or Pre-assign Loot: For a one-shot or short adventure, avoid having players roll for items and then look them up mid-session. Have cards prepared or drop known items to cut the "admin time" down by 15-20 minutes.
  2. Group Actions Post-Break: When returning from a break, try to start with an immediate group threat or event (e.g., "As you sit down, you hear the T-Rex stir nearby"). Starting with individual looting turns makes it hard to rebuild energy.
  3. Simplify Encumbrance: Unless it is a gritty survival campaign, waive the strict "slots/pounds" tracking for the session. Just saying "It's heavy, you're encumbered (disadvantage)" is faster than calculating if a crossbow is 5lbs or 1 slot.

    4. Ideal Pacing Adjustment

    Reduce the duration of the initial loot drop (Scene 2) and the post-break looting (Scene 6) by 15 minutes each. Reallocate that time to the T-Rex/Rival encounter to allow for a slightly more complex interaction with the rivals before the Fireball goes off, enhancing the drama of the betrayal.

FIZZLEBLAST'S FIELDTRIP
Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip -- One-Shot Session
Net Growth 0

Performance Radar
