Generation Notes

  • Suno Version: Latest available model
  • Instrumental Toggle: Off (vocal track)
  • Duration Target: 150-180 seconds
  • Retry Strategy: If the jazz noir feels too smooth and loses the edge, add "gritty" to Style Prompt. If the hook repetitions blur together, try adding a slight tempo increase on each repetition. If the break is too quiet, add "upright bass solo" to keep momentum.
  • Variation Ideas:
  • Heist montage version: "Cinematic heist jazz, uptempo with swing, full jazz ensemble with vibraphone, confident male vocals, film score production, slick and propulsive"
  • Stripped confession version: "Lo-fi jazz ballad, slow, solo upright bass and vocal, whispered male delivery, raw recording, vulnerable and honest"

Structural Variety Check

Character Opening Chorus Position Chorus Count Ending Structure Letter
Moor [Intro (ambient)] Late (after 2 verses + pre-chorus) 2 (earned crescendo) [Whispered callback] F
Aramil [Spoken Word Intro] After Verse 1 2 (identical) [Spoken Outro] E
Opal [Chorus] Immediate (opens with it) 3 (crowd chant) [Belted final chorus] K
Lavender [Verse 1] No full chorus — refrain only 3 refrains (evolving) [Whispered quote] D
Bob [Hook] No full chorus — hook only 4 hooks (transforming) [Transformed hook] G
  • No two structures match.
  • Three different opening types (ambient intro, spoken word, chorus, verse, hook).
  • Chorus placement varies: immediate, delayed, mid-song, never (refrain/hook only).
  • Endings vary: whispered callback, spoken outro, full power belt, whispered quote, transformed hook.
  • Two songs have no traditional chorus at all (Lavender, Bob), two have full choruses (Moor, Aramil), one is almost entirely chorus (Opal).

End of Character Theme Songs

Party Theme Song - Suno AI Prompt

Campaign: Charisma Company: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip (One-Shot)

Generated: February 26, 2026

Party Theme Song: "The Sun Went Out"

For: Charisma Company

Concept Explanation

Charisma Company is a party of five strangers who answered a gnome's flier, raided a Chultan jungle temple, fought apes and snakes and baboons and a sleeping T-Rex, solved every puzzle, survived every trap — and then one of them stole a gem that ended the sun. This is not a party of noble heroes. This is a party of competent disasters who accidentally committed an apocalypse.

The song needs to hold two truths at once: they were genuinely excellent at their jobs (100% survival rate, 148 HP healed, session MVP scored 8.4, the idol heist was flawless), AND one of them doomed the continent to eternal darkness and nobody noticed. The tone is triumphant chaos — a victory anthem with a shadow creeping in at the edges. Think a pub singalong where the last verse makes everyone go quiet.

The genre choice — raucous folk-punk with a darker final act — captures the group's energy. These are people who debated how many squirtons are in a gusher, who named their mercenary company "Charisma Company" despite having a barbarian with the emotional range of "calm or angry," and who let the halfling dangle upside-down over a dinosaur because he had the best stats for it. The music should feel like a tavern full of people who are having the best night of their lives and do not yet know the sun is not coming up.

Individual characters are represented through imagery rather than names. The dragon-scaled healer, the one who swings blind, the jeweled horns walking through fire, the hood with no face, the stained fedora — each is recognizable without breaking the collective voice. The bridge pulls their actual quotes into a moment of character revelation that works as both comedy and elegy.

The central lyrical thesis: we did everything right and still broke the world, but we did it together, and that has to count for something.

Party Musical Identity

  • Genre: Raucous Folk-Punk with a Dark Turn — tavern singalong energy that descends into something heavier in the final act
  • The Group's Sound: Moor's bass-drum steadiness + Opal's stomping energy + Lavender's warmth + Bob's jazz-cool swagger + Aramil's minor-key guilt = a pub band that is one song away from a bar fight or a breakdown
  • Emotional Core: Competent misfits who accidentally ended the world — "we were the best worst thing that ever happened to Faerun"
  • Energy Level: High energy verses and chorus, contemplative bridge, explosive final chorus with an eerie quiet outro

Suno Style Prompt

```

Raucous folk-punk, driving tempo, acoustic guitar and fiddle with stomping drums and electric bass, anthemic mixed group vocals (male and female), pub singalong production, triumphant and reckless

```

Suno Lyrics

```

[Intro]

(Stomping boots, acoustic guitar riff, building crowd energy)

[Verse 1]

A gnome put up a flier and we answered every one

A cleric and a barbarian, two warlocks and a gun

We didn't know each other and we didn't really care

Charisma Company was born the moment we were there

The dragon healer led us through the pressure plates and dust

The tall one raged through mirrors that the rest of us couldn't trust

The jeweled horns walked through fire like the flames were just a breeze

The little one in shadow climbed the walls on hands and knees

[Chorus]

[Singalong, all vocals]

We came, we saw, we broke the world

Charisma Company!

We healed the wounded, killed the snake

And set the darkness free!

A hundred forty-eight HP

Of keeping us alive

The sun went out but look at us --

All five of us survived!

[Verse 2]

The blind one killed the serpent with a single raging swing

The hood hung from the archway and he stole the golden thing

The imp named Tika scouted what the rivals planned to do

The quiet one said "run through door" — the quiet one was true

We crossed the prism chamber, turned the beams from wall to wall

We solved the man-and-crocodile, we did not trip or fall

A T-Rex slept beneath us while we held our breath and prayed

And when the Fireball came down, not a single one of us swayed

[Chorus]

[Bigger, add harmonies]

We came, we saw, we broke the world

Charisma Company!

We healed the wounded, killed the snake

And set the darkness free!

A hundred forty-eight HP

Of keeping us alive

The sun went out but look at us --

All five of us survived!

[Bridge]

[Stripped — acoustic guitar only, tempo slows]

[Spoken, different voices]

"Why use many word when few word do trick?"

"I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!"

"If the men find out we can shapeshift..."

"I'm just here for the loot, baby."

"Maybe my dad will finally notice me."

[Harmonized, building slowly]

One of us took something that we shouldn't speak about

One of us said nothing and the sun went dark without

A warning or a whisper or a chance to make it right

Charisma Company brought home the never-ending night

[Final Chorus]

[Full power, all instruments, all vocals]

We came, we saw, we broke the world

Charisma Company!

We healed the wounded, killed the snake

And none of us walked free!

A hundred forty-eight HP

Won't fix what we undone

Charisma Company survived --

But we put out the sun

[Outro]

[Acoustic guitar fading, single voice]

[Whispered]

The sun went out...

Charisma Company...

All five survived...

(Silence)

[Spoken, barely audible]

If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College... then we're okay.

[End]

```

Song Structure Breakdown

Section Duration Est. What It Represents Musical Energy
[Intro] 10s The call to adventure — boots stomping, guitar riff, crowd gathering Medium-High
[Verse 1] 35s Who they are — each character introduced through imagery, the party forming High
[Chorus] 25s The victory anthem — what they accomplished, the cost they do not yet understand High
[Verse 2] 35s What they did — the blind kill, the heist, the puzzles, the T-Rex High
[Chorus] 25s Same anthem, bigger and more confident Very High
[Bridge] 40s The shadow — character quotes descending into the revelation that someone stole the sun Low -> Medium
[Final Chorus] 25s The anthem transformed — same melody, but "set the darkness free" becomes "none of us walked free" Maximum
[Outro] 20s The haunting aftermath — whispered reminder that the sun is gone, Aramil's quote as a gut-punch Minimal

Generation Notes

  • Suno Version: Latest available model
  • Instrumental Toggle: Off (vocal track)
  • Duration Target: 210-240 seconds
  • Vocal Style Priority: Strong group-singable chorus with multiple voices; the bridge needs distinct spoken delivery for each quote; the outro needs a single quiet voice
  • Retry Strategy: If the bridge spoken parts do not generate well, convert them to sung lines with [Whispered] tags. If the folk-punk energy is too clean, add "raw" and "lo-fi" to Style Prompt. If the final chorus does not feel different enough from the earlier choruses, try an Extend pass with a modified Style Prompt adding "darker" and "minor key."
  • Variation Ideas:
  • Acoustic Campfire Version: "Intimate acoustic folk, slow tempo, solo acoustic guitar and hand percussion, single warm male vocal, campfire recording quality, wistful and bittersweet"
  • Battle Anthem Version: "Aggressive Celtic punk, fast tempo, electric guitar and bagpipes with war drums, shouted group vocals, stadium punk production, furious and unstoppable"
  • Credits Roll Version: "Cinematic orchestral folk, slow mid-tempo, full orchestra with acoustic guitar foundation, mixed choral vocals, film score production, epic and melancholic"

Album Art Image Generation Prompt

```

Square album cover art, 1:1 aspect ratio. Five adventurer silhouettes standing in a line on a jungle ridge, viewed from behind, looking out at a sky where the sun is visibly going dark — half the sky is golden sunset, the other half is void black with no stars. Left to right: a massive scaled figure with a shield (Dragonborn), a huge muscular figure with a great axe and a blindfold around their neck (Goliath), a horned figure with jewels on the horns and a tiny imp on their shoulder (Tiefling), a very short hooded figure with a cape billowing (Halfling), and a gaunt figure in a fedora holding something glowing in a closed fist (Half-Elf). A golden snake idol sits at their feet, one eye socket empty. Style: vintage adventure movie poster with distressed texture and muted earth tones transitioning to deep indigo and black on the dark side. Title text "THE SUN WENT OUT" in bold weathered serif font across the top. "CHARISMA COMPANY" in smaller tracked-out sans-serif below. The overall feeling should be triumphant and ominous simultaneously — a victory portrait where something is clearly wrong with the sky.

```

Album Art Negative Prompt

```

Cartoon style, anime, chibi, bright neon colors, modern city, sci-fi elements, photorealistic faces, text errors, misspelled words, extra limbs, deformed hands, blurry, low resolution, watermark, signature, white background, generic fantasy clipart

```

Context & Character References

Moor (Kyle 1) - Life Domain Cleric:

  • Dragonborn, white robes, stoic healer
  • "Why use many word when few word do trick?"
  • 148 HP healed across the session — sole healer
  • Destroyed 7 skeletons with Turn Undead
  • Enabled Bob's heist with a Light-enchanted rock

Aramil (Kyle 2) - Warlock (Academic/Archaeologist):

  • Half-Elf, stained fedora, "adjunct professor emeritus"
  • Stole the Eye of the Serpent (sleight of hand 18) — no one knows
  • Sun went out as a consequence of the theft
  • "Maybe my dad finally noticed me"
  • "If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay"

Opal (Shannon) - Barbarian:

  • Goliath, massive, "hewed and jacked and kind of dumb"
  • Went blind from mirror beam (Nat 1, 14 radiant damage to eyes)
  • Killed the Giant Constrictor Snake while blind (critical hit, 30 damage)
  • "I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!"
  • Sister Pearl lives in Barovia

Lavender Villani (Kelsey) - Genie Warlock (Pact of the Chain):

  • Tiefling, jeweled horns, imp familiar Tika (NJ accent)
  • Session MVP (8.4 weighted score)
  • Fire immunity via Zephyr Reliquary — walked through beams unharmed
  • Dispersed poison gas with Gust of Wind
  • "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church"
  • Gave Opal a bandana and held her hand while blind

Bob (Josh) - Thief Rogue:

  • Halfling, deep hood, shifting facial features, "just Bob"
  • Climbed over a sleeping T-Rex, stole the Golden Snake Idol
  • Survived a Fireball mid-flight (Cloak of the Bat)
  • Found Belt of Stone Giant Strength for Opal
  • "I'm just here for the loot, baby"
  • "And how many squirtons in a gusher?"

The Mission:

  • Hired by Fizzleblast (eccentric gnome wizard) to retrieve artifacts from a Chultan jungle temple
  • Fought Gorallons, Giant Constrictor Snake, baboons, rival adventurers
  • Solved pressure plates, mirror puzzles, crystalline prism room
  • Heist: Bob stole the Golden Snake Idol from above a sleeping T-Rex
  • Aramil secretly stole the Eye of the Serpent — the sun went out
  • 100% party survival (first group to achieve this)

End of Party Theme Song

Fun Zone

Drinking Game

Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip: The Drinking Game

One-Shot Session — Retroactive Edition

"How drunk WOULD you have been?"

Disclaimer: This is a retroactive analysis for entertainment purposes. Charisma Company does not endorse actually drinking this much during D&D. You would have been unconscious before the snake fight, and honestly, that might have been a mercy.

UNIVERSAL RULES

(Apply to ALL players — anyone at the table takes the specified drink)

# Rule Tier Times Triggered Total Drinks
U1 Someone says "damage" or damage is dealt/taken SIP ~82 82 sips
U2 A natural 20 is rolled or explicitly celebrated as a crit DRINK 4 4 drinks
U3 A natural 1 is rolled or mourned as a crit fail DRINK 5 5 drinks
U4 The DM says "you see" to describe something SIP ~32 32 sips
U5 The DM says "Are you sure?" or "You can certainly try" DRINK 4 4 drinks
U6 Someone mentions inventory slots or carrying capacity SIP ~23 23 sips
U7 The word "mirror" is spoken by anyone at the table SIP ~41 41 sips
U8 Someone heals or gets healed SIP ~14 14 sips
U9 Someone tries to loot a body or search for treasure SIP ~22 22 sips
U10 An NPC or PC name is mispronounced (Aramel, Emeril, Akinquil, Allendill, Tourmaline, Advil, etc.) DRINK 14 14 drinks
U11 Someone says "10 minutes" (Brandon's favorite time increment) SIP ~37 37 sips
U12 The party debates what to do instead of just doing it DRINK ~12 12 drinks
U13 A dinosaur or prehistoric creature is mentioned (T-Rex, pterodactyl, etc.) SIP ~47 47 sips
U14 Someone swears (fuck, shit, damn, hell, etc.) SIP ~67 67 sips

Universal Rule Subtotal: 365 sips + 39 drinks across the session

PLAYER-SPECIFIC RULES

Brandon (DM / Blue Orbit / Fizzleblast)

# Rule Tier Times Triggered Evidence/Best Example
B1 Brandon says "10 minutes" as the time cost for literally everything SIP ~20 "It's going to take about 10 minutes to try and find something useful." / "It'll be about 10 minutes before it rolls." — Sir, this is a dungeon, not a laundromat.
B2 Brandon introduces a new mechanical complication mid-action DRINK ~8 "But before you grab that... the set of scales is actually sitting on a pedestal. It looks like it has a pressure plate on it." — Nothing is ever simple.
B3 Brandon voices Fizzleblast with dramatic flair SIP ~12 "Do you want me to shrivel, Niles? Do you really? Ugh, like a prune in the sun."
B4 Brandon says "go ahead and give me a [check]" SIP ~18 "Go ahead and give me a strength check." / "Go ahead and give me either a history or religion check." — The man loves his checks.
B5 Brandon describes damage as delicious or food-adjacent DRINK 3 "It smells delicious. I mean, it's like cooked steak." / "Plus burnt hair. That's one funky one."
B6 Brandon roasts a player's character name DRINK 5 "Wait, it's not 'Arrow Mill', it's Aramil? Sounds like a budget laundry detergent." / "I wrote down 'Oaf' not Opal." / "Gesundheit!" (at Muravurok)
B7 Brandon drops a pop culture reference (Aladdin, Indiana Jones, Jurassic Park, Home Alone, etc.) SIP ~8 "Touch nothing but the lamp. I mean, wrong franchise." / "You're tacked on there kind of like Macaulay Culkin at the end of Home Alone."

Brandon's Personal Subtotal: 38 sips + 16 drinks from personal rules

Plus universal triggers: 365 sips + 39 drinks from universal rules

TOTAL: 403 sips + 55 drinks

Kyle 1 (playing Moor — Dragonborn Cleric)

# Rule Tier Times Triggered Evidence/Best Example
K1-1 Kyle 1 uses few words or communicates in caveman-speak DRINK 4 "Run through door. Why use many word when small word do trick?" — Kevin from The Office energy, but scalier.
K1-2 Kyle 1 casts Guidance on someone SIP ~12 Cast Guidance more times than some people blink. The man is a walking buff station.
K1-3 Kyle 1 heals someone (any spell or staff use) SIP ~10 Healed Aramil from 0 HP, healed Opal's blindness, cast Prayer of Healing, cast Aid, used Staff of Healing. He IS the healthcare system.
K1-4 Kyle 1 asks a tactical clarifying question about the environment SIP ~8 "Can I access the stone weights?" / "How are these staying?" / "Was this a pathway right here?" — The party strategist nobody asked for but everyone needed.
K1-5 Kyle 1 uses his mace to test something instead of his hands DRINK 3 "I'd like to take out my mace and just push down on one of the tiles in front of me." — Safety first, always.
K1-6 Kyle 1 immediately grabs an item without waiting for the DM to finish DRINK 2 "I grab it." (the Scales of Justice, before Brandon could even explain the pressure plate)
K1-7 Kyle 1 says "Okay" to transition the group forward SIP ~15 "Okay, so the shield is heavy enough, but the mace isn't." / "Okay. I'm going to cast aid on you three. Not you."

Kyle 1's Personal Subtotal: 45 sips + 9 drinks from personal rules

Plus universal triggers: 365 sips + 39 drinks from universal rules

TOTAL: 410 sips + 48 drinks

Kyle 2 (playing Aramil — Half-Elf Warlock)

# Rule Tier Times Triggered Evidence/Best Example
K2-1 Kyle 2 mentions his academic credentials or being a professor DRINK ~5 "I am adjunct professor emeritus." / "I recently took a sabbatical from my position at the Royal Community College." / "I'm a professor."
K2-2 Kyle 2 casts Eldritch Blast SIP ~6 "I'm going to Eldritch Blast this time." — The warlock's answer to every question.
K2-3 Kyle 2 tries to talk to an animal using Speak with Animals SIP ~5 "Hey, big guy. You just saw a lover boy get blasted by this, right? Think about your wife and kids."
K2-4 Kyle 2 does something sneaky or morally questionable DRINK 3 "I rolled an 18 on the sleight of hand to take it." — Casually plunging the entire continent into eternal darkness for a golden eye.
K2-5 Kyle 2 says "I'm appreciating art" or references the murals SIP 3 "I'm appreciating art." / "I'm looking at murals." — While everyone else fights for their lives, Aramil is at the gallery.
K2-6 Kyle 2 gets salty about not getting good loot DRINK 4 "God damn it! That's huge. That's what I wanted!" (when Lavender got the Rod of the Pactkeeper) / "I haven't gotten anything."
K2-7 Aramil gets downed or nearly killed FINISH 1 Went to -10 HP from the constrictor snake. "I am at negative 10." — The adjunct professor almost got tenure in the afterlife.

Kyle 2's Personal Subtotal: 14 sips + 12 drinks + 1 finish from personal rules

Plus universal triggers: 365 sips + 39 drinks from universal rules

TOTAL: 379 sips + 51 drinks + 1 finish

Shannon (playing Opal — Goliath Barbarian)

# Rule Tier Times Triggered Evidence/Best Example
S1 Shannon references being blind or not being able to see SIP ~10 "Can anybody else see? Or is it just me that can't see?" / "Because I'm blind, whoever said that." / "Someone's gotta tell me where to go."
S2 Shannon says "I'm Goliath" or references her size/strength SIP ~6 "I am Goliath. I am Opal." / "Have you seen these quads?" / "I'm gigantic."
S3 Shannon rages or talks about raging DRINK 3 "I rage. I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!" — The most iconic contradiction of the session.
S4 Shannon rolls terribly at a critical moment SIP ~7 Nat 1 on the mirror push (went blind), rolled a 5 on intimidation, rolled poorly on multiple attacks. Dice were NOT her friends.
S5 Shannon volunteers to do something physical/dangerous immediately SIP ~6 "I can do that." / "I'll try to." / "I'm happy to be up front." — Zero hesitation, maximum recklessness.
S6 Shannon puts a die in "timeout" DRINK 3 "That one's in timeout." / "She sucks." / "They're all the D6s I have." — A disciplinary system for dice. We love it.
S7 Shannon says something delightfully dumb as Opal DRINK 4 "Do we smell dinosaurs?" / "I don't think I have sight either." / "What if I punched a wall?"

Shannon's Personal Subtotal: 29 sips + 10 drinks from personal rules

Plus universal triggers: 365 sips + 39 drinks from universal rules

TOTAL: 394 sips + 49 drinks

Kelsey (playing Lavender — Tiefling Warlock)

# Rule Tier Times Triggered Evidence/Best Example
L1 Kelsey says "I don't know how to play D&D" or asks a basic rules question SIP ~5 "Listen, I don't know how to play D&D. Should we put this in here?" / "I don't remember what a d8 looks like, I'm being honest."
L2 Kelsey references Tika the imp or sends Tika to do something SIP ~10 "I'm going to send my imp to fly up there." / "Tika's going to explore it." — MVP familiar of the session.
L3 Kelsey uses a spell at exactly the right time DRINK 4 Gust of Wind to disperse the poison gas, Hellish Rebuke killing two baboons, Shield of Faith for the escape. Session MVP moves.
L4 Kelsey drops a Quote of the Session-tier one-liner FINISH 2 "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." / "D-E-D dead."
L5 Kelsey says "later" about dealing with a problem SIP 6 "Later. Later. Later." / "I'll do it later, obviously." / "Let's not think about it too long." — The queen of procrastination.
L6 Kelsey worries aloud about what bad thing is coming next SIP ~4 "It's concerning that we get so many of these good things." / "Classic, playing the video game, you're like, cool, I just got 10 new lives."
L7 Kelsey volunteers Tika for a dangerous mission she would not do herself DRINK 3 Sent Tika to scout the high shelves, sent Tika invisible to spy on the rival team, sent Tika as a raven to peck out the snake's eye. Management material.

Kelsey's Personal Subtotal: 25 sips + 7 drinks + 2 finishes from personal rules

Plus universal triggers: 365 sips + 39 drinks from universal rules

TOTAL: 390 sips + 46 drinks + 2 finishes

Josh (playing Bob — Halfling Rogue)

# Rule Tier Times Triggered Evidence/Best Example
J1 Josh makes a sarcastic one-liner SIP ~8 "I'm just here for the loot, baby." / "Smells like bullshit. I got a 16." / "Who pissed me off the least?"
J2 Josh uses Sleight of Hand or Stealth (his signature skills) SIP ~8 Sleight of Hand to swap the Scales of Justice (rolled 19), Stealth to sneak on the ceiling (rolled 27), Stealth to grab the idol (dirty 20).
J3 Bob does something absurd with the Spider Slippers or Bat Cloak DRINK 4 Ran up the wall and across the ceiling silently, flew off the archway holding his cape like Batman, walked on walls to bypass pressure plates.
J4 Josh says "Bob" as a complete sentence or identity statement SIP ~5 "I'm, uh... Bob." / "You better run from Bob." / "Bob went crazy." — The entire personality is just the name.
J5 Josh uses Halfling Luck to reroll a die SIP 3 "That's a one, but I am a halfling." / "I'm going to use Luck to reroll." — Statistically unfair and he knows it.
J6 Josh creates an elaborate tactical plan involving climbing or sneaking DRINK 3 The full T-Rex heist plan (sneak up wall, grab idol, Batman cape off the archway), the ceiling baboon distraction gambit, the wall-walking pressure plate bypass.
J7 Josh rolls damage dice and gets all 1s and 2s SIP 3 "God, fucking damage. Another eight damage. Fuck my life, dude. Three D6s in a row, a one, a one, and a two." — The stealth god with the damage output of a wet noodle.

Josh's Personal Subtotal: 27 sips + 7 drinks from personal rules

Plus universal triggers: 365 sips + 39 drinks from universal rules

TOTAL: 392 sips + 46 drinks

BONUS ROUND: EPIC MOMENTS

(Special one-time triggers for the session's most memorable moments)

# Moment Rule Who Drinks
B1 "I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!" — Opal rages, rolls a nat 1, falls into the thermal beam, takes 14 damage, and goes completely blind for an hour. The most poetic self-destruction of the session. FINISH your drink Everyone
B2 "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." — Kelsey delivers the Quote of the Session out of absolutely nowhere during a discussion about turning Tika into a snake. The table erupts. WATERFALL — Kelsey starts, everyone follows clockwise Everyone
B3 Aramil steals the golden eye and plunges Faerun into eternal darkness. Kyle 2 rolls an 18 Sleight of Hand as the party is getting in the boat and casually dooms the entire continent for 5,000 gold and a faculty office decoration. "I will put it in my office in the community college." The person responsible (Kyle 2) FINISHES their drink; everyone else: DRINK Kyle 2 + All
B4 Bob's Batman Moment. Josh climbs the wall in total darkness, hangs upside down from the archway, grabs the golden snake idol, and leaps off holding his Cloak of the Bat, gliding to safety like a halfling Bruce Wayne. "I'm a motherfucking knight." DRINK in reverent silence — no one speaks for 10 seconds Everyone
B5 "And how many squirtons in a gusher?" — The moment Bob asked the real economic question that derailed the first five minutes and established the session's comedic tone. Referenced as gushers, gooshers, and squirts throughout. SIP every time squirton-adjacent currency is mentioned (came up 6 times) Everyone
B6 Opal crits the constrictor snake while blind. Shannon rolls the first and possibly only natural 20 of her night to deal 30 damage and slice through the snake that was squeezing Aramil to death. She can't see. She doesn't care. Everyone FINISHES their drink Everyone

SCOREBOARD: THE FINAL TALLY

Intoxication Calculator

Assumptions: 1 SIP = 1 oz of beer (or equivalent). 1 DRINK = 4 oz. 1 FINISH = 12 oz (one full beer/drink). Standard intoxication rate applied over ~4 hours.

Player Sips (Universal) Sips (Personal) Drinks (Universal) Drinks (Personal) Finishes (Bonus) Total Oz Estimated BAC* Status
Brandon (DM) 365 38 39 16 3 659 oz ~4.2% Dead
Kyle 1 (Moor) 365 45 39 9 3 638 oz ~4.1% Dead
Kyle 2 (Aramil) 365 14 39 12 4 631 oz ~4.0% Dead
Shannon (Opal) 365 29 39 10 3 586 oz ~3.7% Dead
Kelsey (Lavender) 365 25 39 7 5 634 oz ~4.0% Dead
Josh (Bob) 365 27 39 7 3 612 oz ~3.9% Dead

Note: Everyone is dead. Every single person at this table is making death saves IRL. The universal rules alone would have killed you all by the end of hour two. This session was a verbal firehouse of mirrors, damage, swearing, looting, and debate. You are all cautionary tales.

Status Labels:

  • Sober (0-12 oz): "Designated driver material"
  • Buzzed (12-24 oz): "Feeling good, making questionable tactical decisions"
  • Tipsy (24-36 oz): "Confident enough to talk to any NPC"
  • Drunk (36-48 oz): "All plans now involve fire"
  • Wasted (48-72 oz): "Trying to seduce the dragon"
  • Unconscious (72+ oz): "Making death saves IRL"
  • Dead (100+ oz): "You are now a cautionary tale in the next campaign"

\BAC estimates are humorous approximations, not medical advice. Please drink responsibly, unlike the way you play D&D.*

Session MVP (Most Intoxicated):

Brandon (DM) — with 659 total oz, reaching Dead status.

Brandon did this to himself. Between saying "you see" 32 times, "10 minutes" 20+ times, "go ahead and give me a [check]" every other sentence, voicing Fizzleblast like a caffeinated drama professor, and roasting every single character name at the table, the DM was his own worst enemy. The man described cooked gorilla as "smelling delicious" and compared a T-Rex sleeping to a heat lamp. He would have been unconscious before anyone entered the temple.

Session Designated Driver (Least Intoxicated):

Shannon (Opal) — with 586 total oz.

This is misleading. Shannon is still catastrophically dead by any medical standard. She simply triggered fewer personal rules because she spent a full hour of the session blind and unable to participate in anything except holding Lavender's hand and "crying as a free action." The blindness that made her the worst player statistically is the only thing that kept her from being the drunkest. Irony is a flat circle.

DRINKING GAME SUPERLATIVES

Award Winner Stat
Most Repeated Catchphrase Brandon — "Go ahead and give me a [check]" Said ~18 times. The DM's version of a verbal tic.
Most Dangerous Habit Kyle 1 — Casting Guidance on everything that moves Triggered ~12 SIP-tier rules. If Guidance were a drinking rule, Moor would have killed the table.
Designated Party Tank (most universal triggers caused) Brandon (DM) — describing things, saying "you see," and creating situations that caused swearing Caused the majority of the 365 universal sip triggers for the whole table
Most Likely to Survive (fewest personal triggers) Shannon (Opal) Only 29 personal sips + 10 drinks — but only because she was blind for 25% of the session
The Lightweight (reached highest status with fewest triggers) Josh (Bob) Despite being a stealth god, Josh's sneak attack damage dice consistently rolled 1s and 2s. The universe gave him crits on stealth and took it back on damage.
The DM's Burden (most "Are you sure?" triggers) Kyle 2 (Aramil) Between trying to negotiate with hostile gorillas, stealing the golden eye, and casually ending the sun, Brandon questioned Kyle 2's choices more than anyone else at the table.
Best Trigger Moment Shannon — "I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!" Raged, nat 1'd, fell into a thermal death beam, took 14 damage to her eyes, went blind for an hour. In two sentences, she created the most quotable moment AND the biggest disaster of the session.
The Enabler Kelsey — Using Tika as a disposable employee Sent her imp familiar into danger 10+ times while she stayed safely behind. Tika died, was resurrected, and sent right back to work. Girlboss energy.

FORWARD-LOOKING: NEXT SESSION'S RULES

Based on patterns identified in this session, here are suggested ADDITIONAL rules for the Tomb of Annihilation campaign:

New Rule Based On Predicted Frequency
Every time someone says "eternal darkness" or references the sun being gone "Aramil stole the golden eye and plunged Faerun into eternal darkness" — this will be referenced CONSTANTLY ~15-20 times per session. Kyle 2 drinks double.
Every time Kyle 2 tries to justify the eye theft as "for academic purposes" "I will put it in my office in the community college" — he will never stop defending this choice ~5-8 times per session
Every time the DM describes something taking "10 minutes" Brandon physically cannot describe a task without this time increment. It is hardwired into his DMing brain. ~20+ times. It will never stop.
Every time Shannon puts a die in timeout She has developed a full disciplinary system for her dice. This will escalate. Expect a die to get permanently banned. ~4-6 times per session
Every time someone mispronounces Aramil's name It happened 14 times in ONE session. With a full campaign, "Aramel," "Emeril," and "Advil" will achieve legendary status. ~10+ times per session. Bob will lead this charge.
Every time Kelsey sends Tika into danger instead of going herself "Loot around, bud." — Tika is an unpaid intern and Kelsey is middle management. This dynamic will only deepen. ~6-10 times per session

Generated retroactively from the Charisma Company's Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip one-shot session transcript. All counts are derived from actual transcript occurrences and marked with ~ where exact counting was impractical due to transcript formatting. No gorillas, snakes, or halfling rogues were harmed in the making of this drinking game. One imp was temporarily killed. One continent was plunged into eternal darkness. Standard Tuesday.

Roll Credits

Credits

Charisma Company: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip — End Credits

"The Sunless Shore"

MUSIC DIRECTION

Credits Song: "Original composition — see Suno prompt below"

Suno Prompt (if generating original credits music):

```

Cinematic orchestral fantasy credits theme, 5 minutes. Starts with a single solo cello playing a slow, haunting melody over ambient jungle sounds and distant waves. At 0:45, warm strings swell in as a wooden flute enters with an adventurous motif. Builds steadily through layered instrumentation: pizzicato violins, French horn, gentle percussion. Each new instrument enters every 25 seconds, adding warmth and momentum. At 3:00, full orchestra peaks with brass fanfare and triumphant timpani rolls — celebratory, earned, joyful. At 3:45, the arrangement softens to solo piano and cello duet, bittersweet and reflective. At 4:15, near silence — just wind and a single sustained note. At 4:30, a low, dissonant drone on bass strings with a single ominous bell toll. Tempo: 72 BPM building to 90 BPM at peak. No vocals. Orchestral fantasy film score style. Warm, adventurous, with a final turn to foreboding darkness. Production style: Hans Zimmer meets Bear McCreary.

```

Music Emotional Arc:

  • 0:00-0:45 — Quiet, atmospheric, the jungle at dusk, the safety bubble humming, residual adrenaline from the escape
  • 0:45-3:00 — Building, warm, celebratory — each character's section adds a new instrumental layer
  • 3:00-3:45 — Full ensemble, emotional peak — the whole party together in Fizzleblast's bubble
  • 3:45-4:30 — Gentle decrescendo, bittersweet, the canoes drifting away at dawn
  • 4:30-5:00+ — Near silence, then ominous darkness for post-credits

COLD OPEN (0:00 — 0:45)

The Last Lingering Moment

[The final scene of the session: the party has just tumbled into Fizzleblast's safety bubble. The undead swarm the jungle outside. Fizzleblast is asleep with a teddy bear. Niles is shaking on the floor. The adventurers are alive. They should not be. And no one has noticed that the sun is not coming back.]

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

0:00 | Wide shot from outside the Fizzle Field bubble looking in. The translucent shimmer of the barrier catches faint blue-green light. Inside, five exhausted adventurers collapse onto the sand. Outside, dark shapes claw and scrape at the perimeter. The jungle is screaming. | None | Ambient: jungle shrieks, undead groaning, the low hum of the Fizzle Field. No music yet.

0:10 | The camera slowly pushes through the barrier wall. Sound muffles as we cross the threshold into the bubble's silence. Moor leans on his staff, head bowed. Opal sits heavily, blood on her bandaged eyes. Bob clutches the bag with the golden idol, hood low. Lavender exhales, looking up at the sky. Aramil turns away from the group, hand in his pocket, fingers curled around something no one else can see. | None | The barrier hum fades to quiet. First notes of the credits song — a solo cello, low and contemplative.

0:25 | Close-up of Aramil's hand in his pocket, the faintest golden glow leaking between his fingers — the Eye of the Serpent. The camera pulls back slowly. Through the top of the bubble, the sky is wrong. Where stars should be, there is nothing. The last sliver of light on the horizon gutters and dies. Total darkness beyond the barrier. | None | A second instrument joins — a wooden flute, carrying a fragile, adventurous melody.

0:40 | SLOW FADE TO BLACK. The bubble's glow is the last thing to vanish, a single point of light in an ocean of dark. | CHARISMA COMPANY fades in, centered, in elegant gold serif. Below: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip: "The Sunless Shore" | Music continues building into the main credits theme. The cello and flute intertwine.

CHARACTER CREDITS (0:45 — 3:00)

Per Character (~25-35 seconds each)

[Each character gets a dedicated segment. The visual style is consistent: painterly, cinematic illustrations with dramatic lighting against deep jungle-dark backgrounds. Text in warm gold. Each character's instrument enters the arrangement and stays, building the ensemble.]

Moor — 0:45 — 1:10

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

0:45 | Portrait of Moor in profile, white robes stained with soot and blood, the Staff of Healing gripped in one clawed hand. He stands before a heliographic thermal mirror, its focused beam cutting a white line across the frame. Behind him, the smoking remains of a Gorallon dissolve in the light. His draconic face is calm, resolute, lit from the front by the concentrated sunlight. | Kyle (small, upper right, warm gold) | Music: a deep brass horn enters — steady, grounding, the foundation of the arrangement.

0:50 | The image shifts subtly. Moor is kneeling beside a fallen Aramil, one hand on the warlock's chest, golden light pouring from his palm. Cure Wounds. The snake's severed coils lie around them. His expression is exhausted but unwavering. | as Moor (larger, centered, gold serif) | Horn melody continues, warm and dependable.

0:55 | Same visual, held. | "Run through door. Why use many word when small word do trick?" (italics, lower third, white) | Music continues.

1:02 | Flash of a highlight moment: Moor turning the thermal mirror with both hands, angling the death beam directly into the charging Gorallon. The creature's silhouette dissolves in blinding white light. Moor's scales reflect the glow. | Solo healer — 148 HP restored across the session | Music builds slightly, the horn joined by low strings.

1:08 | Slow dissolve. The golden healing light from Moor's hands fades into the warm amber of the next portrait. | Text fades. | Horn sustains as the next instrument enters.

Opal — 1:10 — 1:35

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

1:10 | Portrait of Opal mid-swing, greataxe raised high above her head, muscles taut, face wrapped in a bloodied bandana over her burned-out eyes. She cannot see the Giant Constrictor Snake coiled before her. She does not need to. The axe is descending. Her expression is pure, furious joy. | Shannon (small, upper right) | Music: heavy war drums enter — primal, powerful, a heartbeat rhythm.

1:15 | The image shifts. Opal stands in the beam room, having just fallen into the thermal array. Light sears across her face. Her eyes are wide, the last thing she will see for an hour. One hand grips the mirror she was trying to turn. The other is balled into a fist. | as Opal (larger, centered) | Drums continue, joined by a low chant-like string section.

1:20 | Same composition, held. | "I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!" (italics, lower third) | Music continues.

1:27 | Flash: The critical hit. Opal's Bloodaxe cleaves through layered coils of the Giant Constrictor Snake while Aramil hangs limp in its grip. She cannot see her target. She swings anyway. 30 damage. The snake falls in pieces. | Killed the Giant Snake while blind — Critical Hit, 30 damage | War drums peak, a single thunderous hit.

1:33 | Slow dissolve. The red of blood and firelight softens into the purple-tinged darkness of the next portrait. | Text fades. | Drums settle into a quieter pulse beneath the growing arrangement.

Lavender — 1:35 — 2:00

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

1:35 | Portrait of Lavender in three-quarter view, tiefling horns adorned with jewels, Fizzleblast's cashmere sun hat dangling from one horn tip. She holds the Zephyr Reliquary — a small jar trailing blue mist — up to the light. Behind her, the crystalline prism room refracts rainbows across the stone walls. Her smile is knowing, delighted. Tika the imp perches on her shoulder in raven form. | Kelsey (small, upper right) | Music: a lilting fiddle enters — playful, clever, dancing around the melody.

1:40 | The image shifts. Lavender stands at the edge of the prism room, Eldritch Blast crackling between her fingers, firing across the chamber. The Gorallon stumbles backward into the thermal beam. Tika, as a raven, dives toward the Giant Snake's remaining eye. She is everywhere at once — familiar, patron, blast, strategy. | as Lavender Villani (larger, centered) | Fiddle melody dances, weaving through the arrangement.

1:45 | Same visual, held. | "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." (italics, lower third) | Music continues.

1:52 | Flash: Lavender leaping across the pressure plate during the escape, the toxic gas billowing behind her, acrobatics check: 17. She clears the gap, lands clean, turns to beckon the others forward. The clock is ticking. She does not hesitate. | Session MVP — 8.4 weighted score. Found the Zephyr Reliquary. | Fiddle soars, then settles.

1:58 | Slow dissolve. The prismatic light fades into warm amber torchlight. | Text fades. | Fiddle integrates into the ensemble as the next instrument enters.

Bob — 2:00 — 2:25

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

2:00 | Portrait of Bob hanging upside-down from the stone archway above a sleeping T-Rex, legs hooked over the edge, both hands reaching for the Golden Snake Idol. His deep hood has fallen back just enough to reveal the glint of eyes and the edge of a mustache. Below him, the T-Rex's massive jaw exhales steam. The idol glows faintly gold in his grip. | Josh (small, upper right) | Music: a solo plucked lute enters — quick, nimble, mischievous.

2:05 | The image shifts. Bob leaps from the archway, both hands gripping his Cloak of the Bat, spreading it wide. He is a dark silhouette against the last sliver of fading light, gliding over the T-Rex's head, the idol secure in his pack. A fireball detonates behind him, orange light catching the edges of his cloak. | as Bob (larger, centered) | Lute quickens, joined by a rhythmic tambourine pulse.

2:10 | Same visual, held. | "I'm just here for the loot, baby." (italics, lower third) | Music continues.

2:17 | Flash: Bob's stealth check — dirty 20. The T-Rex stirs but does not wake. His hands close around the idol. He pulls. It comes free. He drops into the dark. Mission accomplished. | Stole the Golden Snake Idol from above a sleeping T-Rex | Lute hits a triumphant ascending run.

2:23 | Slow dissolve. The dark silhouette of the cloak fades into the next portrait's shadows. | Text fades. | Lute melody settles into the growing ensemble.

Aramil — 2:25 — 2:55

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

2:25 | Portrait of Aramil in a pool of sickly golden light, his back to the camera, looking over his shoulder. He is gaunt, hollow-eyed, the stained fedora casting shadow across his face. In one hand, barely visible, the Eye of the Serpent glows between his fingers — warm amber bleeding into something wrong, something hungry. His purple crystal necklace catches the light. Behind him, the headless snake idol looms in darkness, one empty socket staring. | Kyle (small, upper right) | Music: a solo viola enters — minor key, searching, restless, the only dissonant thread in the arrangement.

2:30 | The image shifts. Aramil is wrapped in the coils of the Giant Constrictor Snake, unconscious, blood at his mouth. Negative ten hit points. Moor's golden healing light reaches toward him from the edge of the frame. Opal's axe is mid-swing above. He is utterly helpless, utterly dependent on the people he will later betray. | as Aramil (larger, centered) | Viola deepens, mournful, layered beneath the warmer instruments.

2:35 | Same visual, held. | "I am adjunct professor emeritus." (italics, lower third) | Music continues.

2:42 | Flash: Aramil's hand, Gloves of Thievery gleaming, prying the golden eye from the snake idol as the party loads into the canoes. Sleight of Hand: 18. No one sees. Behind him, through the trees, the horizon darkens. The sun falters. He pockets the gem. He does not look back. | Secretly stole the Eye of the Serpent — plunged Faerun into eternal darkness | Viola plays a descending phrase. The other instruments briefly falter, then recover.

2:50 | The portrait holds longer than the others. Aramil's face, half-lit, half-dark. The eye glows in his pocket. He almost looks sorry. Almost. | Text fades. | All instruments play together now, building toward the NPC montage. The viola's dissonance resolves — for now.

NPC MONTAGE (3:00 — 3:20)

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

3:00 | Quick montage of NPC faces and scenes begins. Each flash is 2-3 seconds, painterly, warm-toned. | NPCs of Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip (header, fading in, gold text) | Music: full ensemble, warm, all instruments together in a major key.

3:03 | Fizzleblast the gnome, standing at the bow of the canoe in his oversized cashmere robe and enormous spectacles, one finger raised, mid-lecture. Niles struggles beneath a massive sack behind him. The Chultan shore stretches out. Fizzleblast's expression: absolute confidence paired with absolute cowardice. | Fizzleblast — Patron & Questgiver | |

3:06 | Niles, red-haired and scrawny, mid-fall, the Bag of Holding bursting open above him as magic items scatter through the air like fireworks. His face: pure terror. Behind him, the party dives for loot. | Niles — Intern | |

3:09 | Tika the imp, in raven form, diving toward the Giant Snake's eye, talons extended, beak open. A tiny, vicious silhouette against the prism room's refracted light. One eye already blinded. Going for the second. | Tika — Familiar (Imp) | |

3:12 | Ransom's Rex — the T-Rex, asleep beneath the archway, steam rising from its nostrils, heat-lamp mirrors glowing above. It looks almost peaceful. Above its head, the Golden Snake Idol hangs, glinting. It does not know what is about to happen. | Ransom's Rex — The T-Rex | |

3:15 | Fade to black. A single beat of silence. | "Dungeon Master: Blue Orbit (Brandon)" (centered, prominent, gold serif, larger than any other credit) | Music: a brief, respectful pause — a single held chord, honoring the architect of the chaos.

SESSION STATS OVERLAY (3:20 — 3:45)

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

3:20 | Dark background with faint imagery of the Temple of the Man and Crocodile visible underneath — stone walls, mirror beams, the crocodile mural. Stats appear one at a time, typed in warm gold text. | Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip — By the Numbers | Music: lighter, playful section — the lute and fiddle take the lead, bouncy and warm.

3:23 | | Encounters: 4 (Gorallons, Giant Constrictor Snake, Toxic Gas Trap, Rival Party Ambush) | |

3:25 | | Nat 20s: 0 / Nat 1s: 1 (Opal, mirror push — went blind) | |

3:27 | | Most Damage (Single Hit): Opal — 30 damage, blind critical hit on the Giant Snake | |

3:29 | | Total Party Damage Taken: 171 HP | |

3:31 | | Total Healing Done: 148 HP (all by Moor, obviously) | |

3:33 | | Characters Downed: 1 (Aramil — crushed by snake, -10 HP) | |

3:35 | | Leeroy Jenkins Moments: 5 | |

3:37 | | Best Line: "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." — Lavender | |

3:39 | | Session Duration: ~4 hours | Escape Timer Remaining: 4 minutes, 20 seconds | |

3:41 | | Worlds Plunged Into Eternal Darkness: 1 | |

3:43 | Stats fade, background image glows brighter — the golden idol pulses once. | | Music transitions to final emotional section, strings swelling.

GROUP ILLUSTRATION FINALE (3:45 — 4:15)

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT OVERLAY | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

3:45 | SLOW REVEAL of the group illustration. The five members of Charisma Company stand together inside the Fizzle Field bubble on the Chultan shore. The barrier shimmers around them in translucent blue-green. Outside, the jungle is alive with undead shadows and glowing predator eyes. The sky above is utterly black — no moon, no stars, no sun. The only light comes from the bubble and Moor's enchanted rock, casting the group in warm amber. Left to right: Opal stands tallest, Bloodaxe resting on one shoulder, bandana still over her eyes, grinning. Moor leans on the Staff of Healing beside her, white robes glowing faintly with residual divine light, his draconic features tired but proud. Lavender sits on a crate of loot in the center, the Zephyr Reliquary in her lap, jeweled horns catching the light, Fizzleblast's cashmere hat still dangling from one horn tip. Bob crouches at the front, hood low, the Golden Snake Idol barely visible poking out of his pack, one eye gleaming beneath the cowl. Aramil stands slightly apart on the right, arms crossed, fedora tipped low, the faintest amber glow leaking from his coat pocket. Behind them all, Fizzleblast is visible through the bubble wall, asleep in a camp chair with a teddy bear, and Niles lies curled on the ground, trembling. | None — let the image breathe. | Music: emotional peak — all instruments, full arrangement. Horn, drums, fiddle, lute, viola, strings. The moment that makes you feel things.

3:55 | Camera slowly pans across the illustration. Opal's blind grin. Moor's steadfast grip on the staff. Lavender's knowing smile. Bob's hidden face and half-visible loot. Aramil's separated stance, the golden glow in his pocket. The dead jungle pressing against the barrier. The black, starless sky. | None | Music sustains, then begins gentle decrescendo.

4:05 | Image holds. | CHARISMA COMPANY (centered above, gold serif) | Music fading to piano and cello.

4:10 | Image holds. | "The sun will not rise again in Faerun." (italicized below title, white text) | Music: final notes, lingering — the cello holds one long, low note.

4:15 | FADE TO BLACK. | | Silence — then the faintest sound of waves on the Chultan shore.

Group Illustration Image Prompt:

```

Epic fantasy group portrait, five adventurers standing together inside a translucent magical barrier bubble on a dark tropical shore at night. The sky is completely black with no stars or moon — supernatural darkness. Outside the bubble, shadowy undead figures and glowing predator eyes press against the barrier. Inside, warm amber light from a glowing enchanted stone illuminates the group. Left to right: a massive female Goliath barbarian with a bloodied bandana over her eyes, grinning, resting a greataxe on her shoulder; a white-robed Dragonborn cleric leaning on a wooden staff with golden divine light emanating from it; a female Tiefling warlock with jeweled horns and a floppy cashmere hat hanging from one horn, sitting on a crate, holding a small misty jar; a small hooded Halfling rogue crouching in front, a golden snake idol barely visible in his pack, only eyes visible under a deep hood; a gaunt Half-Elf warlock in a stained fedora standing slightly apart, arms crossed, faint golden light leaking from his coat pocket. Behind the group, visible through the bubble wall, a tiny gnome sleeps in a camp chair with a teddy bear, and a red-haired teenager lies curled on the ground trembling. Dark jungle, bioluminescent barrier, cinematic lighting, painterly fantasy illustration, 16:9 widescreen aspect ratio, no text, high detail, emotionally resonant, warm interior light contrasting with cold exterior darkness.

```

POST-CREDITS SCENE (4:30 — 5:00+)

[After 15 seconds of black silence, a brief scene teasing the consequences of Aramil's theft.]

TIMECODE | VISUAL | TEXT/DIALOGUE | AUDIO

—|—|—|—

4:30 | BLACK SCREEN. Then — a sound. A low, grinding stone-on-stone scrape, like an idol being moved on a pedestal. | None | Stone grinding in darkness. Nothing else.

4:35 | A new image fades in. The Temple of the Man and Crocodile, but wrong. The thermal mirrors are shattered. The prism room is dark. The archway where the idol hung is broken and empty. Ransom's Rex is gone. In the center of the floor, where the snake idol's pedestal stood, a crack has opened in the stone. Something glows deep beneath — not gold, not amber. Green. Sickly, pulsing green. | None | Low, ominous drone on bass strings. A single dissonant bell toll.

4:45 | Close-up on the crack. The green light intensifies. From below, a voice — not human, not animal, something ancient and vast — whispers upward through the stone. The crack widens. One skeletal hand, wrapped in crumbling gold jewelry, reaches up from the fissure and grips the edge. | "The eye has been taken. The covenant is broken. Let the dark come home." | A sharp string sting — then abrupt silence.

4:55 | SMASH CUT TO BLACK. | Charisma Company will return. | SILENCE.

PRODUCTION CREDITS

```

CHARISMA COMPANY

Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip: "The Sunless Shore"

Recorded February 2026

CAST

Kyle as Moor — Dragonborn Cleric (Life Domain), Level 3

Shannon as Opal — Goliath Barbarian, Level 3

Kelsey as Lavender Villani — Tiefling Warlock (Genie Patron), Level 3

Josh as Bob — Halfling Rogue (Thief), Level 3

Kyle as Aramil — Half-Elf Warlock (Pact of the Chain), Level 3

DUNGEON MASTER

Blue Orbit (Brandon)

WORLD DESIGN

Blue Orbit (Brandon)

ORIGINAL STORY

The players and DM of Charisma Company

TEMPLE ENGINEERING CONSULTANT

Whoever designed those heliographic thermal arrays

(They were very effective at melting gorillas)

CREDITS SEQUENCE DESIGNED BY

Claude (Anthropic)

SPECIAL THANKS

Niles, for dropping the Bag of Holding

(You beautiful disaster)

The Pterodactyls, for not screeching at the wrong moment

Fizzleblast's cashmere robe, for surviving the humidity

And to the T-Rex, for being a very heavy sleeper

"And how many squirtons in a gusher?"

-- Bob

Charisma Company is a production of Blue Orbit's table.

All characters and events are based on actual gameplay.

No gorillas were harmed in the making of this session.

(That is a lie. One was melted by a thermal mirror.

The other ran headfirst into one while blind.

We regret nothing.)

The sun, however, was harmed.

Significantly.

```

Previously On...

Previously On

Previously On... Charisma Company: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip

One-Shot Recap — Read aloud at the start of the next session

Performance Notes

This script covers a wild, high-energy one-shot with a comedic core and a dark twist ending. Start warm and slightly absurd — the opening should feel like a nature documentary narrated by someone who has seen too much. Let the comedy breathe in the early beats, but tighten your delivery as the script accelerates. When you reach the final lines about darkness, slow down dramatically and lower your voice. Look directly at Kyle 2 (Aramil's player) on the last line. Hold the silence for a full five seconds before transitioning.

The Script

[MUSIC CUE: Bright, percussive jungle drums layered with exotic bird calls — adventure-serial energy, almost playful.]

The air is heavy. Not humid — heavy. Like the jungle itself is leaning on your chest, daring you to breathe. The forbidden shores of Chult stretch before five strangers who answered a flyer posted by a gnome in an oversized cashmere robe — and not one of them has asked why the pay is so good.

[PAUSE]

A neurotic wizard called Fizzleblast jams a rod into the sand and retreats behind a shimmering barrier he calls the Fizzle Field — patent pending. He points toward a temple bristling with mirrors that focus sunlight into beams hot enough to melt flesh. Get in. Get the golden snake artifact. Get back before dark. The canoes are rented, and he will not lose his deposit.

His intern Niles trips. A sack of magical items explodes across the beach. A potion of instant attunement shatters. And five mercenaries find themselves bound to whatever touches them first — armor, bracers, gloves, a pearl — the expedition's gear chosen not by skill, but by chaos.

[MUSIC CUE: The drums deepen. Strings creep in underneath — tension building beneath the adventure.]

Inside the temple, the antechamber gleams with mountains of gold and the bones of those who grabbed too greedily. Pressure plates. Poison gas. A massive stone door held by rotting ropes. A mural on the wall shows a man and a crocodile walking arm in arm, swords crossed. It is a riddle. It is a warning. A dragonborn cleric and a tiefling warlock link arms, step in lockstep, and the temple lets them pass.

[PAUSE]

Then the crystalline chamber. Mirrors splitting light into blinding beams. A giant constrictor snake, forty feet of coiled muscle and venom, guarding a prism at the heart of the room. A goliath with a great axe swings blind — literally blind, her eyes seared by a misdirected beam — and still she buries the blade. The snake falls. The path opens.

[MUSIC CUE: Sharp, driving percussion. The pace doubles. No more safety.]

Beyond the inner gate, open sky. A canyon. And sleeping beneath a stone arch, bathed in mirror-light like a basking god — a tyrant lizard. A halfling rogue in a bat-wing cloak scales the wall in total darkness. Silent hands. The golden idol lifts free. He leaps.

A fireball screams down from above. Rivals. The T-Rex wakes.

[PAUSE]

Four minutes. A skill challenge at a dead sprint through collapsing corridors, reactivated blade traps, and a stone door that takes every ounce of strength to raise. Two mages materialize in the doorway. A cleric commands one to flee. A warlock fills the other's mind with phantom raptors. They run.

The jungle rises. The dead claw out of the earth. The rivals scream. The bubble holds.

[MUSIC CUE: The music cuts. Total silence. Then — a single, low, sustained tone. Something wrong.]

Morning. The party sits safe inside the Fizzle Field, loot heavy in their packs. But a half-elf with a stained fedora and the desperate eyes of a disgraced professor reaches into the golden idol when no one is looking. His fingers close around a jeweled eye. He pockets it. No one sees.

[PAUSE]

And the sun does not rise.

[MUSIC CUE: The low tone fades to nothing. Absolute silence.]

Quick Reference

  • Unresolved threads:
  • Aramil secretly stole the Eye of the Serpent from the golden idol — no other party member knows
  • The sun has stopped rising over Faerun; eternal darkness has fallen, and the stolen eye may be the cause
  • One member of the rival party allegedly survived by riding an eagle — a potential future threat
  • Fizzleblast has not yet received the idol or learned of its missing eye
  • The temple was barely explored — entire sections remain untouched, and an obsidian mechanism was left behind
  • Player-specific hooks:
  • Moor (Kyle 1): A cleric of Tyr who kept everyone alive through 148 HP of healing — now the sun his god watches over has gone dark
  • Aramil (Kyle 2): Possesses the stolen Eye of the Serpent and carries a secret that may have doomed the continent — all for his father's approval
  • Opal (Shannon): Fought blind and still killed the snake, but the Belt of Stone Giant Strength and a Bloodaxe cannot fix a world without light
  • Lavender (Kelsey): Session MVP whose imp Tika and quick thinking saved the party repeatedly — but her promised deal to free her genie patron Jezebel remains unfulfilled
  • Bob (Josh): Carries the golden idol itself, the Cloak of the Bat, and most of the loot — a thief who thrives in darkness, and darkness is all that remains
DM Utility

Handouts

DM Utility: Handouts & Reference Materials — Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip

Compiled from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip one-shot session.

DM: Blue Orbit (Brandon) | Players: Kyle 1 (Moor), Kyle 2 (Aramil), Shannon (Opal), Kelsey (Lavender), Josh (Bob)

73. NPC Stat Blocks

Fizzleblast

Small Humanoid (Gnome), Chaotic Neutral

  • Armor Class: 14 (Mage Armor)
  • Hit Points: 40 (9d6 + 9)
  • Speed: 25 ft.
  • Abilities: STR 6, DEX 12, CON 12, INT 18, WIS 10, CHA 14
  • Skills: Arcana +7, History +7
  • Senses: Darkvision 60 ft., Passive Perception 10
  • Languages: Common, Gnomish, Draconic, Elvish
  • Challenge: 5 (1,800 XP)
  • Actions:
  • Fizzle Field (1/day): Creates a 30 ft. radius protective bubble that repels organic matter, undead, and hostile entities for 24 hours. Creatures inside can see out (translucent). Sound does not pass through the barrier.
  • Spellcasting (Wizard, 9th level): Spell save DC 15, +7 to hit. Known spells include Mage Armor, Shield, Dimension Door, and Polymorph (threatened to use on Niles).

DM Tactical Notes: Fizzleblast does not fight. He hides in his bubble, sleeps with a teddy bear, and waits for the hired help to do the work. If confronted, he would Dimension Door away or threaten Polymorph. He is a patron, not a combatant.

Rival Party Leader (Mage)

Medium Humanoid, Neutral Evil

  • Armor Class: 12 (no armor)
  • Hit Points: 33 (6d8 + 6)
  • Speed: 30 ft.
  • Abilities: STR 9, DEX 14, CON 12, INT 16, WIS 12, CHA 10
  • Senses: Passive Perception 11
  • Challenge: 3 (700 XP)
  • Actions:
  • Fireball (3rd level, Recharge 5-6): 8d6 fire damage in a 20 ft. radius, DC 14 DEX save for half. Observed: hit Bob mid-glide and woke Ransom's Rex.
  • Dimension Door: Teleports up to 500 ft. with one willing creature. Observed: two mages teleported to the temple exit for ambush.
  • Firebolt: +6 to hit, 2d10 fire damage.

DM Tactical Notes: The rival mages fight smart and dirty. They let the environment do the killing (waking the T-Rex via Fireball) and use Dimension Door for ambush positioning. Morale is low — Phantasmal Force or Command easily breaks their nerve. They flee when cornered.

Giant Constrictor Snake

Huge Beast, Unaligned

  • Armor Class: 12 (Natural Armor)
  • Hit Points: 60 (8d12 + 8)
  • Speed: 30 ft., Swim 30 ft.
  • Abilities: STR 19, DEX 14, CON 12, INT 1, WIS 10, CHA 3
  • Senses: Blindsight 10 ft., Passive Perception 10
  • Challenge: 2 (450 XP)
  • Actions:
  • Bite: +6 to hit, reach 10 ft. Hit: 11 (2d6 + 4) piercing damage. Target must succeed DC 12 CON save or be paralyzed for 1 round. Observed: paralyzed and killed Tika.
  • Constrict: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 13 (2d8 + 4) bludgeoning damage, target is grappled (escape DC 16) and restrained. Observed: dealt 22 damage to Aramil in a single constriction, dropping him to -10 HP.

DM Tactical Notes: Opens by targeting the smallest/weakest creature (Tika). Cannot be reasoned with — Aramil's Speak with Animals failed. Focuses fire on whoever angered it. Fights to the death. Emerged from a snake statue mouth when the temple heated up.

Gorallons (x2)

Large Monstrosity, Unaligned

  • Armor Class: 13 (Natural Armor)
  • Hit Points: 59 (7d10 + 21)
  • Speed: 40 ft., Climb 40 ft.
  • Abilities: STR 18, DEX 11, CON 16, INT 5, WIS 12, CHA 7
  • Actions:
  • Multiattack: Four claw attacks.
  • Claw: +6 to hit, reach 5 ft. Hit: 7 (1d6 + 4) slashing damage.

DM Tactical Notes: Aggressive but not suicidal. Can be intimidated if shown overwhelming force (one watched its companion get melted and fled when Aramil spoke to it). Use as a tutorial encounter — they teach players that mirrors are weapons and that creative solutions work. One was instantly killed by a redirected mirror beam; the other fled after a DC 15 Intimidation check via Speak with Animals.

Ransom's Rex (Tyrannosaurus Rex)

Huge Beast, Unaligned

  • Armor Class: 13 (Natural Armor)
  • Hit Points: 136 (13d12 + 52)
  • Speed: 50 ft.
  • Abilities: STR 25, DEX 10, CON 19, INT 2, WIS 12, CHA 9
  • Senses: Passive Perception 11
  • Challenge: 8 (3,900 XP)
  • Actions:
  • Bite: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft. Hit: 33 (4d12 + 7) piercing damage. If the target is Medium or smaller, it is grappled (escape DC 17).
  • Tail: +10 to hit, reach 10 ft. Hit: 20 (3d8 + 7) bludgeoning damage.

DM Tactical Notes: DO NOT FIGHT. Ransom's Rex is an environmental hazard, not an encounter. It sleeps under the mirror heat lamps. If woken (by fireball, loud noise, or the mirrors breaking), it rampages — the party must flee. A level 3 party has zero chance against CR 8 with these stats. Used to trigger the escape skill challenge.

74. NPC Voice Cue Cards

Fizzleblast

  • Voice: Rapid, high-pitched, nasal. Flustered middle-manager energy.
  • Speech Pattern: Rambling, self-interrupting. Starts instructions, contradicts himself, blames the listener.
  • Verbal Tics: "All right," "okay," "right?" as constant bridges. References himself in third person.
  • Key Phrases: "I hate repeating myself." / "Consider it a signing bonus." / "Touch nothing but the lamp — wrong franchise."
  • Emotional Range: Neurotic default. Brief generosity when pleased. Shrill when angry (directed at Niles). Retreats to safety when scared.

Niles

  • Voice: High-pitched, breathless, perpetually apologetic.
  • Speech Pattern: Short, stuttering sentences between physical exertion.
  • Verbal Tics: "I'm sorry, I'm sorry" on repeat.
  • Key Phrases: "I'm coming, I'm coming, Mr. Fizzleblast."
  • Emotional Range: Frantic early. Silent and shaking at session end.

Tika (Lavender's Imp)

  • Voice: Nasal New Jersey accent. Sassy.
  • Speech Pattern: Short, confrontational quips.
  • Key Phrases: "Hey! Meow."
  • Emotional Range: Obedient but mouthy. Brave to recklessness in combat.

Rival Party Leader

  • Voice: Low, desperate, calculating.
  • Speech Pattern: Clipped tactical commands. Breaks under pressure.
  • Key Phrases: "Break the mirrors... wake the T-Rex up... kill the guys."
  • Emotional Range: Cunning when in control. Panics when plan fails.

75. Player Handout Packets

Moor (Kyle 1) — Dragonborn Cleric of Tyr

Starting Gear: Adamantine Plate Armor (AC 18, crit immunity)

Found in Temple: Staff of Healing, Amulet of the Devout (+2 spell attack/DC, free Channel Divinity), Alchemy Jug, Manual of Quickness of Action

Key Knowledge: Solved the Man and Crocodile pressure plate puzzle. Turned 7 skeletons with Channel Divinity. Does NOT know Aramil stole the Eye.

Aramil (Kyle 2) — Half-Elf Warlock

Starting Gear: Gloves of Thievery (+5 Sleight of Hand)

Found in Temple: Helm of Telepathy, Rod of Alertness ("Rod of Onondog")

Secret: Stole the Eye of the Serpent from the Golden Snake Idol (Sleight of Hand 18). Caused the eternal darkness. NO other party member knows.

Key Knowledge: Read the mural depicting the serpent/sun connection. Understands the curse.

Opal (Shannon) — Goliath Barbarian

Starting Gear: Pearl of Power (regain spell slot)

Found in Temple: Belt of Hill Giant Strength (STR 21), Eldritch Claw Tattoo, Bloodaxe, Periapt of Wound Closure

Key Moments: Went blind (Nat 1 on mirror, 14 damage). Killed the Giant Snake with a crit while blind (30 damage). Does NOT know about the Eye theft.

Lavender (Kelsey) — Tiefling Warlock (Genie Pact)

Starting Gear: Bracers of Defense (+2 AC)

Found in Temple: Zephyr Reliquary (fire immunity), Rod of the Pact Keeper, Staff of Healing (given to Moor)

Key Moments: Immune to fire, walked through beams to solve the prism puzzle. Lost Tika to the snake, resummoned. Session MVP (8.4 weighted score).

Bob (Josh) — Halfling Rogue

Starting Gear: Slippers of Spider Climbing

Found in Temple: Cloak of the Bat (flight in dim light, advantage on Stealth), Boots of Striding and Springing, Cape of the Mountebank (Dimension Door), Scales of Justice (full dice reroll 1/long rest)

Key Moments: Climbed the arch over the sleeping T-Rex to steal the idol. Dodged the Fireball (DEX save 24). Cleared the entire blade corridor in one jump.

76. Item Cards

Item Type Properties Acquired By Location Found
Staff of Healing Staff, Rare (attunement: Cleric/Druid/Bard) Charges: Cure Wounds, Lesser Restoration, Mass Cure Wounds. 10 charges, regains 1d6+4 at dawn. Moor Treasure pile, Antechamber
Belt of Hill Giant Strength Wondrous, Rare (attunement) STR score becomes 21 while worn. Opal Treasure pile, Antechamber
Cloak of the Bat Wondrous, Rare (attunement) Advantage on Stealth. In dim light/darkness: fly 40 ft., can wrap to polymorph into bat. Bob Treasure pile (rolled 17+)
Scales of Justice Wondrous, Rare Full dice reroll once per long rest for entire party. Stolen Indiana Jones-style from pedestal. Bob/Moor Prism Room pedestal
Zephyr Reliquary Wondrous, Rare (attunement) Grants immunity to fire damage. Matched Lavender's genie patron locket design. Removing it caused the temple to heat up. Lavender 30 ft. high shelf (Tika retrieved)
Golden Snake Idol Quest Item, Artifact Gold snake statue with jeweled eyes. Connected to the sun — removing the Eye caused eternal darkness across Faerun. Party (for Fizzleblast) Arch above T-Rex roost
Adamantine Plate Armor, Very Rare AC 18. Critical hits against the wearer become normal hits. Instantly attuned via spilled potion. Moor Loot Sack Explosion

Additional Notable Items

Item Holder Properties
Bracers of Defense Lavender +2 AC when not wearing armor or shield
Gloves of Thievery Aramil +5 to Sleight of Hand and lockpicking (used to steal the Eye)
Slippers of Spider Climbing Bob Walk on walls and ceilings, hands free
Pearl of Power Opal Regain one expended spell slot (3rd level or lower)
Eldritch Claw Tattoo Opal Unarmed strikes are magical, +1, 15 ft. reach option
Rod of the Pact Keeper Lavender +1 spell attack and save DC, regain one spell slot 1/long rest
Helm of Telepathy Aramil Detect Thoughts at will, Suggestion 1/day
Boots of Striding and Springing Bob Jump distance tripled, speed cannot be reduced below 30 ft.
Eye of the Serpent Aramil (secret) Worth 5,000+ gp. Removal cursed Faerun with eternal darkness.

77. Battle Map Descriptions

Map 1: The Chultan Shore / Fizzle Field

Dimensions: 60 x 40 ft. open beach.

Terrain: Black sand, two beached canoes, jungle treeline to the north, water to the south. Fizzleblast's translucent safety bubble (30 ft. radius dome) sits center-beach.

Features: The bubble glows faintly, repels organic/undead matter. Niles's scattered loot creates difficult terrain in a 10 ft. radius where the Bag of Holding exploded.

Lighting: Bright (daytime arrival), pitch black by session end.

Map 2: Temple Entrance / Mirror Arrays

Dimensions: 40 x 30 ft. stone platform before the temple mouth.

Terrain: Stone steps flanked by two heliographic thermal arrays (large angled mirrors on rotating stone mounts). Jungle encroaches from both sides.

Features: Mirrors can be rotated with DC 14 Strength checks (DC 10 with help). Focused beams deal 8d6 radiant/fire damage to any creature in the path. Two Gorallons pace the beam edges.

Lighting: Blinding in beam paths, shadowed at edges.

Map 3: The Antechamber of Weights

Dimensions: 80 x 30 ft. rectangular hall, 30 ft. ceiling.

Terrain: Two rows of pressure plates run the full length. Treasure piles (gold, weapons, skeletons) heaped along both walls. Portcullis at far end with two mounted greatswords.

Features: Pressure plates trigger wall-mounted blades (4d6 slashing) unless both plates in a pair are weighted simultaneously (Man and Crocodile mural hint). Greatswords must be pulled together to open the portcullis.

Lighting: Dim (reflected light from entrance mirrors).

Map 4: The Prism Room

Dimensions: 60 x 60 ft. vaulted chamber, 40 ft. ceiling.

Terrain: Central crystalline prism on a bar. Two massive stone snake statues with reflective eyes. Four unlit gems crown the far portcullis. Floor-mounted mirrors on rotating bases. Gas vents along floor edges.

Features: Prism splits beams into spectral lances. Snake statue eyes redirect light. Rotating mirrors and statues into alignment lights all four gems to open the exit. Gas trap triggers on pressure plates (DC 15 CON, 14 poison damage). Giant Constrictor Snake emerges from statue mouth when temperature rises.

Lighting: Prismatic beams create shifting colored light; deep shadows between beams.

Map 5: The Rooftop Canyon / T-Rex Roost

Dimensions: 100 x 50 ft. open-air canyon, 60 ft. walls.

Terrain: Natural stone canyon at the temple summit. Ridgeline above crawling with crocodiles, boas, and gorallons (none descend). Stone archway over the center with the Golden Snake Idol hanging from it.

Features: Ransom's Rex sleeps center-canyon under the focused mirror heat. Pteranodons nest on ledges (alarm risk). Canyon walls are climbable (DC 12 Athletics). The arch is 20 ft. above the T-Rex.

Lighting: Fading dusk — dim light transitioning to darkness.

85. Session Infographic Poster

"Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip: The Eye and the Everlasting Dark"

Layout: Vertical 24x36 poster, jungle-temple aesthetic

Header Band: Title in weathered gold serif font over a dark jungle canopy. Subtitle: "A One-Shot by Blue Orbit." The Golden Snake Idol silhouette flanks the title, one eye socket empty.

Character Strip (top third): Five character portraits in circular frames connected by a dotted jungle path. Left to right: Moor (white robes, adamantine plate, amber eyes), Opal (massive frame, blindfold, bloodied greataxe), Aramil (stained fedora, purple crystal, guilty expression), Lavender (jeweled horns, bracers glowing, Tika on shoulder), Bob (hood, mustache, bat-wing cloak spread). Each frame lists name, race, class, and one signature stat.

Timeline Ribbon (middle third): Horizontal snake-shaped timeline winding across the poster. Seven labeled segments matching the seven scenes, each with an icon: canoe (Shore), mirror (Entrance), weight scale (Antechamber), prism (Prism Room), snake eye (Puzzle), T-Rex skull (Rooftop), eclipse (Blackout). Key moments annotated along the snake's body.

Stats Block (lower left): Dark panel with session statistics.

  • Total Party Damage Taken: 171 HP
  • Total Healing Done: 148 HP (all Moor)
  • Characters Downed: 1 (Aramil)
  • Leeroy Jenkins Moments: 5
  • Session MVP: Lavender (8.4)
  • Nat 20s Rolled: (highlight rolls)
  • Magic Items Found: 15+

Loot Grid (lower center): 3x3 grid of illustrated item cards showing the top items: Adamantine Plate, Belt of Hill Giant Strength, Cloak of the Bat, Zephyr Reliquary, Staff of Healing, Scales of Justice, Gloves of Thievery, Golden Snake Idol, Eye of the Serpent.

Quote Banner (lower right): Parchment-style scroll containing the top three quotes:

  1. "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." — Lavender
  2. "Run through door. Why use many word when small word do trick?" — Moor
  3. "And how many squirtons in a gusher?" — Bob

    4. Footer: The sun disk from the mural, half-eclipsed, with the tagline: "The sun will not rise again in Faerun."

    86. Spotify Wrapped: Session Edition

    Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip — Your 2025 Session Wrapped

    Your Session In Numbers

    • Minutes Played: 225
    • Dice Rolled: ~300+
    • Skill Checks Made: 40+
    • Spells Cast: 22
    • Things Looted: "Yes"

    Your Top Genre

    JUNGLE DUNGEON CRAWL

    You spent 67% of your session inside a death-trap temple and loved every minute of it.

    Your #1 Hit

    "Blind Goliath Kills Giant Snake (feat. Critical Hit)"

    Streamed live. Zero skips. Everyone screamed.

    Your Top Artist

    MOOR — The Silent Healer

    148 HP healed. Carried the party. Said 12 words total.

    Your Most Replayed Moment

    Bob stealing the idol while hanging upside-down over a sleeping T-Rex, 20 feet in the air, in total silence.

    Replays: infinite.

    Your Listening Personality: The Loot Goblins

    You are: 89% Greedy, 74% Reckless, 62% Clever, 100% Lucky

    Your Top 5 Tracks

    2 "Shapeshift (Don't Tell the Church)" Lavender Quote of the Session
