Aramil

"I am adjunct professor emeritus. Thank you."

Overview

Aramil is a gaunt, paranoid-looking Half-Elf who resembles "a very dingy, dirty Indiana Jones who has kind of lost it." He wears a stained and torn fedora and a purple crystal necklace, and his entire bearing suggests a man who was once respectable and is now operating several rungs below his self-image. He introduced himself as an adjunct professor emeritus at the [[Royal Community College]], though the "sabbatical" that brought him here was almost certainly not voluntary — a fact Lavender immediately identified: "So you're fired."

Behind the academic pretensions, Aramil is opportunistic, self-serving, and willing to compromise the entire party's mission for personal gain. He speaks with animals as a warlock invocation, using persuasion and intimidation interchangeably depending on what seems more expedient. He is the party member most likely to make a joke about someone else's misfortune and least likely to volunteer for physical labor ("I'm working on the rope that I was told to work on"). His combat contributions are meaningful but secondary to his real interest: artifacts.

The defining act of the session — and perhaps the entire campaign — was Aramil's secret theft of the [[Eye of the Serpent]] from the Golden Snake Idol as the party escaped. With an 18 on the sleight of hand check, nobody saw him do it. The consequence: the sun will not rise again in [[Faerun]]. Aramil's response to learning this? "If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay." This is a character whose desperate need for paternal approval and professional validation has made him capable of world-ending selfishness — and he might not even understand what he has done.

Appearance

  • Physical Build & Features: Gaunt, paranoid-looking Half-Elf.
  • Clothing & Armor: Stained and torn fedora, purple crystal necklace. Described as looking like "a very dingy, dirty Indiana Jones who has kind of lost it."
  • Distinguishing Details: The contrast between how he thinks he looks and how he actually looks was noted by his player: "I liked what he thinks he looks like, vs what he actually looks like."
  • How Others See Them: [[Fizzleblast]] misheard his name as "Arrow Mill" and compared it to a "budget laundry detergent." The party constantly mangled his name throughout the session.

[!note] Appearance Confidence

Physical description is directly from player narration and confirmed in transcript.

Personality

Demonstrated Traits

Trait Evidence Session
Desperate for academic validation and paternal approval "Maybe my dad finally noticed me." "If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay." Stole the Eye of the Serpent specifically for prestige. 1
Opportunistic and self-serving behind a veneer of helpfulness Stole the Eye of the Serpent secretly (sleight of hand 18) while the party was escaping. Earlier, tried to claim two bags of holding. Expressed jealousy when Lavender found the Rod of the Pact Keeper: "That's what I wanted!" 1
Academic posturing masking insecurity Corrected everyone who mispronounced his name. Insisted on the title "adjunct professor emeritus." Used his knowledge of exothermic biology to explain T-Rex behavior — "I'm a professor." 1
Sarcastic and quick-witted When the DM said his name sounded like laundry detergent: "I'll be keeping track of those, thank you." When told the Pearl of Power went to Opal: "That would have been amazing for one of us." 1
Willing to negotiate with anything, even hostile creatures Used Speak with Animals on the Gorallon: "Hey, big guy. Brother Gorallon. Think about your wife and kids." Later attempted (unsuccessfully) to persuade the Giant Constrictor Snake to eat monkeys instead. 1
Avoids physical labor when possible "I'm working on the rope that I was told to work on." "I'm appreciating art." Deferred mirror-moving and heavy lifting to others while investigating murals and using magic. 1

Speech Patterns & Voice

  • Vocabulary: Academic and slightly pretentious. Uses titles and formal constructions ("adjunct professor emeritus," "I recently took a sabbatical from my position").
  • Dialogue Style: Deflective. Answers direct questions with tangents or qualifications. When Lavender said "So you're fired," he responded with his full title rather than confirming or denying.
  • Humor: Dry, sometimes cruel. Noted others' failures with detached amusement ("Just like the professionals" when Opal destroyed half a mural).
  • Under Stress: Becomes more desperate and less careful about concealing his true motivations. His final theft was performed under maximum pressure.

Decision-Making Pattern

Aramil makes decisions that serve Aramil. He volunteered for the rope-untying task (low-effort), chose to investigate murals (matching his archaeologist background), and positioned himself to acquire artifacts throughout the session. His most consequential decision — stealing the Eye of the Serpent — was made unilaterally and in secret. He did not consult, confess, or hesitate. This pattern suggests a character who calculates risk-reward for himself, not for the group.

Abilities & Combat Style

Observed Abilities

Ability / Spell / Feature Type Observed Effect Session Notes
Eldritch Blast Cantrip Used in combat against the Giant Constrictor Snake (6 damage); missed the snake on first attempt (rolled 9) 1 Primary damage cantrip
Speak with Animals Warlock Invocation (at will) Used to communicate with the Gorallon (intimidation/persuasion, rolled 22 with advantage); attempted to persuade the Giant Constrictor Snake to eat monkeys 1 Used creatively for social encounters with beasts
Mage Hand Cantrip Mentioned as option for retrieving the idol ("I'll mage hand it") but determined the idol was too heavy (10+ pounds) 1
Phantasmal Force 2nd Level Spell Made rival adventurers perceive a pack of raptors chasing them during the escape; DM ruled the target rolled a Nat 1 and fled, grabbing his ally 1 Intelligence save; highly effective in the escape
Comprehend Languages 1st Level Spell Mentioned as available ("I can comprehend languages") during the poison gas crisis 1 Not used effectively in-session
Telepathy Via Helm of Telepathy Read the T-Rex's surface thoughts: "annoyed with the pterodactyls when they screech" 1 Provided tactical intelligence for the heist
Sleight of Hand Skill (Enhanced) +6 bonus with Gloves of Thievery. Used to steal the [[Eye of the Serpent]] (rolled 18) without anyone noticing 1 The session's most consequential skill check

Combat Behavior Profile

  • Preferred Range: Back line. Explicitly positioned himself in the rear during marching order.
  • Opening Move: Ranged attack (Eldritch Blast) or social engagement (Speak with Animals).
  • Tactical Role: Utility caster and social manipulator. Limited direct damage output.
  • Risk Tolerance: Low for physical danger, high for moral compromise. Was knocked unconscious by the snake (0 HP) and needed rescue.
  • Signature Moments: Intimidating the Gorallon with Speak with Animals (rolled 22); casting Phantasmal Force to scatter the rival party during the final escape; secretly stealing the Eye of the Serpent.
  • Weaknesses Observed: Low physical stats. Was grappled and constricted by the Giant Constrictor Snake, taking 22 damage and dropping to -10 HP. Required Moor's Cure Wounds to survive.

Equipment & Inventory

Weapons & Armor

Item Type Properties Acquired Notes
Eldritch Blast Cantrip Force damage, ranged Class feature Primary offensive ability

Notable Possessions

Item Description Significance
[[Gloves of Thievery]] +5 to Sleight of Hand and lockpicking Acquired from Loot Explosion. Made the Eye theft possible
[[Sentinel Shield]] Advantage on initiative and Perception Found during looting
[[Helm of Telepathy]] Read surface thoughts Used to scan T-Rex's thoughts
[[Devotee's Censer]] Flail with bonus radiant damage, healing cloud Found during looting
[[Cape of the Mountebank]] Cast Dimension Door once per day Found by Bob, given to Aramil
[[Eye of the Serpent]] Gem stolen from the Golden Snake Idol Secretly stolen; worth ~5,000 gold; may have caused eternal darkness
Purple crystal necklace Worn around neck Part of character description
Stained and torn fedora Headwear Iconic Indiana Jones-style accessory

Consumables & Currency

  • Gold/Wealth: The [[Eye of the Serpent]] is worth approximately 5,000 gold but is cursed and cannot be sold.

Relationships

Party Members

Character Relationship Dynamic Key Moments Trajectory
[[Moor]] Fellow "Kyle," reluctant alliance Bickers but defers when it matters; Moor healed him from 0 HP Moor healed Aramil from unconsciousness after the snake; Aramil worked rope duty with Bob while Moor solved puzzles Complicated — Moor is Lawful Good; Aramil stole a cursed artifact
[[Lavender Villani]] Competitive fellow Warlock Jealous of her loot but willing to trade; teased her about the Rod of the Pact Keeper "God damn it! That's what I wanted!" when Lavender found the Rod; she gave him items she found Complicated — both Warlocks with very different moral frameworks
[[Opal]] Amused observer Flirted briefly ("I'm a fan of big ladies"); generally let Opal handle physical tasks Gave her Guidance; commented on her strength; "Just like the professionals" when she destroyed the mural Stable — superficial
[[Bob]] Closest operational ally Worked rope duty together; joked together; Bob enabled the idol heist that Aramil then secretly compromised Paired for rope untying; Bob's stealth heist gave Aramil the opportunity to steal the Eye Stable — Bob does not know about the theft

NPCs

NPC Relationship Disposition Key Interactions
[[Fizzleblast]] Employer (and mark) Contemptuous tolerance Stole the Eye while Fizzleblast slept; answered questions sarcastically
[[Gorallon]] Temporary negotiating partner Exploitative Used Speak with Animals to intimidate it into fleeing; invoked its "wife and kids"
Aramil's Father Absent parent Desperate longing "Maybe my dad finally noticed me" — the theft of the Eye is partly motivated by desire for paternal recognition

Factions

Faction Relationship Status
[[Charisma Company]] Hired adventurer Active (under false pretenses — has a personal agenda)
[[Royal Community College]] Former faculty Disgraced — "adjunct professor emeritus" (fired/forced out)

Key Decisions

Session Decision Context Consequence What It Reveals
1 Used Speak with Animals to intimidate the Gorallon rather than fight it Second Gorallon was pacing outside the mirror beams Gorallon fled; party avoided combat and conserved resources Prefers manipulation over violence; uses words as weapons
1 Chose to investigate murals rather than help with physical tasks Party split between rope-tying, looting, and exploration Discovered critical lore about the serpent idol's connection to the sun — and chose to steal it anyway Knowledge is a tool for personal gain, not wisdom
1 Attempted to persuade the Giant Constrictor Snake to eat monkeys instead of the party Snake was approaching the party; combat seemed inevitable Failed the persuasion check; snake attacked and nearly killed him Overconfidence in his social abilities; underestimates physical threats
1 Stole the Eye of the Serpent secretly during the escape Party had retrieved the Golden Snake Idol; Aramil saw the gem and took it (sleight of hand 18) The sun will not rise again in Faerun; eternal darkness falls; no one in the party knows The defining act of the session. Reveals a character who will sacrifice the world for personal validation.

Character Arc

Arc Summary

Aramil's arc is a tragedy that the other characters do not yet know is happening. He entered the session as a disgraced academic seeking artifacts for prestige and paternal approval. He contributed meaningfully to the party — his Speak with Animals saved the group from the Gorallon fight, his knowledge of the murals revealed the idol's lore, and his Phantasmal Force was critical to the final escape. But these contributions were always in service of positioning himself for his real goal: acquisition.

The theft of the Eye of the Serpent is the culmination of everything established about Aramil's character. He knew — because he read the murals — that removing the serpent's eye might plunge the land into eternal darkness. He did it anyway. "If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay." This is not villainy for its own sake. It is desperation so deep that consequences cease to register. Aramil is not evil because he wants to cause harm; he is evil because causing harm is a price he is willing to pay for his father's attention.

Arc Trajectory

  • Starting State (Session 1): Disgraced academic, desperate for validation, joining the expedition for artifact acquisition.
  • Inciting Pressure: Discovery of the murals warning about the serpent idol's connection to the sun.
  • Current State (Session 1): Has secretly doomed the continent to eternal darkness. No one in the party knows.
  • Unresolved Tension: What happens when the party discovers what Aramil did? Will his father's approval be worth it?
  • Predicted Direction: Aramil's secret will surface. The party's reaction — particularly Moor's, as a Lawful Good cleric of Tyr — will be explosive. The "Tube of Annihilation" campaign teased by the DM is a direct consequence of Aramil's choice. [SPECULATIVE]

Backstory References

Detail Source Confidence Session Context
Adjunct Professor Emeritus at the Royal Community College Player dialogue (in-character) [CONFIRMED] 1 "I am adjunct professor emeritus."
"Sabbatical" from the college (implied firing) Player dialogue; Lavender's response [CONFIRMED] 1 "I recently took a sabbatical from my position" — Lavender: "So you're fired."
Seeking artifacts for prestige and paternal approval Player dialogue (in-character) [CONFIRMED] 1 "Maybe my dad finally noticed me."
Half-Elf heritage Player description [CONFIRMED] 1 "Aramil is a half-elf"
Archaeologist/academic background Player dialogue and behavior [CONFIRMED] 1 Background choice; chosen to investigate murals; knowledge of exothermic biology

Notable Quotes

Quote Context Session Why It Matters
"I am adjunct professor emeritus. Thank you." Correcting Lavender after she said "So you're fired" 1 Encapsulates his insecurity masked by academic titles
"Maybe my dad finally noticed me." Explaining his motivation for the expedition 1 The emotional core of the character in six words
"If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay." After learning the idol's removal could cause eternal darkness 1 Reveals that personal validation outweighs global consequences
"Hey, big guy. Brother Gorallon. Think about your wife and kids." Using Speak with Animals to intimidate the Gorallon 1 Shows his negotiation style: empathy weaponized as manipulation
"I'm appreciating art." When others were fighting the snake or doing physical tasks 1 His default mode: intellectual detachment from physical danger
"Brother Snake, there are tasty monkeys down there." Attempting to persuade the Giant Constrictor Snake 1 Tried the same approach on every beast; it only works sometimes
"I have no commitment to your patron." Advising Lavender to break her Zephyr Reliquary to stop the heat 1 Comfortable encouraging others to sacrifice things he would never sacrifice himself

Session Timeline

Session Key Events for This Character
Session 1 - Received [[Gloves of Thievery]] from the loot explosion
- Intimidated a Gorallon into fleeing using Speak with Animals (rolled 22)
- Investigated murals and learned the serpent idol's connection to the sun and potential for eternal darkness
- Knocked unconscious by the Giant Constrictor Snake (22 damage, dropped to -10 HP); saved by Moor's Cure Wounds
- Found [[Sentinel Shield]], [[Helm of Telepathy]], and [[Devotee's Censer]] in loot piles
- Read the T-Rex's surface thoughts via Helm of Telepathy, providing tactical intelligence
- Cast Phantasmal Force on a rival mage during the escape (target Nat 1'd, fled with his ally)
- Secretly stole the [[Eye of the Serpent]] from the Golden Snake Idol (sleight of hand 18); plunged Faerun into eternal darkness

Last updated after Session 1 (One-shot). Profile compiled from Session 1.

sessions_appeared: [1]

last_updated: "Session 1"

tags:

  • pc
  • goliath
  • barbarian

Opal

"I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!"

Overview

Opal is a massive Goliath Barbarian — "hewed and jacked," with visible muscles that make her one of the most physically imposing members of [[Charisma Company]]. She towers over her companions, a fact that becomes comedically relevant when the halfling Bob ends up literally between her legs to help move a mirror. She is a force of nature with the intelligence of a gentle breeze, and she knows it: "She's a Goliath. Barbarian. She hewed and jacked. And she kind of, she was kind of dumb."

Opal's personality is pure, unfiltered enthusiasm directed at everything she encounters. She is delighted to be on the expedition ("I'm delighted to be here. I'm delighted by everyone"), fascinated by dinosaurs, obsessed with a black obsidian rock she found in a mechanism, and eager to chop things with her great axe. She does not overthink. When the party debates strategy, Opal is already moving: "What if I just chop the snake?" Her emotional range runs from excited to angry, with brief stops at confused. Complex feelings are processed through the Goliath framework: "Goliath feel calm or angry."

The session's most harrowing moment for Opal was going blind from the mirror beam — a Nat 1 on pushing a mirror resulted in 14 radiant damage directly to her eyes. Rather than retreating, she fought the Giant Constrictor Snake blind, landing a critical hit with her Great Axe for 30 damage that killed the creature outright. It was the most memorable combat moment of the session, performed by a character who could not see her target. When her vision was restored by Moor's Lesser Restoration, she simply moved on to the next challenge. Opal does not dwell; Opal acts.

Appearance

  • Physical Build & Features: Massive Goliath. "Hewed and jacked." Visible muscles. Significantly larger than all other party members.
  • Clothing & Armor: AC 15 at baseline. Picked up a shield from Moor (+2 AC).
  • Distinguishing Details: Size is her most distinguishing feature. The DM described her as "Paleolithic chic."
  • How Others See Them: [[Fizzleblast]] misheard her name as "Oaf" and wrote it down. The party treats her physical dominance as both an asset and a source of comedy.

[!note] Appearance Confidence

Physical description is from player narration. Specific clothing, skin tone, and markings not described in transcript. ⚠️ Standard Goliath features (gray skin, body markings) are assumed but not confirmed.

Personality

Demonstrated Traits

Trait Evidence Session
Boundless enthusiasm regardless of circumstances "I'm delighted to be here. I'm delighted by everyone." Excited about dinosaurs, obsidian rocks, and every new room. Even after going blind: not genuinely distressed. 1
Low intelligence, openly acknowledged and embraced "She kind of, she was kind of dumb." Struggled with math ("I don't know how to do math"), destroyed half a mural while trying to clean it (Sleight of Hand 12), and asked questions others found obvious. 1
Physically fearless, charges into danger Volunteered for the front line with Moor. Raged to move a mirror despite failing the first check. Chopped the snake while blind. Offered to take fire damage to help Lavender move statues. 1
Emotionally simple but genuine "Goliath feel calm or angry." Held Lavender's hand when blind and said "this is very nice, thank you." Her emotional responses are immediate and unfiltered. 1
Fixated on objects of interest (the obsidian rock) Repeatedly returned to the obsidian rock in the mechanism. "I go get it. I want it." Was disappointed about leaving without it: "I didn't get my rock." 1
Competitive about physical tasks "Have you seen these quads?" Immediately volunteered for every strength check. Wanted the Belt of Stone Giant Strength because it made her even stronger. 1

Speech Patterns & Voice

  • Vocabulary: Simple and direct. Short declarative sentences. Occasional malapropisms and confusion about game mechanics.
  • Dialogue Style: Enthusiastic exclamations followed by immediate action requests. "I want to chop the snake!" "I go get it!" "What if I just chop the snake?"
  • Humor: Unintentional. The humor comes from her earnestness colliding with situations that call for subtlety.
  • Under Stress: Gets louder and more physically aggressive. When blinded: "I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!"

Decision-Making Pattern

Opal decides with her muscles. If a problem can be chopped, punched, or pushed, she is first in line. If it requires reading, investigation, or patience, she defers to others or simply ignores it. Her decision-making is immediate and physical — she does not weigh options; she picks the most direct path and applies force. This makes her both the party's most reliable combatant and its most likely source of collateral damage.

Abilities & Combat Style

Observed Abilities

Ability / Spell / Feature Type Observed Effect Session Notes
Rage Class Feature Used to gain advantage on strength checks (mirror moving); provides resistance to bludgeoning, piercing, slashing damage 1 Used strategically for strength checks, not just combat
Reckless Attack Class Feature (Implied) Not explicitly used in transcript but referenced by other players as available 1 [IMPLIED]
Great Axe Weapon Critical hit against the Giant Constrictor Snake for 30 damage (2d12+6), killing it while blind 1 Signature weapon
[[Bloodaxe]] Magical Great Axe Grants temporary HP on kills; can cleave to adjacent targets 1 Acquired during second loot phase
Unarmed Strike (enhanced) Via Eldritch Claw Tattoo +5 reach on unarmed attacks; attacks deal magical damage 1 "You punch the air so hard it hurts someone's life"
Stone's Endurance Racial Feature (Goliath) Not explicitly used but available 1 ⚠️ Inferred from race
Athletics Skill (Proficient) Used for mirror moving, sword pulling (rolled 17 and 20), and escape rope-pulling (rolled 19 with Guidance) 1 Advantage on athletics checks as a class feature
Beast Sense Spell/Feature Mentioned as available ("I have beast sense") but not used 1 Ritual spell
Speak with Animals Ritual Spell Mentioned as available but noted it takes 10 minutes as a ritual 1 Not used due to time constraints

Combat Behavior Profile

  • Preferred Range: Melee. Charges directly into combat.
  • Opening Move: Rage (if not already raging), then attack with Great Axe.
  • Tactical Role: Primary damage dealer and secondary tank. Hits hard but takes hits too.
  • Risk Tolerance: Extremely high. Fought the snake while blind. Offered to take fire damage from mirror beams to help the party.
  • Signature Moments: Critical hit on the Giant Constrictor Snake while blind (30 damage, killing blow). "I'm going to chop like a cake, but less delicately."
  • Weaknesses Observed: Low Dexterity and low Intelligence. Rolled Nat 1 on the mirror check that blinded her. No darkvision as a Goliath. Vulnerable to traps and effects requiring saves she is not proficient in.

Equipment & Inventory

Weapons & Armor

Item Type Properties Acquired Notes
Great Axe Martial Melee 1d12 slashing Starting equipment Used for the critical hit on the snake
[[Bloodaxe]] Magical Great Axe Temp HP on kill, cleave to adjacent targets Found in loot Upgraded weapon
Shield Armor +2 AC Given by Moor Added to base AC

Notable Possessions

Item Description Significance
[[Pearl of Power]] Regains one spell slot Ironic — Opal has no spell slots. Offered to give it to Moor after the session
[[Eldritch Claw Tattoo]] +5 reach on unarmed attacks, magical damage Permanent body modification; does not take an inventory slot
[[Belt of Stone Giant Strength]] Sets Strength to 23 (+6 modifier) Found by Bob for Opal; massive upgrade to her primary stat
[[Periapt of Wound Closure]] Stabilize at 1 HP when dropping to 0 Insurance against death
[[Potion of Superior Healing]] Healing potion Emergency use
Bandana Given by Lavender Worn over eyes when blind; sentimental

Consumables & Currency

  • Potions: [[Potion of Superior Healing]] (1)
  • Gold/Wealth: Not focused on wealth accumulation.

Relationships

Party Members

Character Relationship Dynamic Key Moments Trajectory
[[Lavender Villani]] Closest friend in the party Warm, nurturing dynamic. Lavender gave Opal a bandana for her eyes, held her hand while she was blind, "booped" her on the head. "I hold Lavender's hand. This is very nice, thank you." Lavender led Opal by hand through the temple. They worked together to move the snake statues. Strengthening — genuine affection
[[Moor]] Healer and protector Grateful trust. Moor healed her blindness, monitored her health, gave her Guidance before checks. "More! Thank you, Dragon." Moor used Lesser Restoration to cure her blindness and Cure Wounds throughout. Stable — functional appreciation
[[Bob]] Comedic odd couple The massive Goliath and the tiny Halfling. Bob stood between her legs to help move the mirror. "I'm like, ah" when Bob was literally between her legs. "What are you, Bob? Some kind of halfling robot?" Stable — amused tolerance
[[Aramil]] Wary acquaintance Aramil flirted with her ("I'm a fan of big ladies"). She was mildly uncomfortable. Later, he was supportive during combat. "Are you hitting on me?" Aramil: "I'm a fan of big ladies. Big ladies are fans of me." Complicated — she does not know about the Eye theft

NPCs

NPC Relationship Disposition Key Interactions
[[Fizzleblast]] Employer Amused tolerance Fizzleblast wrote down "Oaf" instead of "Opal"; she responded "Oaf, Opal, same, same"
[[Pearl]] Sister Love and connection "Her sister's name is Pearl and she still lives in Barovia. Just hanging out. She's living a very happy life, actually."

Factions

Faction Relationship Status
[[Charisma Company]] Hired adventurer Active

Key Decisions

Session Decision Context Consequence What It Reveals
1 Raged to move a mirror after failing the initial strength check Failed the first attempt (rolled 8); DM suggested Rage for advantage Succeeded on the second attempt (rolled 16 with advantage); helped redirect the beam Does not accept failure; applies more force until the problem yields
1 Continued fighting the snake after going blind Took 14 radiant damage to the eyes; DM confirmed blindness would last an hour Landed a critical hit (30 damage) that killed the snake Physical courage transcends physical limitation; her most defining moment
1 Obsessed over the black obsidian rock in the mechanism Found it under a rotating crank; wanted to take it Party chose to leave it behind due to time pressure; "I didn't get my rock" Attachment to objects she finds interesting, regardless of strategic value
1 Offered to take fire damage to help Lavender move the snake statues Lavender had fire immunity; Opal did not Took damage from the beams but helped move the statue successfully (rolled 21) Willingness to sacrifice personal safety for the team without hesitation

Character Arc

Arc Summary

Opal's arc is the simplest and most satisfying in the party. She entered the temple as a strong, enthusiastic, intellectually limited Barbarian, and she exited it having proven that heart and muscle can accomplish what brains and caution cannot. The session tested her in the cruelest way possible — blinding the character who relies most on physical engagement — and she responded by killing the thing that was killing her friends. While blinded. With a critical hit.

There is a quiet emotional layer underneath Opal's boisterous exterior. She mentioned her sister [[Pearl]] living happily in [[Barovia]], suggesting a family life she left behind. She accepted Lavender's hand-holding and bandana with genuine warmth. And her fixation on the obsidian rock, while played for comedy, suggests a character who forms attachments to things she finds beautiful or interesting, regardless of their utility. Opal is not complicated, but she is complete.

Arc Trajectory

  • Starting State (Session 1): Enthusiastic, physically dominant, eager for adventure.
  • Inciting Pressure: Going blind from the mirror beam (Nat 1, 14 radiant damage to eyes).
  • Current State (Session 1): Vision restored; proved herself as the party's most reliable combatant under pressure.
  • Unresolved Tension: Left the obsidian rock behind. Her sister is in [[Barovia]], a place with its own darkness.
  • Predicted Direction: Opal's straightforward morality may put her in direct conflict with Aramil when the Eye theft is discovered. She is the character most likely to react with immediate physical anger. [SPECULATIVE]

Backstory References

Detail Source Confidence Session Context
Sister named Pearl living in Barovia Player dialogue (in-character) [CONFIRMED] 1 "Her sister's name is Pearl and she still lives in Barovia. Just hanging out. She's living a very happy life, actually."
Goliath Barbarian, physically massive Player description [CONFIRMED] 1 "She's a Goliath. Barbarian. She hewed and jacked."
Self-described as "kind of dumb" Player dialogue [CONFIRMED] 1 "She kind of, she was kind of dumb."
Saw a flier for Fizzleblast's expedition Player dialogue [CONFIRMED] 1 "I definitely saw a flier."

Notable Quotes

Quote Context Session Why It Matters
"I hate mirrors! Hold this mirror!" Raging to move a mirror after failing the first attempt 1 Perfectly captures her approach: anger at the obstacle, then direct engagement
"Opal. Oaf, Opal, same, same." Fizzleblast wrote down "Oaf" instead of her name 1 Self-aware about how others perceive her; unbothered by it
"Her sister's name is Pearl and she still lives in Barovia. Just hanging out. She's living a very happy life, actually." Character introduction 1 The sweetest line in the session; reveals a genuine emotional life beneath the muscle
"Do we smell dinosaurs?" First moment after landing in Chult 1 Pure Opal: immediate, sensory, enthusiastic
"Goliath feel calm or angry." When Lavender said she would be sad if she were genuinely blind 1 Reduces her emotional range to its essential elements
"I didn't get my rock." After the escape, reflecting on what was left behind 1 Reveals her attachments; the rock mattered more to her than any magic item
"I don't know what that does but cool." Receiving the Eldritch Claw Tattoo 1 Accepts gifts with enthusiasm, understands them later (or never)

Session Timeline

- Raged to move a mirror; helped redirect the beam with Bob's assistance
- Went blind from a Nat 1 on a mirror check (14 radiant damage to eyes)
- Killed the Giant Constrictor Snake blind with a critical hit Great Axe attack (30 damage)
- Received the [[Belt of Stone Giant Strength]] (Strength 23) and [[Bloodaxe]] from Bob's looting
- Helped Lavender move snake statues despite taking fire damage from the beams
- Obsessed over a black obsidian rock in the mechanism; left it behind due to time constraints
- Pulled the escape ropes with the party (rolled 19 with Guidance)

Lavender Villani

"If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church."

Overview

Lavender Villani is a Tiefling Warlock who did not grow up magical — she became one after finding a locket containing a genie named [[Jezebel]] and making a deal: warlock powers in exchange for eventually freeing the genie. She has jewels on her horns, dresses in bright, excited fashion, and radiates an optimism that seems wildly misplaced in a death-trap Chultan temple. Her familiar, an imp named [[Tika]] with a New Jersey accent, shapeshifts between raven and rat forms and serves as Lavender's eyes, hands, and comic relief in places she cannot reach.

Lavender is the session's MVP (weighted score 8.4), and for good reason. She found the [[Zephyr Reliquary]] granting fire immunity, which allowed her to walk unscathed through the mirror beams that blinded Opal and damaged everyone else. She used Gust of Wind to disperse the poison gas that dealt 14 damage to the entire party. She deployed [[Tika]] to scout the rival party's plans, locate loot in high places, and scout the T-Rex room. She used Hellish Rebuke to kill two baboons at once when their fire damage spread in the overheated temple. And her Eldritch Blast, while not always accurate (she rolled a Nat 1 that hit Moor for 5 damage), was consistent enough to matter.

What separates Lavender from a purely tactical character is her warmth. She gave Opal a bandana when the Barbarian went blind and held her hand through the temple. She gave items she found to both Kyles rather than hoarding them. She was the first to suggest retreating into her genie vessel to protect the party from the gas. She even offered to sacrifice her [[Zephyr Reliquary]] by breaking it to stop the temple's rising heat. Lavender is sarcastic, practical, and genuinely kind — a combination that made her both the session's most effective character and its emotional glue.

Appearance

  • Physical Build & Features: Tiefling with horns adorned with jewels. High Strength (noted by DM and party as surprising for a Warlock).
  • Clothing & Armor: Dressed "very excited and happy." Carries a locket with [[Jezebel]] inside. No armor (relies on Bracers of Defense and shield for AC 16+).
  • Distinguishing Details: Jeweled horns are the most visually distinctive feature. [[Fizzleblast]] hung his cashmere sun hat on them.
  • How Others See Them: Approachable and friendly. [[Fizzleblast]] commented on her horns as convenient hat hooks. Opal was immediately drawn to her warmth.

[!note] Appearance Confidence

Horns with jewels and cheerful attire are confirmed from player description. Specific skin color, tail details, and other Tiefling features not described. ⚠️ Standard Tiefling features inferred.

Personality

Demonstrated Traits

Trait Evidence Session
Sarcastic wit delivered with a smile "If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." "There's so much cashmere in this jungle!" The Quote of the Session was a throwaway joke that perfectly captured her humor. 1
Practically generous Gave items she found to both Kyles. Offered her shield to Opal. Suggested using her genie vessel to shelter the party from poison gas. Was willing to break her Zephyr Reliquary to stop the heat. 1
Emotionally nurturing despite the chaos Gave Opal a bandana when she went blind. Held Opal's hand through the temple. "Booped" Opal on the head. "I come over and I give you another very pretty bandana to put over your eyes." 1
Tactically creative — uses all tools available Deployed Tika for scouting, looting, and intelligence gathering. Used Gust of Wind to disperse gas. Used Voice of the Chain Master to communicate through Tika. Used Hellish Rebuke defensively to kill two baboons at once. 1
Self-deprecating about game mechanics "I don't know how to play D&D." "I'm not going to know what anything is." Openly struggled with the digital character sheet and spell interactions. 1
Loyal to her patron and familiar "If she says do it, I'm going to do it. She's my butt." Referred to Jezebel by name, treated Tika as a friend, spent 10 minutes to resummon Tika after he was killed. 1

Speech Patterns & Voice

  • Vocabulary: Casual, modern, with pop culture references ("Taylor Swift's reputation," "it's Taylor's version"). Uses contemporary slang naturally in a fantasy setting.
  • Dialogue Style: Quick reactions, often finishing others' sentences or adding commentary. Thinks out loud about spell mechanics.
  • Humor: References that deliberately break the fourth wall of the setting. Cultural callbacks delivered deadpan.
  • Under Stress: More focused and decisive. When the gas hit: "Can we get out of here?" When debating the escape: "We need to leave. We're gonna leave. I wanna leave. I wanna go home."

Decision-Making Pattern

Lavender makes decisions by weighing the group's needs against her resources. She was willing to sacrifice her Zephyr Reliquary (breaking it to disperse cold air) if the party needed it, but held off when a better solution presented itself. She gave loot away when others could use it better. She used her most powerful abilities (genie vessel, Tika's scouting, Gust of Wind) at the moments they would have the most impact. She is not selfless to the point of martyrdom — she kept the Zephyr Reliquary and the Rod of the Pact Keeper because they made her more effective — but her default orientation is toward the group.

Abilities & Combat Style

Observed Abilities

Ability / Spell / Feature Type Observed Effect Session Notes
Eldritch Blast Cantrip 9 damage against the first Gorallon (7 force + 2 thunder from Genie bonus). Rolled Nat 1 against baboons, hitting Moor for 5 force damage instead. 1 Primary attack; Genie patron adds thunder damage
Hellish Rebuke 1st Level Spell (Reaction) Used when bitten by a baboon; 2d10 fire damage killed the baboon, and the fire spread to kill an adjacent baboon in the overheated temple 1 Reaction-based; devastating in the overheated environment
Gust of Wind 2nd Level Spell Dispersed the noxious gas cloud that dealt 14 damage to the party. Used again to disperse gas during the snake statue sequence. 1 "The gust disperses gas" — critical utility spell
Voice of the Chain Master Warlock Invocation Spoke through Tika to communicate while the imp scouted remotely; perceived through Tika's senses 1 Used to scout the rival party and high shelves
Bottled Respite (Genie's Vessel) Class Feature Offered to shelter the entire party inside her genie vessel (locket) for 10 minutes during the gas attack; ultimately held in reserve 1 5 willing creatures, once per long rest
Investment of the Chain Master Warlock Invocation Used reaction to grant Tika resistance to the snake's bite damage 1 Reduced damage taken by familiar
Find Familiar (Ritual) Spell Resummoned Tika after the imp was killed by the Giant Constrictor Snake; 10-minute ritual casting 1 Ritual cast — no spell slot needed
Shield (via equipment) Equipment Used a shield for +2 AC; placed it on pressure plates 1 Dual utility

Combat Behavior Profile

  • Preferred Range: Ranged. Stays behind the front line, using Eldritch Blast and directing Tika.
  • Opening Move: Eldritch Blast from range, or deploy Tika for scouting/distraction.
  • Tactical Role: Utility and control. Gust of Wind, Tika scouting, Hellish Rebuke. More impactful through environmental manipulation than raw damage.
  • Risk Tolerance: Moderate. Willing to walk through fire beams (immune via Zephyr Reliquary) but preferred to let others handle physical confrontations. Used shield and positioning to minimize risk.
  • Signature Moments: Dispersing the poison gas with Gust of Wind; killing two baboons with Hellish Rebuke fire spread; walking through mirror beams unharmed while the party watched in awe; scouting the rival party through invisible Tika.
  • Weaknesses Observed: Rolled a Nat 1 on an Eldritch Blast, hitting Moor for 5 damage. Limited spell slots as a level 3 Warlock (2 slots total).

Equipment & Inventory

Weapons & Armor

Item Type Properties Acquired Notes
Shield Armor +2 AC Given by Moor Also used to weigh down pressure plates and shield Opal from beams

Notable Possessions

Item Description Significance
[[Bracers of Defense]] +2 AC while not wearing armor From loot explosion; combined with shield for AC 16+
[[Zephyr Reliquary]] Small jar with faint blue mist; grants immunity to fire damage Found by Tika on high shelf; was the temple's "AC unit." Removing it raised the temple's temperature.
[[Rod of the Pact Keeper]] +1 to Warlock spell save DC and attacks; can regain a spell slot Found in first loot pile; Aramil was jealous
Genie's Locket Contains [[Jezebel]], her Genie patron Her vessel for Bottled Respite; central to her backstory
[[Tika]] (Imp Familiar) Imp with shapeshifting (raven, rat), invisibility, flight Chain Pact familiar; New Jersey accent; killed and resummoned during the session

Consumables & Currency

  • Potions: None specifically noted.
  • Gold/Wealth: Not focused on personal enrichment. Gave items to party members.

Relationships

Party Members

Character Relationship Dynamic Key Moments Trajectory
[[Opal]] Closest bond in the party Nurturing and protective. Gave her a bandana, held her hand, "booped" her head. Genuine warmth. "I hold your hand." "I give you another very pretty bandana to put over your eyes." Worked together to move snake statues. Strengthening — mutual affection
[[Moor]] Puzzle-solving partner Respectful collaboration. Solved the pressure plate puzzle together, paired for the Man-and-Crocodile walk. Walked the pressure plates in synchronized steps; absorbed his heals gratefully; accidentally shot him with Eldritch Blast Stable — competent partnership
[[Aramil]] Fellow Warlock, mild rivalry Gave him items from her looting but competed over the Rod of the Pact Keeper. "Get your fucking rope" (Aramil, bitter about not getting the Rod); she gave him the Devotee's Censer and other items Complicated — does not know about the Eye theft
[[Bob]] Tactical ally Limited direct interaction but coordinated through group decisions. Both contributed to puzzle solving and escape planning Stable — cordial

NPCs

NPC Relationship Disposition Key Interactions
[[Fizzleblast]] Employer Amused by his eccentricity Let him hang his hat on her horns; "There's so much cashmere in this jungle!"
[[Jezebel]] Genie Patron Loyal, contractual bond Made a deal to free Jezebel; follows her guidance ("If she says do it, I'm going to do it")
[[Tika]] Imp Familiar Affectionate, partner-like Treated Tika as a friend; spent 10 minutes to resummon him after death; used Voice of the Chain Master constantly

Factions

Faction Relationship Status
[[Charisma Company]] Hired adventurer Active

Key Decisions

Session Decision Context Consequence What It Reveals
1 Removed the Zephyr Reliquary despite knowing it would raise the temple's temperature Jezebel directed her to it; it was acting as the temple's "AC unit" Temple temperature rose, causing escalating fire damage to the party every 10 minutes Trusts her patron's guidance; willing to accept consequences for the group's long-term benefit
1 Offered to shelter the party in her genie vessel during the gas attack Party was taking 14 poison damage each; vessel could hold 5 creatures for 10 minutes Held in reserve for a worse emergency; the party used other solutions Conservative resource management — saves her best tool for the worst moment
1 Used Gust of Wind to disperse the poison gas Gas had already dealt 14 damage; the party was debating next steps Gas dispersed immediately; the party stopped taking damage Decisive when she sees the right moment; self-critical that she did not think of it sooner
1 Sent Tika to scout the rival party's plans Rival adventurers heard on the temple roof; the party needed intelligence Learned the rivals planned to break mirrors and wake the T-Rex; the party used this to plan their approach Uses all available tools; understands that information is as valuable as combat power
1 Gave the Staff of Healing to Moor and items to both Kyles Found multiple items through Tika's looting on high shelves Moor became far more effective as a healer; Aramil received items he needed Generosity as strategy — makes the team stronger by distributing resources optimally

Character Arc

Arc Summary

Lavender's arc is one of quiet competence becoming recognized. She entered the session as the new Warlock who "just recently" gained her powers, admitted she did not know how to play D&D, and seemed positioned as the party's least experienced member. By the end, she was the session's MVP — not through dramatic combat moments, but through consistent, creative problem-solving that prevented disasters and enabled victories.

Her relationship with [[Jezebel]] and [[Tika]] defines her character. She is a person who forms bonds and honors them. She promised to free Jezebel. She resummoned Tika within 10 minutes of his death. She held Opal's hand when the Barbarian was scared. Lavender's strength is not her Eldritch Blast; it is her ability to care about people and use that care as motivation to find solutions.

Arc Trajectory

  • Starting State (Session 1): New Warlock, unfamiliar with her powers, cheerfully optimistic.
  • Inciting Pressure: The escalating crises (gas, heat, blindness, combat) forced her to use every tool creatively.
  • Current State (Session 1): Validated as the party's most versatile and effective member. Session MVP.
  • Unresolved Tension: Her deal with Jezebel — she promised to free the genie. What does that cost? And how does a character who values bonds react to Aramil's betrayal of the group's trust?
  • Predicted Direction: Lavender's loyalty to her companions and her patron will be tested if the consequences of the Eye theft unfold. She may be the moral voice that challenges Aramil while Moor delivers judgment. [SPECULATIVE]

Backstory References

Detail Source Confidence Session Context

Made a deal with Jezebel: warlock powers in exchange for freeing the genie Player dialogue (in-character) [CONFIRMED] 1 "She made a deal with the genie that she would turn the genie free at the end."
Wished to be a Warlock Player dialogue (in-character) [CONFIRMED] 1 "I wish to be a warlock."

Notable Quotes

Quote Context Session Why It Matters
"If the men find out we can shapeshift, they're going to tell the church." Discussing Tika's shapeshifting abilities 1 Quote of the Session; captures her humor and the party's absurdist energy
"There's so much cashmere in this jungle!" Reacting to Fizzleblast's cashmere robe 1 First sign of her observational wit
"If she says do it, I'm going to do it. She's my butt." Asked whether to take the Zephyr Reliquary against Jezebel's guidance 1 Reveals the depth of her loyalty to her patron
"I don't know how to play D&D." During the loot explosion mechanic explanation 1 Endearing self-deprecation that made her MVP performance all the more impressive
"We need to leave. We're gonna leave. I wanna leave. I wanna go home." During the poison gas crisis, urging the party to move 1 Under stress, she becomes the voice of decisive action
"I come over and I give you another very pretty bandana to put over your eyes." Opal was blind and distressed 1 The session's warmest moment; shows her instinct to comfort
"Sorry I didn't do that before we all lost a lot of health." After casting Gust of Wind to disperse the poison gas 1 Self-critical; holds herself to a high standard even when no one else blames her

Session Timeline

Session Key Events for This Character
Session 1 - Received [[Bracers of Defense]] (+2 AC) from the loot explosion
- Paired with Moor for the Man-and-Crocodile pressure plate walk
- Found the [[Rod of the Pact Keeper]] in the first loot pile
- Deployed Tika to scout high shelves; found the [[Zephyr Reliquary]] granting fire immunity
- Used Gust of Wind to disperse poison gas that dealt 14 damage to the party
- Used Hellish Rebuke to kill two baboons when fire spread in the overheated temple
- Gave Opal a bandana and held her hand while the Barbarian was blind
- Walked through mirror beams unharmed (fire immune) to move snake statues with Opal
- Sent invisible Tika to scout the rival party; learned their plan to break mirrors and wake the T-Rex
- Named Session MVP with a weighted score of 8.4

Last updated after Session 1 (One-shot). Profile compiled from Session 1.

Bob

type: pc

name: "Bob"

aliases:

  • "The Night"

player: "Josh"

race: "Halfling"

class: "Rogue"

subclass: "Thief"

level: "3"

alignment: "UNKNOWN (implied Chaotic Neutral)"

status: "Active"

sessions_appeared: [1]

last_updated: "Session 1"

tags:

  • pc
  • halfling
  • rogue
  • thief

Bob

"I'm just here for the loot, baby."

Overview

Bob is very short. He wears a backpack festooned with gear and a hood so deep that you can only see the shine in his eyes. Occasionally, a nose or a mustache becomes visible — "which may or may not have changed a couple times already." Whether the facial features are in the right place is an open question. He is a Halfling Rogue of the Thief archetype, and his name is just Bob. Not short for anything. Just Bob. When the DM asked if his parents ran out of room on the birth certificate, he confirmed: Bob.

Bob is the party's loot-focused pragmatist. While others debated puzzles and read murals, Bob was calculating inventory slots, evaluating items, and planning the most efficient path to the treasure. "I'm just here for the loot, baby" was his opening statement, and he never deviated from it. He searched for and found the party's most powerful items — the [[Cloak of the Bat]] for himself, the [[Belt of Stone Giant Strength]] for Opal — and his instinct for acquisition made him the natural choice for the session's climactic heist.

That heist was the session's defining moment: Bob climbed the temple archway, hung by his legs over a sleeping T-Rex, sleight-of-handed the [[Golden Snake Idol]] into his bag, and flew out with his Cloak of the Bat — only to be hit by a rival party's Fireball mid-flight. He survived (half damage, 9 points), the T-Rex woke up, and the escape began. It was a sequence that required stealth (dirty 20, then 21), sleight of hand (14+8=22), and nerve. Bob had all three. He also had the sarcastic detachment to follow it up by crawling on the ceiling like Spider-Man while screaming to lure baboons into spike traps.

Appearance

  • Physical Build & Features: Very short (Halfling). Slight build.
  • Clothing & Armor: Backpack with lots of gear. Deep hood obscuring his face. Only the shine of his eyes is visible, with occasional glimpses of a nose or mustache.
  • Distinguishing Details: The nose and mustache "may or may not have changed a couple times already" and may not be "where we would expect it to be." This suggests disguise kit use or just deliberate ambiguity.
  • How Others See Them: Opal asked "What are you, Bob? Some kind of halfling robot?" Aramil compared him to a "black mage" due to the deep hood.

[!note] Appearance Confidence

Physical description is from player narration. The shifting facial features are deliberately mysterious.

Personality

Demonstrated Traits

Trait Evidence Session
Unapologetically loot-focused "I'm just here for the loot, baby." Immediately calculated inventory slots. Searched for items for himself and others. Planned the idol heist around inventory capacity. 1
Sarcastic with deadpan delivery "And how many squirtons in a gusher?" "Smells like bullshit." (Animal handling check for dinosaur smell.) "I'm married." (When asked to describe himself.) 1
Brave when the objective is clear Climbed the archway over a sleeping T-Rex alone, grabbed the idol, and flew out. Ran screaming down a hallway to lure baboons into traps. Jumped 36 feet across the pressure plates during the escape. 1
Generous with acquisitions for others Found the Belt of Stone Giant Strength for Opal, the Manual of Quickness of Action for himself, and the Boots of Striding and Springing. Offered items to party members. 1
Physically creative, uses environment Climbed walls with Spider Climbing slippers. Ran on the ceiling to evade baboons. Used his 36-foot jump (3x normal with Boots of Striding) to clear the pressure plate gauntlet. 1
Maintains a persona of mystery Deep hood, shifting facial features, minimal backstory. "Bob." Just Bob. The mystique is part of the joke. 1

Speech Patterns & Voice

  • Vocabulary: Casual, modern, sarcastic. Uses quips and one-liners.
  • Dialogue Style: Short bursts of humor interspersed with quiet competence. Does not dominate conversations but delivers the best punchlines.
  • Humor: Deadpan absurdism. The "squirtons in a gusher" line, pretending to read his own name off his hand, claiming to be married.
  • Under Stress: Gets quieter and more focused. During the idol heist, he stopped joking and executed.

Decision-Making Pattern

Bob makes decisions based on a clear cost-benefit analysis weighted toward loot and survival. He volunteered for the idol heist because he had the right skills (climbing speed, stealth, sleight of hand) and the right equipment (Cloak of the Bat, then Boots of Striding). He screamed to lure baboons away because it was the tactically sound choice — his stealth let him evade them afterward. He is not reckless; he is calculated. The persona of chaos disguises careful planning.

Abilities & Combat Style

Observed Abilities

Ability / Spell / Feature Type Observed Effect Session Notes
Sneak Attack Class Feature Used with shortbow when Opal was within 5 feet of the snake; 8 damage (1d6 SA + weapon) 1 Requires advantage or adjacent ally
Cunning Action Class Feature Used for bonus action disengage, dash, and hide throughout the session 1 Core mobility tool
Second-Story Work Thief Feature Climbing speed equal to walking speed; used to climb the temple archway to reach the idol 1 Made the heist possible without flight
Halfling Lucky Racial Feature Rerolled a Nat 1 on a loot search roll; got 18 on the reroll, earning the [[Cloak of the Bat]] 1 "I am a halfling. I'm lucky."
Shortbow Weapon Primary ranged weapon; used for Sneak Attack against the snake (9+6=15 to hit) 1
Daggers Weapon Mentioned in inventory but not used in combat 1
Sleight of Hand Skill (+8 bonus) Swapped the Scales of Justice with oil flasks on the pressure plate (19 with Guidance); grabbed the Golden Snake Idol from the archway (22) 1 Party's best Sleight of Hand
Stealth Skill (+8 bonus) Dirty 20 sneaking to the archway; 21 flying out with the idol; 27 hiding on the ceiling from baboons 1 Consistently high rolls
Performance Skill Screamed to lure baboons (rolled 10 with advantage); marginally effective but bought time 1 Creative use of a non-combat skill
Dimension Door Via Cape of the Mountebank Available but not used; considered for the heist but ultimately unnecessary 1 Single use per day

Combat Behavior Profile

  • Preferred Range: Ranged, with shortbow. Gets into melee only when necessary.
  • Opening Move: Find a flanking position for Sneak Attack, or use stealth to gain advantage.
  • Tactical Role: Scout, infiltrator, and burst damage dealer. Best at single-target elimination and heist-type objectives.
  • Risk Tolerance: High when the objective justifies it. Climbed over a sleeping T-Rex, flew through a Fireball, crawled on ceilings past baboons.
  • Signature Moments: The idol heist (stealth, sleight of hand, Bat-cape flight, survived Fireball); ceiling-crawling Spider-Man escape from baboons; 36-foot jump across pressure plates.
  • Weaknesses Observed: Low hit points (29 max). Took 9 damage from the Fireball and 13 from the blade traps. Limited healing options.

Equipment & Inventory

Weapons & Armor

Item Type Properties Acquired Notes
Shortbow Ranged 1d6 piercing Starting equipment Primary weapon
Daggers Melee/Thrown 1d4 piercing, finesse Starting equipment Backup weapons

Notable Possessions

Item Description Significance
[[Cloak of the Bat]] Advantage on Dex checks; fly speed 40 in dim light (both hands on cloak required) Halfling Lucky reroll turned a Nat 1 into an 18; this item made the heist possible
[[Cape of the Mountebank]] Cast Dimension Door once per day; take one person within 500 feet Emergency escape tool; held in reserve
[[Boots of Striding and Springing]] 3x normal jump distance; speed not reduced by encumbrance or heavy armor Swapped from Slippers of Spider Climbing for the heist; 36-foot standing long jump
Slippers of Spider Climbing Walk on walls and ceilings From Loot Explosion; used for wall/ceiling movement; left on the archway when switching to Boots
[[Manual of Quickness of Action]] Permanently +2 to Dexterity Used immediately upon finding it
[[Golden Snake Idol]] The quest objective; golden, snake-shaped, heavy (10 inventory slots) Stolen from above the sleeping T-Rex; delivered to Fizzleblast

Consumables & Currency

  • Potions: None specifically noted.
  • Gold/Wealth: Focused on valuable items rather than raw currency.

Relationships

Party Members

Character Relationship Dynamic Key Moments Trajectory
[[Opal]] Comedic physical contrast The tiny Halfling and the massive Goliath. He stood between her legs to help move a mirror. He found her the Belt of Stone Giant Strength. "I'm literally in between your legs." Found loot specifically for her. Stable — odd-couple camaraderie
[[Moor]] Operational trust Moor enabled Bob's heist with the Light cantrip. Bob trusted Moor to keep the party alive while he took risks. Moor tossed the light rock; Bob executed the stealth mission Stable — mutual competence
[[Aramil]] Rope-tying buddies Worked together on the ropes. Bob consistently mangled Aramil's name ("Aramel," "Allendill," "Akinquil"). Paired for rope untying; held the swords together to keep the portcullis open Stable — collegial
[[Lavender Villani]] Indirect collaborator Limited direct interaction but complementary skills; her scouting informed his heist planning. Both contributed to puzzle solutions and escape coordination Stable — professional

NPCs

NPC Relationship Disposition Key Interactions
[[Fizzleblast]] Employer (and treasure delivery) Transactional Delivered the Golden Snake Idol; "I'm just here for the loot, baby"

Factions

Faction Relationship Status
[[Charisma Company]] Hired adventurer Active

Key Decisions

Session Decision Context Consequence What It Reveals
1 Searched for loot specifically for other party members (Opal) Given the option to search for self or others Found the Belt of Stone Giant Strength for Opal, massively boosting her effectiveness Despite the "just here for the loot" persona, he invests in the team's success
1 Ran screaming down a hallway to lure baboons away from the party Party needed to move the snake statues without baboon interference Baboons chased him; he escaped by running up a wall and hiding on the ceiling (stealth 27) Brave and resourceful; uses his body as bait because he trusts his escape abilities
1 Volunteered for the idol heist over a sleeping T-Rex Party debated who should retrieve the Golden Snake Idol Successfully climbed the archway, grabbed the idol, and flew out — triggering the climactic escape Steps up for the highest-stakes moment because he has the right tools
1 Swapped slippers for boots mid-heist Needed the Boots of Striding and Springing for the jump-escape instead of Spider Climbing Successfully jumped 36 feet across the pressure plates during the escape Adaptive equipment management; understands when to change tools

Character Arc

Arc Summary

Bob's arc is deceptively simple: the Rogue who says he is only there for the loot proves he is there for the team. His opening line — "I'm just here for the loot, baby" — established a persona of mercenary self-interest. But his actions throughout the session told a different story. He searched for loot for Opal. He ran screaming through corridors to draw enemies away from the party. He volunteered for the most dangerous job in the session. And he executed it flawlessly.

The idol heist is Bob's arc in miniature. He assessed the situation, identified himself as the best candidate, and acted. The Fireball that hit him on the way out was the universe testing his commitment, and he passed (literally — half damage, 24 Dex save). Bob's character is built on the gap between what he says (selfish, mercenary, "just Bob") and what he does (generous, brave, clutch).

Arc Trajectory

  • Starting State (Session 1): Self-proclaimed mercenary; "just here for the loot."
  • Inciting Pressure: The idol heist required someone to risk everything for the party's objective.
  • Current State (Session 1): The hero of the heist. Proved that his skills are matched by his nerve.
  • Unresolved Tension: He claims to be married. Is this a joke or a backstory thread? His identity is deliberately obscured.
  • Predicted Direction: Bob's mysterious background (shifting facial features, minimal backstory, just "Bob") suggests there is more to discover. [SPECULATIVE]

Backstory References

Detail Source Confidence Session Context
Name is just "Bob" Player dialogue, confirmed repeatedly [CONFIRMED] 1 DM: "Just Bob? Your parents weren't out of room on the birth certificate?"
Claims to be married Player dialogue [RUMORED] 1 "I'm, uh... Married." — delivered as possible joke during character introduction
Facial features may shift Player description [CONFIRMED] 1 Nose and mustache "may or may not have changed a couple times already"

Notable Quotes

Quote Context Session Why It Matters

"You're grabbing the mirror from the top because you're tall and I'm underneath, holding the bottom part." Explaining how he and Opal are moving a mirror together 1 The physical comedy of the Halfling-Goliath partnership
"That's right. You better run from Bob." After the Gorallon fled 1 Taking credit for Aramil's intimidation; pure bravado
"Who pissed me off the least?" Discussing who to save with his Cape of the Mountebank's Dimension Door 1 The mercenary persona, maintained even when he has no intention of abandoning anyone

Session Timeline

- Helped Opal move a mirror (standing between her legs for comedic effect)
- Found the [[Cloak of the Bat]] (Halfling Lucky reroll: Nat 1 to 18)

- Climbed the archway, stole the [[Golden Snake Idol]] from above the sleeping T-Rex (stealth 20, sleight of hand 22)
- Flew out with the Cloak of the Bat; survived a Fireball (24 Dex save, 9 half damage)
- Jumped 36 feet across pressure plates using Boots of Striding and Springing during the escape

THREAT ASSESSMENT: The Rival Party (Blackwater)
THREAT

---

> Classification: High | Status: Unknown (Presumed Dead) | Last Known Activity: Session 1

> “They'll definitely kill you guys.” -- [[Fizzleblast]], with characteristic understatement

Executive Summary
THREAT

The Rival Party -- referred to by Fizzleblast as “Red Fangs, Black Skulls, something edgy” -- was a competing adventuring team that followed [[Charisma Company]] to the Chultan temple after Fizzleblast accidentally posted expedition details on a public forum. They arrived approximately one hour behind the party and positioned themselves on the temple's upper ridgeline to observe and wait. Their strategy was classic vulture tactics: let the party do the dangerous work of clearing traps and solving puzzles, then steal the artifact during extraction. [CONFIRMED]

The rival party included at least two spellcasters capable of Fireball and Dimension Door -- significant magical resources for a temple heist. They launched a Fireball at [[Bob]] as he flew from the archway with the [[Golden Snake Idol]], narrowly missing him but waking the [[Tyrannosaur]] and triggering the climactic escape. Two members appeared via Dimension Door between the party and the [[Fizzle Field]] safety perimeter, but were routed by [[Aramil]]'s Phantasmal Force (the target rolled a natural 1 on the Intelligence save). [CONFIRMED]

The critical unknown: their ultimate fate. They fled into the Chultan jungle at night, moments before the undead rose and the darkness became permanent. Screams were heard. Whether any survived is undetermined. [UNKNOWN]

Known Abilities
THREAT

| Ability | Type | Observed Effect | Threat Level | Session | Notes |

|---------|------|-----------------|-------------|---------|-------|

| Fireball | Offensive Magic | Launched from ridgeline; hit the archway and the T-Rex. Bob took 9 half damage (Dex save 24). Full blast woke the Tyrannosaur. | CRITICAL | 1 | At least one caster with 3rd-level slots. |

| Dimension Door | Movement | Two members appeared between the party and the safety bubble -- teleported from the ridgeline to the beach. | HIGH | 1 | At least one caster with 4th-level slots or scrolls. |

| Tactical Positioning | Strategic | Positioned on the ridgeline above the temple, observing the party's progress without engaging. Waited for the optimal ambush moment. | HIGH | 1 | Disciplined and patient -- not impulsive. |

Tactical Profile
THREAT
Fighting StyleVulture tactics. They did not engage the temple's traps or inhabitants. They waited for the party to clear the path, then struck during extraction. This is a team built for ambush, not dungeon crawling. [CONFIRMED]
OpeningLong-range magical strike (Fireball) from an elevated, concealed position. [CONFIRMED]
When LosingFled into the jungle when Phantasmal Force broke their nerve. The target rolled a Nat 1 on the save and panicked, grabbing his companion and running. [CONFIRMED]
Estimated Power LevelComparable to or slightly above the party. Multiple 3rd- and 4th-level casters suggest a well-funded, professional operation. [ASSESSED]
Known Weaknesses
THREAT

| Weakness | Evidence | Confidence |

|----------|----------|------------|

| Morale collapse under magical fear | One member fled from Phantasmal Force (Nat 1 save), dragging an ally with him | HIGH |

| Dependent on the party's success | Their entire strategy required the party to solve the temple first | CONFIRMED |

Intelligence Gaps
THREAT
[ ] Total team size -- only two members were directly encountered
[ ] Identity of their employer/buyer
[ ] Whether any survived the undead rising in the jungle
[ ] Their original base of operations and organizational affiliation
[ ] Whether they had additional teams or resources in reserve

Port Nyanzaru

> A city of dinosaur races, merchant princes, and the persistent smell of jungle rot and exotic spices -- now plunged into a darkness that may never lift.

Overview
LORE

Port Nyanzaru is the primary civilized settlement on the Chultan peninsula and the staging ground for [[Fizzleblast]]'s expedition. The party departed from its marina in rented canoes and would presumably return there after the mission. [CONFIRMED] The city is governed by Merchant Princes who control trade, resources, and policy. [EXTRAPOLATED from D&D canon]

The city features a Grand Souk marketplace, temples to multiple deities (including [[Tyr]], Savras, Waukeen, and [[Ubtao]]), a harbor district, and a marina where Fizzleblast rented his canoes (Slip 7). [CONFIRMED -- Fizzleblast's logistical details; EXTRAPOLATED for broader features]

Current Status
LORE

Following [[Aramil]]'s theft of the [[Eye of the Serpent]], Port Nyanzaru is now in crisis. The sun has not risen. The undead are rising across the peninsula. Candle and oil prices have tripled. The temples are overwhelmed. The Flaming Fist garrison at Fort Beluarian has gone silent. [EXTRAPOLATED from session consequences]

---

> Black sand, screaming jungle, and a shimmering protective bubble where a gnome slept through the apocalypse.

Vivid Description
LORE

The landing site is a strip of black volcanic sand where the river meets the Chultan jungle interior. The canoes scraped against the shore as the party arrived, and the jungle screamed -- howler monkeys, cicadas, unseen things with too many limbs. The air carried weight: thick with humidity, rot, scale, and earth turned over by massive feet. [CONFIRMED -- DM narration]

The [[Fizzle Field]] is Fizzleblast's patented protective bubble -- a shimmering energy barrier that repels “organic matter, undead matter, and aggressive sales tactics.” [CANONICAL] It is translucent, allowing those inside to see out (Niles witnessed something traumatic through the barrier during the night). The bubble served as the party's extraction point and sole safe haven. [CONFIRMED]

Notable Features
LORE

| Feature | Description | Significance |

|---------|-------------|--------------|

| Fizzle Field Bubble | Shimmering protective barrier; patent pending | Only safe zone; undead could not penetrate; sound is muted inside |

| Black Sand Beach | Volcanic sand shoreline | Canoe landing point; loot explosion occurred here |

| Canoes (x2) | Rented from Port Nyanzaru Marina | 24-hour rental; must be returned by noon; Fizzleblast's deposit at risk |

Events
LORE

| Session | Event | Participants | Consequences |

|---------|-------|-------------|-------------|

| 1 | Arrival and briefing | Full party, Fizzleblast, Niles | Mission established; equipment distributed via Loot Explosion |

| 1 | Loot Explosion | Niles (catalyst), full party | Magical items randomly bound to each character via Potion of Instant Attunement |

| 1 | Final escape / extraction | Full party | Party reached the bubble; undead rose outside; rival party screams heard |

| 1 | Dawn fails to arrive | All | The sun does not rise; sky remains black; Niles found shaking on the floor |

```yaml
“[[Snake Sanctum]]”
“[[T-Rex Courtyard]]”

status: “Intact (traps partially deactivated)”

controlling_faction: “None (abandoned)”

sessions_featured: [1]

first_visited: “Session 1”

tags:

location
dungeon
temple
chult

```

> A solar-powered death trap built by a civilization that understood one truth: the world runs on sunlight, and the serpent holds the key.

Sub-Location Details
LORE

#### Temple Entrance / Heliographic Array

The entrance is flanked by heliographic thermal arrays -- mirrors angled to focus sunlight into lethal beams. Two [[Gorallons]] (four-armed jungle apes) paced at the edge of the light, their skin blistering where the beams caught them. [[Moor]] turned the first mirror to melt a Gorallon; [[Aramil]] intimidated the second into fleeing. [CONFIRMED]

#### Antechamber of Blades

A rectangular chamber thirty feet high, ceiling lost in shadow and cobwebs. Treasure heaped along the walls -- gold coins, weapons, trinkets, magical artifacts, interspersed with skeletons. Two rows of pressure plates run the length of the chamber, with residual blood spray marking where previous adventurers failed. A mural at the far end depicts a man and crocodile walking in lockstep with crossed swords overhead -- the key to the pressure plate puzzle. Two rusted greatswords hang above the main gate as the final activation mechanism. [CONFIRMED]

#### Prism Room / Crystalline Heart

The temple's mechanical core. A massive crystalline prism hangs from a central bar, splitting focused beams into spectral lances. Two enormous stone snake statues serve as reflectors. Four unlit gems crown the far portcullis -- all four must be lit simultaneously to open the way. The [[Giant Constrictor Snake]] emerged from one of the stone snake statues. Poison gas vents line the floor, triggered by a dead baboon falling on a pressure plate. [CONFIRMED]

#### Gas Chamber / Monkey Room

A section of the temple infested with feral baboons and accessible from the upper terrace. Noxious gas traps deal 14 poison damage to all creatures in the area. The baboons were drawn by the temple's rising heat after [[Lavender]] removed the [[Zephyr Reliquary]] (the temple's cooling mechanism). [CONFIRMED]

#### Snake Sanctum

The inner chambers containing treasure hoards, skeleton guardians (9 skeletons, 7 destroyed by Moor's Turn Undead), and the mechanisms for redirecting the snake statue reflectors. Additional loot found here included the [[Staff of Healing]], [[Belt of Hill Giant Strength]], [[Bloodaxe]], and [[Periapt of Wound Closure]]. [CONFIRMED]

#### T-Rex Courtyard

An open-air canyon at the temple's crown, enclosed by ridged stone walls. The jungle presses in from above -- crocodiles on the ridgeline, boa constrictors draped over ledges, gorallons watching. None descend because the [[Tyrannosaur]] sleeps in the center, bathing in the last concentrated warmth of the mirror arrays. The [[Golden Snake Idol]] hangs from an archway above the sleeping Rex. [CONFIRMED]

Atmosphere
LORE

> Hot stone. The taste of old copper and dry blood. Focused sunlight that burns rather than warms. The sound of mechanisms grinding in the walls -- gears that have been turning for centuries. A silence in the deepest chambers that is not emptiness but patience.

---

---

> “We Go Where You Won't!”

Leadership
LORE

| Name | Title/Role | Status |

|------|-----------|--------|

| [[Fizzleblast]] | Proprietor, Wizard | Active |

| [[Niles]] | Intern / Graduate Assistant | Active (Traumatized) |

Resources
LORE
Magical EquipmentFizzleblast provided a Bag of Holding containing multiple magical items and a Potion of Instant Attunement. The total value of distributed equipment significantly exceeded the 500gp retrieval fee. [CONFIRMED]
Fizzle Field TechnologyFizzleblast's patented protective barrier is his most significant asset -- a portable safe zone that repels organic matter, undead, and hostile entities. [CONFIRMED]
Financial ResourcesSufficient to rent canoes, outfit an expedition, and distribute magical equipment. Not extravagant -- he is concerned about canoe deposits. [CONFIRMED]
IntelligenceFizzleblast identified the temple, researched its defenses (heliographic arrays), and determined the artifact's general location. His intelligence was incomplete but actionable. [CONFIRMED]
Relationship with Party
LORE

| PC | Standing | Basis |

|----|---------|-------|

| [[Moor]] | Neutral | Professional. Answered roll call, completed mission. |

| [[Opal]] | Amused tolerance | Fizzleblast wrote “Oaf” instead of “Opal.” |

| [[Aramil]] | Hostile (if discovered) | Aramil stole the Eye of the Serpent from Fizzleblast's artifact. |

| [[Lavender]] | Warm | Let him hang his hat on her horns. Complimentary about cashmere. |

| [[Bob]] | Transactional | Delivered the idol. Minimal personal interaction. |

Open Questions
LORE
[ ] Is Charisma Company a legitimate business or a front for something else?
[ ] Has Fizzleblast noticed the missing Eye from the idol?
[ ] What does he intend to do with the Golden Snake Idol?
[ ] How will he respond to the eternal darkness -- with investigation, panic, or profit?
[ ] Were there previous Charisma Company expeditions, and what happened to those teams?

---

Golden Snake Idol
ITEM

---

```yaml

type: item

name: “Golden Snake Idol”

item_type: “Quest Object / Artifact”

rarity: “Legendary”

magical: true

current_owner: “[[Fizzleblast]]”

location_found: “[[T-Rex Courtyard]]”

session_acquired: “Session 1”

status: “In Fizzleblast's Possession (Missing One Eye)”

tags:

item
quest-object
artifact
cursed

```

> A golden serpent coiled around a sun disk, its jeweled eyes catching the last light of a dying sun -- and one hand, quick and faithless, reached for what should never have been taken.

Physical DescriptionSolid gold, snake-coiled around a sun disk. Heavy (10+ inventory slots). Two jeweled eyes -- one of which has been secretly removed by [[Aramil]]. Warm from the focused sunlight. Hung from an archway above the sleeping [[Tyrannosaur]]. [CONFIRMED]
PropertiesThe idol is the temple's anchor -- the binding that connects the solar mechanism to whatever divine or arcane principle sustains the sun's relationship with the temple (and possibly the world). With both eyes intact, the sun rises. With one eye removed, the sun forgets. [CONFIRMED -- inscription + observed consequence]
Current StatusDelivered to Fizzleblast. Missing the right eye (the [[Eye of the Serpent]]). Fizzleblast has noted: “Why does the idol feel lighter?” [EXTRAPOLATED]

---

Eye of the Serpent
ITEM

---

```yaml

type: item

name: “Eye of the Serpent”

item_type: “Artifact Component / Cursed Gem”

rarity: “Legendary”

magical: true

current_owner: “[[Aramil]]”

location_found: “[[Golden Snake Idol]]”

session_acquired: “Session 1”

status: “In Aramil's Possession (Secret)”

tags:

item
artifact
cursed
plot-critical

```

> Warm as a coal that does not burn. Worth five thousand gold. Worth, potentially, everything.

Physical DescriptionA gem the size of a quail's egg, possibly chrysoberyl or alexandrite variant. Warm -- warmer than body temperature, warmer than the jungle. [CONFIRMED]
PropertiesThe Eye is the functional heart of the [[Golden Snake Idol]]. Its removal severed the connection between the idol and the sun, plunging all of [[Faerun]] into permanent darkness and causing the undead to rise. [CONFIRMED]
Estimated Value~5,000 gold (per Aramil's assessment), though its true value is incalculable. [CONFIRMED]
Current StatusHidden in Aramil's breast pocket, next to his purple crystal necklace. No one in the party knows. [CONFIRMED]

SignificanceThe single most impactful item in the session. Without it, Moor could not have sustained the 148 HP of total healing that kept the party alive. [CONFIRMED]

Properties (Demonstrated)Advantage on Stealth (Dexterity) checks. Fly speed 40 ft in dim light or darkness (both hands must hold the cloak). [CONFIRMED]
AcquisitionBob rolled a Nat 1 on his loot search, invoked Halfling Lucky, rerolled to 18, and got the Cloak. “I am a halfling. I'm lucky.” [CONFIRMED]
SignificanceMade the Golden Snake Idol heist possible. Bob flew from the archway after stealing the idol, surviving the rival party's Fireball mid-flight. [CONFIRMED]

Properties (Stated)Set of scales on a pressure plate pedestal. Allows one full die re-roll per long rest for anyone in the party. Requires Lawful Good alignment. [CONFIRMED]
Acquisition[[Moor]] grabbed it (“No, I grab it”) while [[Bob]] performed a sleight-of-hand swap (rolled 19 with Guidance) to replace it with oil flasks on the pressure plate. [CONFIRMED]
SignificanceA Lawful Good item claimed by the party's Lawful Good cleric of [[Tyr]], in a session where another party member committed an act of world-ending selfishness. The thematic irony is profound. [EXTRAPOLATED]

---

Zephyr Reliquary
ITEM

```yaml

type: item

magical: true

current_owner: “[[Lavender]]”

location_found: “[[Prism Room]] (high shelf)”

session_acquired: “Session 1”

status: “In Party Possession”

tags:

item
reliquary
fire-immunity

```

Properties (Demonstrated)Small jar with faint blue mist. Grants immunity to fire damage. Was functioning as the temple's “air conditioning” -- removing it caused the temple's internal temperature to rise, attracting baboons and increasing environmental fire damage. [CONFIRMED]
SignificanceLavender's MVP performance was built on this item. Fire immunity allowed her to walk through the mirror beams unharmed, move snake statues in areas of concentrated heat, and generally operate in zones lethal to everyone else. [CONFIRMED]
