# 15. Event Timeline

---

```yaml

type: timeline

title: “Charisma Company: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip -- Event Timeline”

campaign: “Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip”

sessions_covered: “Session 1 (One-shot)”

last_updated: “Session 1”

tags:

timeline
master-reference
chronology

```

> A chronological record of significant events from Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip, compiled from the session transcript.

---

Session 1 -- Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip
LORE

Event 1.1: Arrival on the Chultan Shore

TypeTravel
Location[[The Shore / Fizzle Field]]
What HappenedThe party arrived by canoe on the black sand shore of the Chultan jungle. [[Fizzleblast]] delivered a hurried briefing: retrieve the golden snake artifact from the temple, keep all other loot, watch out for rivals one hour behind them. [[Niles]] tripped on a crab and flung the Bag of Holding, shattering the Potion of Instant Attunement and randomly binding magical equipment to each party member.
Participants[[Moor]], [[Opal]], [[Aramil]], [[Lavender]], [[Bob]], [[Fizzleblast]], [[Niles]]
ConsequencesEach PC received random magical equipment. Fizzleblast retreated to the [[Fizzle Field]] bubble. The clock started.

Event 1.2: Gorallon Encounter

TypeCombat / Social
Location[[Temple Entrance]]
What HappenedTwo [[Gorallons]] blocked the temple entrance. [[Moor]] redirected a thermal mirror beam to melt the first Gorallon. [[Aramil]] used Speak with Animals to intimidate the second into fleeing (“Think about your wife and kids”).
Participants[[Moor]], [[Aramil]]
ConsequencesTemple entrance cleared. Moor established the mirror mechanic as a combat tool. Aramil demonstrated his negotiation-first approach.

Event 1.3: The Antechamber of Blades

TypeDiscovery
Location[[Antechamber of Blades]]
What HappenedThe party solved the Man-and-Crocodile pressure plate puzzle. [[Moor]] and [[Lavender]] walked the plates in synchronized steps. [[Bob]] bypassed the plates entirely by crawling on the ceiling with Slippers of Spider Climbing. The [[Scales of Justice]] were retrieved via Bob's sleight-of-hand swap.
ParticipantsFull party
ConsequencesInner temple accessed. Scales of Justice claimed by Moor (Lawful Good alignment requirement noted).

Event 1.4: Opal's Blinding

TypeLoss
Location[[Prism Room]]
What Happened[[Opal]] attempted to push a mirror and rolled a natural 1. She fell into the focused beam, taking 14 radiant damage directly to her eyes. She went blind. [[Lavender]] tied a bandana over her eyes. [[Moor]] determined the blindness would pass with time or healing magic.
Participants[[Opal]], [[Lavender]], [[Moor]]
ConsequencesOpal fought the remainder of the Prism Room encounter blind. This set up the session's most dramatic combat moment.

Event 1.5: Poison Gas Attack

TypeCombat (Environmental)
Location[[Prism Room]]
What HappenedA dead baboon fell onto a pressure plate, triggering noxious gas vents. The entire party took 14 poison damage. [[Moor]] used the [[Staff of Healing]] for emergency healing. [[Lavender]] cast Gust of Wind to disperse the gas.
ParticipantsFull party
ConsequencesParty resources strained significantly. Moor began his 148 HP healing marathon. Lavender's utility casting established her MVP trajectory.

Event 1.6: The Giant Constrictor Snake

TypeCombat
Location[[Prism Room]]
What HappenedA forty-foot Giant Constrictor Snake emerged from a stone snake statue. [[Aramil]] attempted to persuade it to eat monkeys instead -- failed. The snake killed [[Tika]] (Lavender's familiar) and constricted Aramil for 22 damage, dropping him to -10 HP. [[Moor]] healed Aramil. [[Opal]], blind, killed the snake with a critical hit greataxe strike for 30 damage.
ParticipantsFull party
ConsequencesAramil nearly died. Tika was killed (resummoned later). Opal's blind kill became the session's signature combat moment.
Notable DetailsOpal heard the snake's scales and felt the vibration through the stone. She located it through sound alone. The critical hit sheared through multiple coils.

Event 1.7: Temple Puzzle Completion

TypeDiscovery
Location[[Prism Room]] / [[Snake Sanctum]]
What Happened[[Lavender]] (fire immune via [[Zephyr Reliquary]]) walked through the beams and helped [[Opal]] (vision restored by Moor's Lesser Restoration) redirect the snake statues to light all four gems. The portcullis opened. Moor's Turn Undead destroyed 7 of 9 skeletons behind a blocked door.
Participants[[Lavender]], [[Opal]], [[Moor]]
ConsequencesAccess to the T-Rex Courtyard. Second wave of loot acquired.

Event 1.8: The Idol Heist

TypeDiscovery / Turning Point
Location[[T-Rex Courtyard]]
What Happened[[Bob]] climbed the archway above the sleeping [[Tyrannosaur]], stole the [[Golden Snake Idol]] (stealth 20, then 21; sleight of hand 22), and flew out using the [[Cloak of the Bat]]. The rival party launched a Fireball that missed Bob (half damage, 9 HP) but woke the T-Rex.
Participants[[Bob]] (primary), [[Moor]] (Light cantrip support)
ConsequencesIdol secured. T-Rex awakened. Escape sequence triggered. The session's climactic moment.

Event 1.9: The Escape

TypeCombat / Travel
LocationEntire temple, then [[The Shore]]
What Happened4 minutes 20 seconds to reach the [[Fizzle Field]]. [[Aramil]] athletics-checked through the closing door. [[Lavender]] vaulted the gas trap. [[Bob]] jumped 36 feet across pressure plates. [[Opal]] and [[Moor]] hauled counterweight swords. Two party members took 13 damage each from blade traps. Two rival mages appeared via Dimension Door; [[Aramil]]'s Phantasmal Force (raptors) caused one to roll a Nat 1 and flee, dragging his ally. [[Moor]]'s Command (“Flee”) hit the other.
ParticipantsFull party vs. rival party
ConsequencesParty reached safety. Rivals fled into the jungle. Undead rose outside the bubble. Rival screams heard, then silence.

Event 1.10: Aramil's Theft of the Eye

TypeTurning Point
Location[[The Shore]]
What HappenedWhile the party rested and Fizzleblast slept, [[Aramil]] secretly pried the [[Eye of the Serpent]] from the [[Golden Snake Idol]] using his [[Gloves of Thievery]] (sleight of hand 18). No one saw.
Participants[[Aramil]] (alone)
ConsequencesThe sun did not rise. Eternal darkness fell on [[Faerun]]. The undead rose across the continent. No one in the party knows Aramil is responsible.

Event 1.11: Dawn Fails

TypeTurning Point / Loss
Location[[The Shore / Fizzle Field]]
What HappenedHours passed. The sky remained black. No stars. No sun. The darkness that pressed against the Fizzle Field was not the darkness of night -- it was the darkness of forever.
ParticipantsAll (affected globally)
ConsequencesThe world has fundamentally changed. The session's final image: Aramil sitting apart, holding the warm gem, saying nothing.

---

Chronological Summary
LORE

| # | Event | Session | Location | Type |

|---|-------|---------|----------|------|

| 1 | Arrival, briefing, Loot Explosion | S1 | The Shore | Travel / Discovery |

| 2 | Gorallon encounter -- mirror melt + intimidation | S1 | Temple Entrance | Combat / Social |

| 3 | Antechamber pressure plates solved | S1 | Antechamber of Blades | Discovery |

| 4 | Opal blinded by mirror beam (Nat 1) | S1 | Prism Room | Loss |

| 5 | Poison gas -- 14 damage to all; Gust of Wind dispersal | S1 | Prism Room | Combat |

| 6 | Giant Snake fight -- Aramil downed, Opal blind kill | S1 | Prism Room | Combat |

| 7 | Temple puzzles completed -- portcullis opens | S1 | Prism Room / Snake Sanctum | Discovery |

| 8 | Bob steals idol from above sleeping T-Rex | S1 | T-Rex Courtyard | Discovery / Turning Point |

| 9 | Escape -- rival ambush, Phantasmal Force, blade traps | S1 | Entire Temple / Shore | Combat / Travel |

| 10 | Aramil steals the Eye of the Serpent | S1 | The Shore | Turning Point |

| 11 | Dawn fails -- eternal darkness | S1 | Global | Turning Point / Loss |

Event Chains
LORE

Chain: The Idol Retrieval

Fizzleblast hires party -> Temple entered -> Puzzles solved -> Idol stolen by Bob -> Idol delivered to Fizzleblast -> COMPLETED

Chain: The Eye Theft

Aramil investigates murals -> Learns serpent-sun connection -> Acquires Gloves of Thievery -> Steals the Eye (sleight of hand 18) -> Sun fails to rise -> Eternal darkness -> ACTIVE -- consequences unfolding, party unaware

Chain: The Rival Party

Fizzleblast's accidental public posting -> Rivals follow one hour behind -> Rivals observe from ridgeline -> Fireball ambush during extraction -> Phantasmal Force rout -> Rivals flee into jungle -> Undead rise -> Screams -> UNKNOWN -- fate undetermined

Unresolved Events
LORE

| Event | Session | What's Unresolved | Potential Consequences |

|-------|---------|-------------------|----------------------|

| Aramil's theft of the Eye | 1 | No one knows. The world is in darkness because of one sleight of hand check. | When discovered: party fracture, moral judgment from Moor, potential quest to return the Eye |

| Fizzleblast's interrupted warning | 1 | “Do not press the--” was cut off. What was he about to say? | May relate to a trap or mechanism the party unknowingly triggered or avoided |

| Niles witnessed something | 1 | Found shaking on the floor of the bubble. What did he see through the translucent barrier? | Potential testimony about what happened during the night -- undead, the darkness, or something worse |

| Rival party's fate | 1 | Fled into jungle before undead rose. Screams heard. | Could return as undead, could have survived, could provide information about their unknown patron |

| The unknown buyer | 1 | Someone hired the rival party and has resources, knowledge, and intent | A loose end -- they did not get what they paid for and may try again |

---

At a Glance

---

Retrieve the Golden Snake Idol
QUEST

#### Quest Summary

| Field | Detail |

|-------|--------|

| Status | Completed |

| Type | Main Quest |

| Quest Giver | [[Fizzleblast]] |

| Completed In | Session 1 |

| Objective | Retrieve a golden, snake-shaped artifact from a Chultan jungle temple |

| Outcome | Idol retrieved by [[Bob]] and delivered to [[Fizzleblast]]. However, [[Aramil]] secretly removed one of the idol's eyes, causing catastrophic consequences. |

| Reward Received | 550 gp (credited, minus 770 gp in debits per Fizzleblast's invoice -- net: party owes 220 gp, forgiven in exchange for a five-star review). All temple loot retained by the party. |

| Duration | Session 1 (single session) |

#### Resolution Summary

The party entered the [[Temple of the Man and Crocodile]], solved its pressure plate puzzles, survived its mirror traps and poison gas, defeated a Giant Constrictor Snake, navigated past a sleeping Tyrannosaur, and extracted the idol through Bob's climactic heist -- climbing the archway, stealing the idol, and flying out with the Cloak of the Bat. They escaped the temple under time pressure, routed the rival party's ambush, and delivered the artifact to Fizzleblast.

The quest was mechanically completed. The idol was retrieved and delivered. But the consequences of Aramil's secret theft of the Eye transformed a successful retrieval into an apocalyptic event.

#### Consequences & Ripple Effects

The [[Golden Snake Idol]] is in Fizzleblast's possession, but missing one eye
The [[Eye of the Serpent]] is secretly in Aramil's possession
The sun has not risen -- eternal darkness has fallen on [[Faerun]]
The undead are rising across the Chultan peninsula
Port Nyanzaru is in crisis
Fizzleblast has noticed the idol “feels lighter” and is asking “Where is the other eye?”
The rival party's unknown patron did not receive what they paid for

---

Undo the Eternal Darkness
QUEST

#### Quest Overview

| Field | Detail |

|-------|--------|

| Status | Active (Implicit -- self-directed by consequences) |

| Type | Main Quest (World-Scale) |

| Quest Giver | Self-directed / Consequence of Aramil's actions |

| Given In | Session 1 -- triggered by the theft of the [[Eye of the Serpent]] |

| Objective | Return the [[Eye of the Serpent]] to the [[Golden Snake Idol]] and restore the sun |

| Motivation | The world is in darkness. The undead are rising. Civilization is collapsing. |

| Reward | The sun. Also, not dying in eternal darkness surrounded by undead. |

| Deadline | Unknown -- the longer the darkness persists, the more catastrophic the consequences |

| Difficulty Assessment | Extreme -- requires confronting Aramil's theft, returning to Chult, and placing the Eye back in the idol while navigating a now-darkened jungle full of risen undead and a very angry Tyrannosaur |

#### Objective Breakdown & Progress

[ ] Discover that the Eye of the Serpent is missing from the idol
[ ] Identify Aramil as the thief
[ ] Confront Aramil and recover the Eye
[ ] Return to the [[Temple of the Man and Crocodile]]
[ ] Navigate the temple without solar-powered mechanisms (mirrors no longer function in darkness)
[ ] Return the Eye to the [[Golden Snake Idol]]
[ ] Confirm the sun rises

#### Complications & Obstacles

| Complication | Description | Status |

|-------------|-------------|--------|

| No one knows Aramil stole the Eye | The theft was performed in secret (sleight of hand 18) | Active -- critical obstacle |

| The temple's mechanisms depend on sunlight | Without the sun, the mirrors, beams, and prism do not function -- the temple is a different place in darkness | Active |

| The jungle is now full of undead | The Chultan peninsula is experiencing a zombie apocalypse | Active |

| Aramil may resist returning the Eye | His motivation (paternal approval, professional reinstatement) may override his guilt | Active |

| The inscription warns “greed alone” cannot unbind what was taken | Returning the Eye may require an act of selflessness or sacrifice | Unknown |

#### Notes

This quest exists as the natural continuation of the one-shot. If the campaign extends into the teased “Tube of Annihilation” sequel, this is the central throughline: undo what Aramil did. The challenge is not merely logistical (get the Eye back to the temple) but moral (confront the person who did this and decide what justice looks like). For [[Moor]], as a cleric of [[Tyr]], this is a test of everything his faith demands. For [[Opal]], whose sister lives in [[Barovia]] -- a land that is now even darker -- this is personal. For [[Lavender]], who values bonds above all else, Aramil's betrayal is a violation of the trust she gave freely. And for [[Bob]], the pragmatist, the question is simpler: is Aramil still useful, and what is the fastest way to fix this?

---

Aramil's Reinstatement
LORE

| Field | Detail |

|-------|--------|

| Status | Active (Aramil is still pursuing this) |

| Driven By | [[Aramil]] |

| First Expressed | Session 1 -- “If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay” |

| Objective | Regain his position at the [[Royal Community College]] and earn his father's approval |

| Progress | Stole the [[Eye of the Serpent]], which he believes is valuable enough to warrant reinstatement |

| Obstacles | The Eye may be cursed. The world is ending. His father may still not care. |

| Connections | Directly conflicts with [[Undo the Eternal Darkness]] -- the Eye must be returned, but Aramil does not want to give it up |

Opal's Rock
LORE

| Field | Detail |

|-------|--------|

| Status | Abandoned (involuntarily) |

| Driven By | [[Opal]] |

| First Expressed | Session 1 -- “I go get it. I want it.” |

| Objective | Retrieve the black obsidian rock from the temple mechanism |

| Progress | Identified the rock; could not retrieve it due to time pressure |

| Obstacles | The rock is inside a temple in a jungle that is now dark and full of undead |

| Connections | Could serve as motivation for Opal to return to the temple (aligned with [[Undo the Eternal Darkness]]) |

Lavender's Promise to Jezebel
LORE

| Field | Detail |

|-------|--------|

| Status | Active (long-term) |

| Driven By | [[Lavender]] |

| First Expressed | Session 1 -- “She made a deal with the genie that she would turn the genie free at the end” |

| Objective | Free [[Jezebel]] from the locket |

| Progress | No progress during the session |

| Obstacles | The conditions for freeing Jezebel are undefined |

| Connections | Lavender's loyalty to Jezebel defines her character; this promise will test her when the cost becomes clear |

Bob's Identity
LORE

| Field | Detail |

|-------|--------|

| Status | Unknown |

| Driven By | [[Bob]] (involuntarily -- his past pursues him) |

| First Expressed | Session 1 -- shifting facial features, “just Bob,” minimal backstory |

| Objective | Unknown -- Bob's goals beyond loot are deliberately obscured |

| Progress | N/A |

| Obstacles | Bob himself -- he does not want this thread pulled |

| Connections | If the campaign continues, Bob's identity will become relevant when the party needs trust and transparency |

---

Immediate Priorities
LORE

1. Discover the connection between the missing Eye and the eternal darkness

2. Identify Aramil as the thief

3. Decide the party's response to Aramil's betrayal

4. Determine whether returning the Eye to the idol will restore the sun

Decisions Pending
LORE
Whether to confront Aramil or investigate independently
Whether to return to the temple or seek other solutions
Whether Fizzleblast is an ally or a liability in the restoration effort
How to navigate the Chultan jungle without sunlight
Leads Not Yet Followed
LORE
Fizzleblast has noticed the idol “feels lighter” -- he may independently discover the missing Eye
The temple inscription's warning about “greed alone” suggests a specific reversal condition
The Ubtao priest in Port Nyanzaru stated: “The serpent held the sun. Someone took the serpent's eye.”
The rival party's unknown patron remains at large and may have relevant knowledge

---

World Wiki current through Session 1 (One-shot). Compiled from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip session transcript.

THREAT
ITEM
QUEST
LORE
SCROLL TO ZOOM · DRAG TO PAN · CLICK NODE TO VIEW
Backstories

Character Backstories

8 Expanded Origins
Character
▼

No backstory available.

▼

▼

No backstory available.

No backstory available.

Cross
▼

No backstory available.

Thematic
▼
The Cost of Taking

The session's central act -- Aramil stealing the Eye of the Serpent -- is the extreme expression of a theme that runs through every character. Moor takes damage to test traps. Opal takes blindness to fight the snake. Lavender takes the Reliquary knowing it will raise the temperature. Bob takes the idol knowing it might wake the Rex. Everyone takes something, and every taking has a cost. Aramil simply took the thing whose cost was largest. - **Found Family Under Pressure:** Five strangers, assembled by a flyer and a gnome in cashmere, became a unit in the space of one afternoon. Lavender held Opal's hand. Moor healed Aramil from death. Bob found loot for people he had met hours ago. The speed with which they bonded makes Aramil's betrayal all the more devastating -- he stole from people who were already treating him like family. ### Narrative Potential The most explosive unresolved thread is the Eye of the Serpent. Aramil stole a gem that plunged the world into eternal darkness, and no one in the party knows. When the connection is made -- and it will be, because Moor is perceptive and Tyr's justice is not subtle -- the party will fracture along moral lines. Moor, as a Lawful Good cleric of the god of justice, cannot ignore a crime of this magnitude. Opal, whose morality is simple and physical, will react with rage. Lavender, who values bonds above all else, will feel the betrayal personally. Bob, the pragmatist, will calculate whether Aramil is still useful. The deeper question is whether Aramil can be redeemed, and whether redemption is possible for someone who knew what they were doing and did it anyway. "If it gets me reinstated at the Royal Community College, then we're okay" is either the most tragic line in the session or the most damning, and the campaign that follows will determine which. Opal's sister Pearl living in Barovia is a quiet thread with enormous potential. Barovia is a land defined by darkness and entrapment -- a realm where the sun does not shine. If Aramil's theft has consequences that extend to Barovia, Opal's simple, happy fact ("She's living a very happy life, actually") becomes a fuse. The sister she left behind to be safe may no longer be safe, and the person responsible is sitting across the campfire.

In-World Documents

Documents

In-World Documents — Charisma Company: Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip

Generated from the Fizzleblast's Fieldtrip one-shot session.

Documents: 6

Table of Contents

  • [Document 1: Fizzleblast's Expedition Contract](#document-1-fizzleblasts-expedition-contract)
  • [Document 2: Temple Entrance Inscription — The Man and the Crocodile](#document-2-temple-entrance-inscription)
  • [Document 3: Aramil's Research Notes — The Serpent's Eye](#document-3-aramils-research-notes)
  • [Document 4: Blackwater's Orders — Rival Party Briefing](#document-4-blackwaters-orders)
  • [Document 5: Fizzleblast's Post-Expedition Invoice](#document-5-fizzleblasts-post-expedition-invoice)
  • [Document 6: The Darkness Broadsheet — Port Nyanzaru Herald](#document-6-the-darkness-broadsheet)

Document 1: Fizzleblast's Expedition Contract

Document Metadata

  • Author: Fizzleblast, Wizard, Proprietor of Charisma Company
  • Date (in-world): Three days before the expedition, Port Nyanzaru
  • Intended recipient: Prospective adventurers (Moor, Opal, Aramil, Lavender, Bob)
  • Found/obtained: Handed to each party member upon recruitment; identical copies distributed

Physical Description

A single sheet of moderately expensive parchment, folded twice into quarters and sealed with a glob of blue wax impressed with a stylized explosion — the Charisma Company seal. The handwriting is small, precise, and increasingly cramped toward the bottom of the page, as though the author did not anticipate the length of his own terms. Several words are crossed out and replaced with marginally different words. The ink is standard iron gall, though three separate colors are visible — the author clearly wrote this over multiple sittings. A faint scent of cashmere fibers clings to the paper.

The Document

[Elegant but small script, clearly written by someone with formal training but limited patience]

EXPEDITION CONTRACT

Charisma Company — Chultan Artifact Retrieval Division

Proprietor: Fizzleblast, Wiz., M.Arc., F.R.S. (Pending)

[Blue wax seal, partially obscured by a thumbprint — the seal depicts a starburst with the letters "CC" in the center]

This contract, executed on the [date left blank — the author forgot to fill it in] day of [month also blank], in the sovereign port of Nyanzaru, hereby establishes the terms of engagement between CHARISMA COMPANY (hereafter "the Company," "the Employer," "Fizzleblast," or "Me") and the UNDERSIGNED ADVENTURER (hereafter "the Contractor," "the Hired Muscle," or "You").

ARTICLE I: SCOPE OF WORK

The Contractor agrees to enter a temple of indeterminate age and [the word "moderate" is crossed out, replaced with "manageable," then crossed out again and replaced with "non-fatal"] danger located in the Chultan interior, approximately [a number has been erased and rewritten three times — the final figure reads "2"] hours upriver from the landing site designated "Fizzle Field."

The Contractor shall retrieve ONE (1) artifact described as GOLDEN and SNAKE-SHAPED from the temple's interior vaults. [Margin note in smaller script: "Touch nothing but the lamp — WRONG FRANCHISE, delete this before printing"] [This note was not deleted before printing.]

All other treasures, relics, weapons, armor, curiosities, and [the word "garbage" is crossed out] incidental finds within the temple are the property of the Contractor upon removal from the premises.

ARTICLE II: COMPENSATION

The Contractor shall receive:

- Retention of all non-specified loot found within the temple

- Access to Charisma Company's patented Fizzle Field safety perimeter for the duration of the expedition

- Use of one (1) canoe [margin note: "RENTAL — DO NOT DAMAGE — I will dock your pay for scratches"]

- A signing bonus of magical equipment to be distributed at the Employer's discretion [a second margin note in different ink: "Niles dropped the bag. Consider the signing bonus whatever you caught."]

The Contractor shall NOT receive:

- Hazard pay

- Medical coverage

- Burial services

- Emotional support

- The golden snake artifact (THIS IS MINE)

ARTICLE III: TIMELINE

The expedition shall commence at DAWN and conclude no later than the following NOON.

[Underlined three times:] The canoe rental expires at noon. NOT 12:01. NOON. I am not paying the late fee again.

ARTICLE IV: LIABILITY

The Employer accepts no liability for injury, death, dismemberment, petrification, polymorph [this word is circled with an exclamation mark], madness, cursing, or existential dread sustained during the expedition. The Contractor acknowledges that the temple may contain but is not limited to: bladed traps, thermal arrays, venomous creatures, structural instability, and [long pause in the writing, as if the author considered and rejected several words] surprises.

[Different ink, added later:] The Employer also accepts no liability for rival adventuring parties that may or may not have been alerted to the expedition's existence through an accidental posting on a public forum. This was not the Employer's fault. The forum interface is [the word "confusing" is crossed out] poorly designed.

ARTICLE V: TERMINATION

This contract terminates upon:

a) Successful delivery of the golden snake artifact to the Employer

b) Death of the Contractor [margin note: "preferably not"]

c) [crossed out: "Mutual agreement"] The Employer saying so

ARTICLE VI: NON-COMPETE

The Contractor agrees not to sell, trade, barter, or gift the golden snake artifact to any rival organization, competing adventuring company, or [ink blot] mothers-in-law.

SIGNED:

[An elaborate signature reading "Fizzleblast" with no fewer than seven unnecessary flourishes. The "F" is twice the height of the other letters.]

CONTRACTOR:

[Five blank lines. Only two have marks on them:]

[Line 1: A single word in block letters: "MOOR"]

[Line 3: A scrawl that might say "Bob" or might be a small drawing of a circle]

[At the very bottom of the page, in tiny script:] Charisma Company is not responsible for the end of the world, the dimming of the sun, or any eschatological events that may or may not result from artifact removal. All complaints should be directed to the Employer's intern.

DM Notes

  • Hidden information: The final clause about "eschatological events" suggests Fizzleblast knew more about the idol's connection to the sun than he disclosed. Whether this was genuine legal caution or specific foreknowledge is left to the DM.
  • Plot hooks: The non-compete clause could become relevant if Aramil tries to sell the Eye of the Serpent. The crossed-out liability additions suggest Fizzleblast has done this before with equally disastrous results.
  • Authenticity notes: The multiple ink colors, margin notes, and crossed-out words reflect Fizzleblast's scattered personality. The contract is legally thorough but emotionally chaotic — exactly the document a brilliant, neurotic gnome would produce.

Document 2: Temple Entrance Inscription — The Man and the Crocodile

Document Metadata

  • Author: Unknown — ancient Chultan temple builders
  • Date (in-world): Pre-historical; estimated several thousand years before present
  • Intended recipient: Temple visitors / supplicants
  • Found/obtained: Carved into the stone archway above the temple's main entrance; referenced in the pressure-plate mural

Physical Description

Deeply carved glyphs in a stone lintel, weathered but still legible. The carving style is precise and formal — the work of master stonemasons working with bronze or obsidian tools. The glyphs are accompanied by a bas-relief depicting a man and a crocodile walking side by side, arms linked, swords crossed overhead. Mineral deposits have filled some of the deeper grooves with pale green crystal, giving the inscription a faintly luminous quality in direct sunlight. Lichen grows in patches around but not over the carved text — as if the stone itself resists organic encroachment. [Small cracks radiate from the bottom-right corner, suggesting seismic damage at some point in the temple's history.]

The Document

[Carved in Old Chultan glyphs; translated below]

[The first line is carved deeper than the rest, the characters nearly an inch into the stone:]

TWO WHO WALK AS ONE SHALL PASS UNHARMED.

[Below, in slightly smaller characters:]

The Man steps with the Crocodile.

The Crocodile steps with the Man.

Neither leads. Neither follows.

Their blades cross overhead — not in combat, but in covenant.

[A horizontal line separates this from the next section. Below the line, the carving style changes — less formal, more hurried, as though added later by a different hand:]

When the Serpent's Eye looks upon the world,

the Sun answers.

When the Eye is taken,

the Sun forgets.

[At the bottom, nearly eroded to illegibility, a final line in the oldest carving style:]

[Several glyphs are damaged beyond recognition] ... WHAT IS BOUND ... [damaged] ... SHALL NOT BE UNBOUND BY ... [damaged] ... GREED ALONE.

[Margin carving, scratched rather than chiseled — clearly vandalism from a later period, in Common script:] "Blacktooth's Blades were here — Year of the Scarlet Moon"

[Below the vandalism, in different but equally crude scratching:] "Blacktooth's Blades were NOT here the following year — Ed."

DM Notes

  • Hidden information: The middle stanza directly prophesies the consequence of removing the Eye of the Serpent — "When the Eye is taken, the Sun forgets." Aramil read this (he investigated the murals) and stole the Eye anyway.
  • Plot hooks: The damaged final line hints at a reversal condition — the binding can potentially be restored, but "greed alone" is insufficient. This suggests that returning the Eye requires a sacrifice or act of selflessness that Aramil is constitutionally unlikely to perform.
  • Authenticity notes: The vandalism graffiti ("Blacktooth's Blades") and the editorial response ("Ed.") ground the temple in a living history — other adventuring parties have come here before, and not all of them survived.

Document 3: Aramil's Research Notes — The Serpent's Eye

Document Metadata

  • Author: Aramil, Adjunct Professor Emeritus, Royal Community College
  • Date (in-world): Written during the temple exploration, with additions after the theft
  • Intended recipient: Personal notes — not intended for any audience
  • Found/obtained: Tucked inside Aramil's fedora; would only be found if someone searched his personal effects

Physical Description

Three pages torn from a cheap notebook, folded twice and jammed inside the crown of a stained fedora. The handwriting is a scholar's shorthand — rapid, abbreviated, legible only to the author and possibly a trained academic. The ink is standard travel-kit quality, applied with a reed pen that was clearly splitting by the third page. The pages smell of jungle rot, sweat, and something faintly metallic — copper or old blood. [A dried insect is pressed flat between pages two and three.] [The bottom of page three has a dark circular stain where something hot was set down — likely the Eye itself.]

The Document

[Page 1 — written in hurried academic shorthand]

FIELD NOTES — Chultan Temple (unnamed), Interior Survey

Expedition: Charisma Co. (Fizzleblast, patron — unreliable)

Date: [blank — Aramil did not bother recording the date]

Antechamber mural — Man + Crocodile motif. Paired pressure plates confirm:

the temple's security is built on COOPERATION. Two must walk as one.

Whoever built this understood that the greatest threats come from

individuals acting alone.

[margin note:] Ironic.

Heliographic arrays — pre-Netherese? Solar engineering of this

sophistication suggests a civilization with advanced understanding of

optics and thermodynamics. The mirrors are not magical — they are

MECHANICAL. The magic is in the prism and the gems. The mirrors

simply focus sunlight. [Note:] Could the same principle be used to

[this thought trails off without completion]

[Page 2 — writing becomes more agitated]

Snake statues = reflectors. The prism splits the beam. Four gems

above the portcullis need to be lit simultaneously. The ENTIRE TEMPLE

is a solar clock — the beams only align at specific times of day.

After sunset, the mechanism goes dark. Everything that depends on

sunlight — the traps, the mirrors, the gems --

[large underline:] EVERYTHING DEPENDS ON SUNLIGHT.

The serpent idol. Gold. Snake-coiled around a sun disk. Two eyes --

gemstones, possible chrysoberyl or alexandrite variant. The

inscription says the Serpent's Eye "looks upon the world" and "the

Sun answers."

Hypothesis: the gems are not decorative. They are FUNCTIONAL.

The idol is the temple's anchor — the thing that binds the solar

mechanism to whatever divine or arcane principle sustains it.

[different ink, added later:] I was right. I was right and I took it

anyway.

[Page 3 — handwriting deteriorates significantly]

The Eye.

[crossed out:] I shouldn't have

[crossed out:] Father would say

[crossed out:] It's not too late to

Sleight of hand, 18. Nobody saw.

The gem is warm. Warmer than body temperature. Warmer than the

jungle. It feels like holding a coal that does not burn.

Est. value: 5,000 gold. Enough for [crossed out: "reinstatement"]

[crossed out: "a new start"] [the final attempt reads:] enough.

[At the bottom of the page, in very small writing, pressed hard

enough to tear the paper slightly:]

The sun should have risen by now.

[The circular burn mark from the Eye sits directly below this line.

The paper is slightly translucent at the mark, as though the heat

changed its molecular structure.]

[On the reverse of page 3, a single line in shaking handwriting:]

It was supposed to be enough.

DM Notes

  • Hidden information: These notes confirm that Aramil understood the consequences before he stole the Eye. "EVERYTHING DEPENDS ON SUNLIGHT" and "the Serpent's Eye looks upon the world and the Sun answers" constitute a clear warning that he deliberately ignored.
  • Plot hooks: If the party finds these notes, they have irrefutable evidence of premeditated theft. The crossed-out beginnings on page three — "Father would say" — reveal the psychological motive in Aramil's own handwriting. The final line — "It was supposed to be enough" — suggests he is already experiencing regret.
  • Authenticity notes: The deteriorating handwriting mirrors Aramil's psychological deterioration. The early pages are competent academic fieldwork; the final page is a confession written by a man who is beginning to understand what he has done. The burned circle from the Eye is physical evidence that the gem produces unnatural heat.

Document 4: Blackwater's Orders — Rival Party Briefing

Document Metadata

  • Author: Unknown commander, possibly a faction leader or crime lord
  • Date (in-world): Two days before the expedition
  • Intended recipient: Blackwater team leader (name unknown)
  • Found/obtained: Could be found on one of the rival party members who fled into the jungle, or recovered from the Chultan undead tide the following morning

Physical Description

A single sheet of dark parchment, folded into thirds and sealed with black wax bearing no insignia — deliberately anonymous. The handwriting is blocky, utilitarian, and clearly disguised — the author wrote with their non-dominant hand or deliberately altered their script. The ink is a deep indigo, nearly black, expensive enough to suggest resources but practical enough to suggest professionalism. [A water stain covers the bottom third — the Chultan humidity has warped the parchment.] [Fold creases are crisp, suggesting this was received recently and not carried long.]

The Document

[Disguised block lettering on dark parchment]

TO: TEAM LEADER, BLACKWATER

RE: CHULTAN RETRIEVAL — PRIORITY TARGET

Your window opens tomorrow at dawn. The gnome's expedition launches at first light from the southern landing. He has hired five — descriptions attached [no attachment found — likely separated in transit or the jungle].

OBJECTIVE: Acquire the golden serpent artifact from the temple interior. The gnome has identified the correct temple. Let his team do the work. You follow.

METHOD: Do NOT enter the temple ahead of them. The heliographic arrays will kill you. [Underlined:] I have lost people to those mirrors before. Wait until the gnome's team has cleared the interior mechanisms, then move. If they solve the light puzzles, the beams will be redirected away from the corridors. Their success creates your path.

TIMING: You are one hour behind them. This is deliberate. Maintain the gap. If you arrive too early, you compete with them in the traps. If you arrive too late, they escape with the artifact. One hour. [Margin note in same hand:] Bring the dimension door scrolls. You will need a fast extraction.

CONTINGENCY: If the gnome's team reaches the artifact first, you have two options:

1. Ambush during their exit. The temple has one entrance. They must come out the way they went in. Set positions at the canyon mouth.

2. If ambush fails, the gnome's safety field cannot be breached by physical force. Wait for them to break camp and take them on the water.

RULES OF ENGAGEMENT:

- Lethal force authorized. [This line was written, crossed out, then written again.]

- Minimize magical expenditure until the extraction. You will need spell slots for the return journey.

- The artifact is worth more than your team. Act accordingly.

[At the bottom, in slightly different pressure, as though added as an afterthought:]

The gnome does not know what the artifact does. Neither do I. But the buyer is paying enough that I do not care. If it does something when you take it from the temple, deal with the consequences. That is what I am paying you for.

[No signature. No name. The wax seal is blank.]

[On the reverse, in a different hand — hastier, written with charcoal rather than ink:]

the gnome posted on PUBLIC forums — he's either stupid or wants us here

the half-elf in the fedora keeps looking at the gem eyes on the idol — watch him

the dragonborn is the healer. kill him first if it comes to a fight

[A crude sketch of the temple entrance, drawn from memory, with the mirror positions marked in charcoal. One mirror is circled with the note: "break this one to kill the beams"]

DM Notes

  • Hidden information: The orders reveal that the rival party was not an independent group but hired operatives working for an unknown buyer. This buyer knows enough about the temple to warn against the mirrors but does not know what the artifact does — suggesting either a collector or a power broker working from incomplete intelligence.
  • Plot hooks: The unknown buyer is a dangling thread. Someone with resources, knowledge of Chultan temples, and willingness to authorize lethal force is still out there — and they did not get what they paid for. The charcoal note about Aramil ("keeps looking at the gem eyes") suggests the rivals were more observant than the party realized.
  • Authenticity notes: The clinical professionalism of the main orders contrasts with the practical, nervous annotations on the reverse. The team leader was following orders but thinking independently. The note about killing the healer first is tactically sound and reveals professional assessment of the party's composition.

Document 5: Fizzleblast's Post-Expedition Invoice

Document Metadata

  • Author: Fizzleblast, on behalf of Charisma Company
  • Date (in-world): The morning after the expedition (or what should have been morning — the sun did not rise)
  • Intended recipient: The party, collectively
  • Found/obtained: Handed to the party by Niles, who was still visibly shaking

Physical Description

A crisp sheet of high-quality linen paper — Fizzleblast clearly brought his stationery kit on the expedition. The handwriting is Fizzleblast's characteristic small, precise script, though it becomes increasingly erratic in the final entries. Written in three colors of ink: black for the main entries, red for the totals, and a shaky green for the final note added after dawn failed to arrive. [The paper is unwrinkled but slightly damp from the Chultan humidity.] [A small cashmere fiber is embedded in the wax seal.]

The Document

[Precise script on high-quality linen paper]

CHARISMA COMPANY

Artifact Retrieval — Expeditionary Services — Cashmere Consultation

[Blue wax seal with CC starburst]

POST-EXPEDITION FINANCIAL RECONCILIATION

Project: Chultan Temple Retrieval (CODENAME: Fieldtrip)

TO: The Contractors (collectively, "You Lot")

FROM: Fizzleblast, Wiz., M.Arc., F.R.S. (Still Pending)

—

CREDITS (Owed to Contractor)

| Item | Amount |

|——|——--|

| Artifact retrieval fee (golden snake idol, delivered intact) | 500 gp |

| Hazard bonus (discretionary — you all looked very beaten up) | 0 gp |

| Performance bonus (nobody died — unprecedented) | 50 gp |

| Subtotal | 550 gp |

DEBITS (Owed to Charisma Company)

| Item | Amount |

|——|——--|

| Magical equipment distribution (signing bonus, via Niles) | 350 gp |

| Potion of Instant Attunement (shattered — NILES) | 200 gp |

| Canoe rental (2x canoe, 24-hour lease, Port Nyanzaru Marina) | 25 gp |

| Canoe damage surcharge (scratches on hull, stern section) | 10 gp |

| Fizzle Field deployment and maintenance (12 hours) | 150 gp |

| Niles (labor, emotional damages, therapy estimate) | 5 gp |

| Cashmere cleaning (salt spray, jungle particulate, one unidentified stain) | 30 gp |

| [crossed out: "Emotional distress suffered by the Employer watching through the bubble"] | [crossed out: "75 gp"] |

| Subtotal | 770 gp |

—

NET BALANCE: (220 gp) owed TO Charisma Company

[In red ink, underlined:] You owe ME money.

[Below, in smaller script:] However, given that you successfully retrieved the artifact and all survived (truly unprecedented — my last three teams did not), I am prepared to forgive the balance in exchange for the artifact and a positive review on the Mercenaries, Murders, and Mothers-in-Law forum. Five stars. Minimum.

[At the very bottom, in green ink, clearly added later — the handwriting is shaking:]

ADDENDUM: The sun has not risen. I have checked my calculations, my calendar, and my pocket watch. It is now four hours past dawn and the sky is black. This is... [several words scratched out] I am certain this is unrelated to the expedition. Almost certain. Mostly.

[Margin note in the same green ink:] Why does the idol feel lighter?

[Final margin note, barely legible:] Where is the other eye?

DM Notes

  • Hidden information: Fizzleblast's final notes reveal that he has noticed the missing Eye and is beginning to connect it to the absence of sunrise. "Where is the other eye?" is the question that, when answered, will implicate Aramil.
  • Plot hooks: Fizzleblast is owed a five-star review. More importantly, he is now holding an idol with one eye missing and a sky with no sun. His next actions — investigation, accusation, or panic — depend on whether the campaign continues.
  • Authenticity notes: The invoice is pure Fizzleblast: petty, precise, self-serving, and ultimately undercut by events too large for his accounting to contain. The three ink colors tell the story: black for business as usual, red for financial grievance, green for creeping existential dread.

Document 6: The Darkness Broadsheet — Port Nyanzaru Herald

Document Metadata

  • Author: The Port Nyanzaru Herald (editorial staff, likely a single harried journalist)
  • Date (in-world): The day after the expedition — or what would have been the day after, if days still functioned
  • Intended recipient: General public of Port Nyanzaru
  • Found/obtained: Posted on every message board, tavern door, and market stall in Port Nyanzaru; copies would be available throughout the city

Physical Description

A single broadsheet, cheaply printed on rough pulp paper using a basic woodblock press. The ink is uneven — heavier on the left side, lighter on the right, suggesting a worn printing block. The headline is set in the largest available typeface, and even so, it is not large enough for the news it carries. [The paper is slightly damp and already beginning to yellow in the humid air.] [Several copies have been posted over existing notices, covering merchant advertisements and a previous broadsheet about dinosaur racing results.] [Pinhole marks in all four corners from hasty posting.]

The Document

[Woodblock-printed broadsheet, uneven ink distribution]

THE PORT NYANZARU HERALD

"All the News That Survives the Jungle"

[Crude woodcut of Port Nyanzaru's harbor, clearly reused from a previous edition]

—

## THE SUN HAS NOT RISEN

Merchants Guild Calls Emergency Session — Dinosaur Races Cancelled — Temples Overwhelmed

The sun did not rise this morning.

This reporter wishes to add additional context, explanation, or reassurance to that statement, but there is none to add. The sun did not rise. It is now [the time is left blank — the printer apparently could not keep up with the passage of hours] past the expected dawn, and the sky over Port Nyanzaru — over all of Chult, as far as can be determined — remains the same empty black it has been since midnight.

The Merchant Princes have convened an emergency session in the Grand Souk. [[Kwayothe]], whose control of the city's oil and candle supply has made her the most powerful person in Nyanzaru overnight, has reportedly tripled prices already. [Margin stamp in red ink from the Herald's editor: "CONFIRMED — candles now 3 sp each. Torches 5 sp. Lantern oil 1 gp per flask."]

The temples of Savras, Waukeen, and Tyr [a small symbol of scales appears here, hand-stamped] have opened their doors to the frightened. The temple of [[Ubtao]] — or what remains of its congregation — has been silent. One priest, who declined to give his name, was heard saying: "The Old One warned us. The serpent held the sun. Someone took the serpent's eye."

[A crude woodcut illustration, clearly carved in haste, depicts a snake coiled around a circle representing the sun. One of the snake's eyes is blank.]

No official explanation has been provided by the Merchant Princes. The harbormaster has suspended all outgoing vessels pending "improved visibility," a phrase that this reporter suspects may prove optimistic.

UNDEAD SIGHTINGS REPORTED ACROSS THE PENINSULA

Multiple reports have reached the Herald of corpses rising from graves, burial sites, and jungle floors across the Chultan peninsula. A merchant caravan arriving [the word "this morning" is crossed out and replaced with "during the current period of darkness"] from Camp Vengeance reported that "the dead walked out of the trees and simply stood there, as if waiting for something."

The Flaming Fist garrison at Fort Beluarian has not responded to communication attempts. A runner dispatched at [time blank] has not returned.

City guard patrols have been doubled. Citizens are advised to remain indoors, keep lights burning, and avoid the harbor district after [the concept of "after dark" has been crossed out, since it is always dark now] at all times.

WHAT WE KNOW:

- The sun did not rise at its expected time

- The stars are not visible

- The undead are rising across the peninsula

- Candle and oil prices have increased dramatically

- The temples are open but have no answers

WHAT WE DO NOT KNOW:

- Why

- For how long

- Whether the sun will return

- [This line has been left blank, as if the author considered writing something and decided it was too frightening to print]

—

[At the bottom of the broadsheet, in the advertising section, printed before the current crisis:]

CHARISMA COMPANY — EXPEDITIONARY SERVICES

"We Go Where You Won't!"

Now hiring: experienced adventurers for artifact retrieval.

Competitive compensation. Equipment provided. Survival not guaranteed.

Contact: Fizzleblast, c/o Port Nyanzaru Marina, Slip 7.

[Someone has drawn a crude arrow from this advertisement to the headline about the sun, and written in charcoal: "COINCIDENCE?"]

[Below that, in different charcoal: "Definitely not."]

DM Notes

  • Hidden information: The priest of Ubtao's statement — "The serpent held the sun. Someone took the serpent's eye" — is the closest anyone in Port Nyanzaru has come to understanding the cause. The connection between Fizzleblast's expedition and the disappearance of the sun is, at this point, a matter of public speculation (see: the charcoal annotation linking the Charisma Company ad to the headline).
  • Plot hooks: The Charisma Company advertisement appearing on the same broadsheet as the crisis it caused is darkly comic and potentially dangerous — if anyone connects Fizzleblast's expedition to the darkness, the party becomes targets. Kwayothe's candle monopoly positions her as a political antagonist who profits from the crisis. The silent Fort Beluarian is a dangling thread suggesting the military is already overwhelmed.
  • Authenticity notes: The broadsheet captures the chaos of a city trying to process an apocalyptic event through its normal institutions. The blank spaces where times should be, the crossed-out references to "morning" and "after dark," and the increasingly desperate editorial stamps all convey a world whose fundamental assumptions have just been shattered. The charcoal annotations on the Charisma Company ad are the kind of detail that makes a prop feel lived-in — someone in Port Nyanzaru is already putting the pieces together.
Novelization

Campaign Novelization

5-Chapter Prose Retelling

"Gold is heavy, but history is heavier still. And in Chult, the history is hungry."

5 CHAPTERS
+The Cashmere and the Clay

The air in Chult did not merely exist; it pressed. It was a physical weight, a wet wool blanket draped over the lungs, smelling of rotting vegetation, wet fur, and the ancient, reptilian musk of things that had not evolved in ten million years.

Moor wiped a hand across his snout, his scales slick with condensation. The cleric of Tyr stood knee-deep in the surf, the sand sucking at his sabatons, watching their employer fuss over the hem of a robe that cost more than Moor’s entire monastery.

"The cashmere, Niles! The cashmere!" Fizzleblast shrieked, his voice cracking like a dry twig. The gnome was a caricature of an academic—beard too big, spectacles too thick, courage too small. "If it touches the water, it shrivels. Do you want me to shrivel, Niles?"

Niles, a red-headed boy whose limbs seemed to have grown faster than his coordination, threw himself into the mud to serve as a human bridge. Fizzleblast stepped delicately onto the boy’s back, hopping to the dry sand without dampening a single thread.

Moor sighed, a low rumble in his chest. "We are walking into a tomb," he muttered, though no one was listening.

The party—if one could call this loose association of mercenaries and desperate souls a party—was already scrambling up the beach. There was Opal, a Goliath woman who looked as though she had been hewn from granite, currently checking the edge of a greataxe that was larger than the gnome hiring them. There was Lavender, a Tiefling whose horns were bedecked in jewels that caught the harsh jungle sun, radiating a manic optimism that Moor found deeply unsettling. Aramil, the half-elf archaeologist, looked like a man continuously on the verge of a nervous breakdown, clutching a purple crystal and muttering about tenure. And somewhere, supposedly, was Bob. The halfling was a shadow in a hood, a collection of knives and pockets that occasionally manifested a nose or a mustache.

"Coordinates posted publicly," Fizzleblast was saying, talking fast to outrun the jungle silence. "Administrative error. Mercenaries, Murderers, and Mothers-in-Law forum. Dreadful mistake. The Red Fangs are an hour behind us."

"An hour?" Aramil’s voice pitched up. He looked back at the tree line as if expecting an arrow.

"Give or take. They’ll kill you, of course. Ransom me. But look!" Fizzleblast gestured grandly toward the tree line.

Ahead, the jungle broke against a wall of stone. The temple rose in tiered steps, a ziggurat of moss-slicked grey rock. But it was the light that drew the eye. Mirrors—dozens of them, polished to a killing shine—were arrayed along the terraces, catching the midday sun and focusing it into blinding, searing beams that crisscrossed the entrance.

"Heliographic thermal arrays," Fizzleblast said, jamming a metal rod into the sand. A shimmer of blue force sprang up—a magical dome. "I shall supervise from here. The Fizzle Field. Repels organic matter and aggressive sales tactics. You get the artifact—golden, snake-shaped, I don't know, I haven't seen it—and you keep the rest. Just get back before sunset. Or before the Red Fangs kill you. Or the dinosaurs."

"Dinosaurs?" Opal asked, squinting at the jungle.

"Smell that?" Moor asked.

Opal sniffed. "Scales. Big ones."

"Go," Fizzleblast shooed them. "Niles! The loot sack!"

The boy stumbled forward, tripping over a crab, and the bag in his arms exploded. It wasn't just a sack; it was a rupture in space. Potions, scrolls, and weapons vomited forth, scattering into the sand.

"Take it! Keep it! Just go!"

Moor watched his companions scramble like seagulls on a chip. Opal snatched a tattoo needle that hummed with eldritch power. Lavender shoved bracers onto her wrists. Moor knelt, his hand closing around a heavy amulet half-buried in the sand. He felt the pulse of it immediately—a connection to the divine, a reservoir of faith. He clipped it over his white robes.

"The mirrors," Moor said, pointing his mace toward the temple entrance. "We can't walk through that light."

+The Burning Path

The heat near the temple entrance was distinct from the humidity. It was dry, sharp, and tasted of ozone. Two massive apes—Girallons, four-armed and foaming—were pacing near the entryway, testing the light beams. Moor watched as one ape brushed a beam; the fur didn't burn, it vanished. The flesh beneath boiled instantly.

"Move the mirrors," Moor commanded, his voice dropping into the register he used for sermons and battlefield triage.

Opal moved to one of the ground-level reflectors. She grunted, muscles coiling under her grey skin, and shoved the heavy stone housing. The beam swung wild, slicing through the humid air.

"Angle it!" Moor shouted.

She heaved. The beam swept across the stone terrace and caught the lead Girallon in the chest. There was no scream, only the wet sound of fluids turning to steam. The beast fell, cooked from the inside out before it hit the ground. The second one, seeing its packmate liquidated, shrieked and fled into the canopy.

"Effective," Aramil noted, his face pale.

"Deadly," Moor corrected. "Single file. Stay in the shadows of the beams."

They breached the antechamber. It was cool inside, silent as aheld breath. The smell of copper and old blood hit them instantly. The ceiling vanished into darkness thirty feet up, but the floor was the concern. It was a gauntlet of pressure plates, flanked by piles of gold that reached their waists.

"Greed," Lavender whispered, her eyes reflecting the glint of coins. "It's literally paved with greed."

"Don't touch it," Moor warned. He stepped up to the first plate. He pressed his mace down. Click.

A blade, three feet of rusted iron, shot up from the floor with the speed of a striking viper.

"Right," Bob said from somewhere inside his hood. "Traps."

"The mural," Aramil called out. He was standing by the far wall, brushing dust off a faded painting. "Look. A man and a crocodile. Standing together. Crossing swords."

Moor looked at the floor. The plates were paired. "Weight distribution," he realized. "It requires two. Walking in lockstep."

He looked at Lavender. "You and me."

"I weigh as much as one of your legs, Moor," the Tiefling said.

"Use the shield. Pile the gold. Just move when I move."

They stepped onto the first set of plates. Moor felt the mechanism depress, a groan of ancient gears beneath the stone. He held his breath. No blade.

"Step," he said.

They moved. Left foot. Right foot. It was a dance of trust in a room designed for murder. Behind them, Opal and Aramil were solving it their own way, brute-forcing the mechanism with the sheer weight of gathered treasure.

They reached the far door—a portcullis held by counterweights. Above the arch, two rusted greatswords hung crossed, mimicking the mural.

"Grab them," Moor said to Opal. "Pull down."

The Goliath reached up, her height making it easy. Moor grabbed the other. They pulled. The resistance was immense, the friction of centuries fighting them, but the metal groaned and the gate began to rise.

"Go!" Moor roared.

Bob and Aramil darted under the rising spikes. Lavender followed. Moor and Opal released the swords and rolled through just as the iron teeth slammed back into the stone.

+The Serpent's Eye

The next chamber was a furnace.

The heat was oppressive, radiating from a central crystalline prism hanging from the ceiling. Beams of sunlight from the roof were hitting it, scattering into searing lances of energy that cut the room into deadly geometric shapes.

"It’s getting hotter," Aramil said, wiping sweat from his brow. "The mechanism... it's cooking us."

"The statue," Bob pointed.

In the center of the room, bathing in the refracted light, was a massive stone head of a snake. Its mouth was open, and from the stone throat, something wet and alive was uncoiling.

It was immense—a constrictor snake forty feet long, its scales shimmering with an unnatural iridescence. It tasted the air, and its head snapped toward Aramil.

"Brother Snake!" Aramil shouted, raising his hands. "We are not food! The monkeys outside—"

The snake struck. It was a blur of muscle. It slammed into Aramil, wrapping around the half-elf before he could finish his plea. Moor heard the sickening crunch of ribs.

"Aramil!" Moor surged forward, calling upon Tyr. A shield of shimmering force sprang up around Lavender as she unleashed a blast of eldritch energy.

Opal roared, a sound that shook dust from the ceiling. She didn't bother with weapons; she slammed her fist into the snake’s flank. The tattoo she’d found on the beach flared with light, and the punch connected with the force of a battering ram.

The snake hissed, tightening its coil. Aramil’s face was turning purple.

"The mirrors!" Moor shouted, realizing the geometry of the room. "Turn the snake statues! Redirect the light!"

Bob was already moving, darting through the shadows. Lavender sent her familiar—a raven named Tika—to distract the beast. The snake snapped, jaws closing around the bird. The familiar vanished in a puff of sulfur.

"My bird!" Lavender screamed. "You killed my bird!"

She unleashed hell. Blasts of force hammered the serpent. Opal grabbed the snake by its tail and heaved, her muscles tearing under the strain. Moor reached through the chaos, his hand glowing with white light, and slapped his palm against Aramil’s exposed leg. Healing energy flooded the half-elf, knitting bone just enough to keep him alive.

Bob reached the stone head. He shoved. The stone ground against stone. The light beam shifted, hitting the crystal at a new angle.

The room flashed white. The heat spiked, then broke. The beam refracted, splitting into four harmless rays that illuminated the exit.

Opal brought her greataxe down. The snake’s head severed with a wet thud.

Silence rushed back into the room, heavy and hot. Aramil gasped on the floor, clutching his chest.

"We need to move," Moor said, hauling the half-elf up. "The sun is setting. And I can hear them."

From the roof above, the sound of boots on stone. Voices. The Red Fangs.

+The King of Feathers

They burst out of the temple’s suffocating interior into the twilight.

They were in a high-walled courtyard, open to the sky. The sun was a dying ember on the horizon, painting the clouds in bruises of purple and red. The air here was cooler, but the danger was absolute.

In the center of the courtyard, beneath a stone archway, a Tyrannosaurus Rex slumbered.

It was magnificent and terrifying, a mountain of scales and muscle rising and falling with deep, rumbling breaths. And hanging directly above its snout, dangling from the archway like a bauble on a holiday tree, was the Golden Snake.

"Don't. Touch. The dinosaur," Moor whispered.

"I can get it," Bob breathed. The halfling was staring at the artifact, his eyes wide. He pulled a pair of boots from his pack—strange, spring-loaded contraptions he’d looted from the foyer. "I can jump."

"Bob," Moor warned.

"I’m fast. I’m quiet."

"They are on the roof," Aramil hissed, pointing upward. Shadows were moving along the rim of the courtyard. The rival party.

"Go," Moor said. "We'll cover you."

Bob didn't hesitate. He sprinted toward the sleeping titan. The boots engaged, magic flaring, and the halfling launched himself into the air. He soared over the T-Rex’s head, a silent arc against the darkening sky.

He reached the archway. He hung by his legs, inverted, and snatched the Golden Snake.

"Got it!" he whispered.

Then the sky exploded.

A fireball, cast from the rim of the wall, roared down into the courtyard. It wasn't aimed at Bob. It was aimed at the dinosaur.

The explosion was deafening. The T-Rex roared—a sound that vibrated in the marrow of Moor’s bones. The beast thrashed, the stone archway crumbling around it. Bob dropped, clutching the idol, his cloak snapping open to slow his fall.

"Run!" Opal screamed.

The courtyard dissolved into madness. The Red Fangs were dropping from the walls, weapons drawn. The T-Rex, blinded by pain and rage, snapped its jaws at anything that moved. A tail the size of a siege tower swept the area, shattering stone pillars.

"To the door!" Moor shoved Lavender toward the exit. "Don't look back!"

They sprinted. Behind them, the screams of the rival adventurers were cut short by the wet crunch of the T-Rex’s jaws.

+The Gauntlet

The run back was a blur of terror.

They hit the pressure plate room. Gas hissed from the vents—poison, thick and green.

"Hold your breath!" Moor commanded.

He grabbed Lavender’s arm and hurled her across the gap. Opal vaulted over a trap, landing heavy but running. Aramil stumbled, gasping, and Moor grabbed him by the collar of his jacket, dragging him through the poison fog.

"Don't you die on me, professor!"

They reached the foyer. The sun was gone. The mirrors were dark. But the jungle outside was alive. The undead—skeletons, zombies, things that had clawed their way out of the Chultian mud—were swarming the entrance.

"The bubble!" Fizzleblast’s voice was a tinny squeak in the distance. "Get to the bubble!"

A wall of rotting flesh stood between them and the blue shimmer of safety.

"Opal!" Moor shouted. "Clear a path!"

The Goliath roared, her bloodaxe glowing crimson. She became a whirlwind of steel, cleaving through bone and rot. Moor raised his shield, the symbol of Tyr flaring with holy light, turning the undead away, burning them with the radiance of judgment.

They crashed through the line. Bob dove, sliding into the sand. Aramil fell, clutching a smaller artifact he’d swiped from the temple—a single obsidian eye. Opal and Lavender tumbled after them.

Moor was the last. He felt a cold, dead hand grab his ankle. He spun, bringing his mace down in a skull-shattering arc, and threw himself across the threshold.

The noise cut out instantly.

Inside the Fizzle Field, it was quiet. The screams of the dying, the roar of the dinosaur, the hiss of the undead—all of it was muted, distant, like a nightmare observed through thick glass.

Moor rolled onto his back, staring up at the stars appearing over the jungle canopy. His lungs burned. His armor felt like it weighed a thousand pounds.

Fizzleblast stood over them, holding a teddy bear, looking terrifyingly calm.

"Did you get it?" the gnome asked.

Bob sat up, reached into his bag, and pulled out the Golden Snake. It gleamed in the starlight, beautiful and cursed.

"Good," Fizzleblast said. "Niles, bring the canoe. We’re leaving."

Moor closed his eyes, listening to the frantic rhythm of his own heart. They were alive. Richer, perhaps. But as he touched the scales of his holy symbol, he felt a tremor in the earth, a deep vibration that seemed to come from the artifact itself.

They had robbed the past. And the past, Moor knew, rarely forgave such debts.

The sun had set on Chult, but Moor had the sinking suspicion that for them, the long night was just beginning.

The Verdant Herald

Herald

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

THE PORT NYANZARU CHRONICLE

"From the Harbour to the Horizon, We Print the Truth"

Volume XLIV, Issue 312 | The 7th of Hammer, Year of the Scarlet Witch, 1494 DR | Price: 5 Squirtons

Port Nyanzaru, Chult

═══════════════════════════════════════════════════

FRONT PAGE

HEADLINE: SUN EXTINGUISHED OVER CHULT; SCHOLARS BLAME "CATASTROPHIC CELESTIAL EVENT" AS DARKNESS ENTERS THIRD DAY

Temple Expedition in Aldani Basin Believed Connected; Entire Rival Adventuring Company Found Dead in Jungle; Port Authorities Urge Calm

By Kwame Obo, Senior Correspondent

The sun has not risen over Chult in three days.

This newspaper has confirmed, through consultation with the Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu's personal astrologers, the Priesthood of Savras, and two independent diviners who have since gone mad, that the celestial body commonly referred to as "the sun" is no longer visible in the sky above the Chultan Peninsula. Nor, by all reports arriving via sending stone from the Sword Coast, above Faerun at large. The stars have likewise vanished. The moon offers no light. The darkness is, in the words of Harbormaster Zindar, "not the darkness of night, but of something else entirely."

Public panic, while officially discouraged, is well underway.

The Chronicle has traced the onset of the darkness to the evening following an unsanctioned temple expedition in the deep jungle southwest of the Aldani Basin. A gnomish wizard of modest reputation and immodest wardrobe — identified by port records as one "Fizzleblast," proprietor of a concern called Charisma Company — hired five mercenaries through channels described by the Mercenary Licensing Board as "irregular at best" and departed upriver three days prior. The party returned the following morning aboard rented canoes (the deposit for which remains unpaid) carrying significant quantities of looted artifacts and one fewer rival adventuring company than had entered the jungle.

"They came paddling back like nothing happened," reported Eku, a Chultan guide who observed the party's return from a neighbouring dock. "The big one — enormous, grey-skinned, built like a siege tower — was laughing about chopping a snake in half while blindfolded. The small one in the hood was counting gold. They were all singed. And the sun was gone. I do not think these facts are unrelated."

A second witness, the canoe rental proprietor Shago, offered a different account. "The gnome owed me forty gold for the canoes and another ten for the scorch marks. When I asked about the sun, he told me it was 'probably seasonal' and retreated into a shimmering bubble. His assistant — the red-haired boy — was lying on the floor of the bubble, shaking. He would not speak. He has not spoken since."

Port authorities have confirmed that the bodies of an adventuring company known as "Blackwater" were discovered along the jungle trail between the temple site and the river. The remains showed evidence of both predator attacks and what the coroner described as "extensive undead activity." A preliminary inquest has attributed their deaths to the sudden onset of darkness, which is believed to have awakened the peninsula's considerable undead population en masse.

Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu has called an emergency session of the Council to address the crisis. A curfew has been imposed from the sixth bell onward. All ships in harbour have been ordered to maintain full lantern complement. The Temple of Savras has declared the event "a rupture in the covenant between the mortal world and the celestial spheres" and has called for seven days of prayer and fasting.

When reached for comment, a scaled individual in white robes who appeared to be a member of the expedition party said only: "Sun gone. Not our fault."

He then purchased a lamp and left.

SECONDARY STORY 1: TEMPLE ARTIFACTS SURFACE IN PORT NYANZARU MARKETS; PROVENANCE QUESTIONED

By Adaeze Nri

Within hours of the expedition party's return, several items of considerable magical power began appearing in Port Nyanzaru's open markets. A staff carved from an unfamiliar white wood, radiating restorative energy. A belt of extraordinary craftsmanship that one merchant claimed "made a halfling lift a barrel over his head." A pair of bracers that bent light around themselves. And, most alarmingly, a cloak fashioned from what appears to be living bat membrane, which was observed allowing its wearer to fly short distances above the harbour district.

The Merchant Princes' Trade Commission has issued a formal inquiry into the provenance of these items, noting that the removal of artifacts from Chultan temple sites without governmental license is a violation of the Antiquities Preservation Act of 1489 DR. The gnomish expedition leader, Fizzleblast, holds no such license. His company, "Charisma Company," is not registered with the Port Nyanzaru Bureau of Commerce.

"We are not accusing anyone of looting," said Trade Commissioner Jessamine. "We are merely noting that a great deal of powerful magic has arrived in our city at the exact moment the sun disappeared, and we would very much like to understand the connection."

The expedition party could not be reached for further comment, as they appear to have departed Port Nyanzaru aboard a vessel bound for the Sword Coast. The gnome's shimmering protective bubble was observed floating downriver early yesterday morning.

SECONDARY STORY 2: UNDEAD ACTIVITY SURGES ACROSS PENINSULA FOLLOWING CELESTIAL EVENT; FORT BELUARIAN REPORTS "UNPRECEDENTED" ATTACKS

By Kwame Obo

The Order of the Gauntlet garrison at Fort Beluarian has reported a threefold increase in undead encounters since the sun's disappearance. Commander Niles Breakbone — no relation to the gnomish wizard's intern — has dispatched urgent requests to Baldur's Gate for reinforcement, stating that the fort's current complement is "wholly insufficient to manage a peninsula of undead that no longer have a sunrise to drive them back underground."

Reports from jungle guides and merchant caravans paint a grim picture. Creatures that previously emerged only at night now roam freely at all hours. The Death Curse, thought to have been broken two years prior, has resurfaced in whispered rumour, though no confirmed cases of soul-trapping have been reported.

The Ytepka Society, Port Nyanzaru's secretive guardian order, has reportedly tripled its patrols. Dinosaur racing has been suspended indefinitely, a decision that has caused nearly as much public outrage as the loss of the sun itself.

SECONDARY STORY 3: PORT NYANZARU LAMP OIL PRICES SURGE 800%; MERCHANT PRINCE IFAN DENIES PROFITEERING

By Adaeze Nri

The price of lamp oil in Port Nyanzaru has increased eightfold since the onset of the eternal darkness. Merchant Prince Ifan Tal'roa, who controls the city's oil trade, has attributed the increase to "market forces" and "unprecedented demand" rather than personal enrichment. Public sentiment, however, has been less generous.

"He raised the price before the second bell had finished ringing," said a dockworker who requested anonymity. "The sun had been gone for four hours. Four hours! And suddenly a flask of oil costs more than a week's wages. If that is not profiteering, I do not know the meaning of the word."

Ifan Tal'roa has pledged to donate a "modest portion" of oil revenues to the Temple of Savras for their prayer vigil. The temple has not confirmed receipt of any donation.

OPINION & EDITORIAL

"Five Strangers Walk Into Our Jungle, and the Sun Walks Out"

Opinion of Grandfather Zitembe, Priest of Savras, Temple of Divination, Port Nyanzaru

I have served at the Temple of Savras for forty-seven years. I have read the stars. I have interpreted the dreams of princes and paupers alike. I have, on six occasions, correctly predicted the winner of the annual tyrannosaur sprint. My record of prognostication is, I say without false modesty, unimpeachable.

I did not see this coming. None of us did. And that, more than the darkness itself, is what terrifies me.

When the god Savras grants visions, they come as fragments — shapes in smoke, whispers in the space between waking and sleep. For three decades, I have interpreted these fragments faithfully. But in the hours before the sun vanished, every diviner in the temple received the same vision simultaneously: a serpent of gold, its eye a burning coal, being carried away from a place of ancient binding by hands that moved with the practiced greed of a man who has stolen before and will steal again. And behind him, in the place where the eye had rested, a darkness that spread like ink in water.

We sent warnings. We cried out to the harbour authorities. We were told that "temple business" was not sufficient cause to delay a licensed expedition. By the time we convinced the Merchant Princes to act, the canoes had already departed.

I do not blame the five mercenaries who entered that temple. They are tools — blunt instruments wielded by a gnome who understood precisely enough about ancient magic to be catastrophically dangerous. I blame the system that allows any wealthy fool with a cashmere robe to hire strangers off a public forum and send them to disturb sites that were sealed for reasons our ancestors understood and we have forgotten.

The eye must be returned. The serpent must be made whole. And whoever removed it — whoever carries it now, warm in their pocket, worth five thousand gold and the light of the entire world — must be found.

The sun did not go out by accident. Someone stole it.

CLASSIFIEDS

HELP WANTED

Posting Details
LAMPLIGHTERS (URGENT — CITY-WIDE) "The Port Nyanzaru Harbourmaster's Office seeks FORTY (40) additional lamplighters for immediate deployment. Permanent darkness has rendered existing staff insufficient. Must be comfortable working at heights, in total darkness, and near areas of increased undead activity. Pay: 8 squirtons per day plus hazard supplement. Oil provided. Apply at the Harbourmaster's Office, Tiryki Anchorage. Night shifts only. All shifts are night shifts."
JUNGLE RECOVERY CREW "EXPERIENCED GUIDES AND PORTERS needed to recover remains and equipment from the Aldani Basin jungle trail. Multiple casualties reported. Must be willing to work in undead-active territory without sunlight. Armed escort provided by the Order of the Gauntlet. 15 squirtons per day. No questions about what happened to the previous recovery crew. Apply at Fort Beluarian."
TEMPLE ASSESSMENT SCHOLARS "The Council of Merchant Princes seeks QUALIFIED ARCHAEOLOGISTS AND DIVINE SCHOLARS to assess a recently disturbed temple site in the deep jungle. Applicants must hold proper antiquities credentials — adjunct professor status is NOT sufficient. Knowledge of serpent iconography, solar binding rituals, and Chultan religious history required. Danger pay negotiable. Contact Trade Commissioner Jessamine."
CANOE REPAIR SPECIALIST "SHAGO'S CANOE RENTALS seeks a skilled woodworker to repair fire and acid damage to three (3) river canoes. Damage described as 'consistent with a gnome who does not respect other people's property.' Materials provided. 6 squirtons. Apply dockside, Tiryki Anchorage. Deposit still owed."

LOST & FOUND

Item Notice
LOST: One Golden Serpent Eye "LOST (or STOLEN): A gemstone of considerable size and warmth, believed to have been pried from a golden serpent idol located in a sealed temple in the Aldani Basin. The gem is described as 'the size of a quail's egg, burning gold in colour, and warm to the touch.' IT IS BELIEVED THAT THE RETURN OF THIS ITEM WILL RESTORE THE SUN. Reward: The Merchant Princes will pay ANY PRICE for its safe return. The Temple of Savras will grant a lifetime of favourable divinations. Contact Grandfather Zitembe at the Temple of Savras or any Merchant Prince's representative. THIS IS NOT A DRILL."
FOUND: One Stained Fedora "FOUND on the Tiryki Anchorage docks: A wide-brimmed leather hat of a style popular among academic adventurers. Heavily stained with sweat, jungle rot, and what appears to be constrictor venom. Smells of regret. No one has claimed it. If uncollected within seven days, it will be burned. Inquire at SHAGO'S CANOE RENTALS."
MISSING: Members of Blackwater Adventuring Company "MISSING: The adventuring company known as 'Blackwater' (4-6 members, variously armed, believed to include at least two practitioners of the arcane arts) departed Port Nyanzaru three days ago for a temple expedition in the Aldani Basin. They have not returned. Partial remains were recovered along the jungle trail. Any information regarding surviving members is welcome. Contact the Mercenary Licensing Board, Market Ward."

SHOPS & SERVICES

Business Advertisement
WAKANGA'S WONDROUS WICKS "In these DARK TIMES, illuminate your home with WAKANGA'S WONDROUS WICKS — enchanted candles that burn for 30 days without replacement! Each candle infused with a minor continual flame cantrip by Merchant Prince Wakanga O'tamu's personal apprentices. Available in Jungle Orchid, Sea Breeze, and New Scent: Existential Dread. 50 squirtons per candle. Bulk discounts for taverns and temples. 'When the sun fails, Wakanga's Wicks prevail!'"
THE THUNDERING LIZARD TAVERN "ETERNAL HAPPY HOUR! Since no one can tell what time it is anymore, The Thundering Lizard is pleased to announce that happy hour pricing is now in effect INDEFINITELY. Half-price tej and Chultan rum until the sun returns or the casks run dry, whichever comes first. Live music nightly. Every hour is nightly. Located on Trader's Row, Port Nyanzaru."
EKENE-AFE, LEATHERWORKER "Has YOUR armour been damaged by giant constrictor snakes, toxic gas, or solar mirror arrays? EKENE-AFE offers expert repairs to all leather and hide armours. Special rates for adventurers returning from temple expeditions. We do not ask questions about provenance. We DO ask that you leave cursed items outside. Shop located near the Grand Coliseum."

WANTED

```

═══════════════════════════════════════

WANTED

FOR QUESTIONING BY ORDER OF

THE COUNCIL OF MERCHANT PRINCES

DESCRIPTION: A half-elf of middling

height and gaunt build. Wears a

battered leather hat and a stained

overcoat. Carries a purple crystal

on a chain around his neck. Speaks

with the cadence of a man who has

been denied tenure. Seen clutching

a glowing golden object during the

expedition's departure from the

Aldani Basin. Described by one

witness as "the grungy one who

talked to the monkeys."

ALSO SOUGHT: A gnome of advanced

age in an oversized cashmere robe,

travelling in a shimmering bubble

with a traumatized adolescent

assistant. Proprietor of "Charisma

Company." Owes 50 gold in canoe

rental fees.

REWARD: 500 gold for information

leading to the recovery of the

GOLDEN SERPENT EYE and/or the

restoration of the sun.

5 gold for information leading

to the collection of the canoe

rental debt.

Jessamine, Market Ward, or any

officer of the Ytepka Society.
```
